# Thread: The 8th most important thing you need to know about Diet and Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-8th-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.4973/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-11-25T14:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-8th-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.4973/post-65569>

Nothing revolutionary here... just an add-on to some of my previous posts. This is a piece I wrote for a national newspaper... so it's put in more "laymans" terms... which may seem a bit basic for some on this forum... still, hopefully some reminders and a rehash of what I've already written about... but in Muhammad Ali words:

"It's the repetition of affirmations that leads to belief, and once that belief becomes a deep conviction, things begin to happen."

**Â 

The whole is only as good as the sum of its parts**

Â 

Improving your health and fitness is a multifactorial equation. It involves many different factors which all have to be figured out and then put in place. Once they are in place, they need to be put in the right order so that they learn how to work in harmony. The equation and the factors are different for everyone. This is a crucial point to understand. There is no one equation that we can all use. Everything needs to be put in context and the context has to be based on the individual.

Having said that, there are certain principles that can be applied in order to put that equation together. These principles need to be correct and valid. If you start off with the wrong principles then you are destined for failure. Nutrition is one of these principles. It is part of the equation, but I will emphasize the word â€œpartâ€.

You see, modern nutrition and health is based on magic bullets and quick fixes. If you want to lose weight, you are told to go on a magic two week cabbage soup diet. If you want to lower your cholesterol, you are told to drink a yoghurt drink with magical plant extracts. Apart from the fact the neither of these things solve the problem, they are both just easy fixes. Easy fixes donâ€™t work. Unfortunately, when it comes to health, the majority of people are always looking for the quick way out. Take a multivitamin, to get all your vitamins. Drink a smoothie to get all your fruits. Take a special powder to get all your antioxidants. This is not what proper nutrition is about and itâ€™s not the approach you should take if you want to improve your health.

As the title of this article suggests, nutrition is about the sum of its parts. Itâ€™s not about just taking a multivitamin and a few fish oil capsules and itâ€™s not about simply eliminating one certain bad food or adding one certain type of good food back in. In order to truly get the benefits of proper nutrition, you need to make wholesale changes to the way you eat. These changes need to be based on the most modern concepts, the best (non biased/commercially funded) science, and fit with your individual make up.

I have written 24 articles so far for this magazine (Fit Mag). I only have a few hundred words to explain the millions of words of theory I have read and studied. I am essentially only scratching the surface but I am trying to get the key points across. The key points are many. Iâ€™ve talked about how the foods we should eat are not those that are falsely advertised to us as being healthy. Iâ€™ve talked about carbohydrate and its affect on insulin. Iâ€™ve talked about protein and its importance for our immune and endocrine system. Iâ€™ve talked about particular foods which can help certain conditions like sleep and stress. Iâ€™ve talked about how to recover from training and Iâ€™ve talked about how fat metabolism works and how to fat adapt. In all these articles, Iâ€™ve talked about general foods and how important the quality and source of that food you eat is. There is a difference between eating grass fed pasture raised animal meats to those that are grain fed and factory farmed. There is a difference between eating real butter and refined vegetable oil butters. So there are a lot of things to take into account. But the key point is that in order for the equation to work, you need to cover all the bases.

The problem is that people generally only want to cover the easy bases and cherry pick. I get many emails and comments from people that they are now happily eating loads of bacon and eggs with lashings of butter. Yet they are still not losing the weight they want to lose and still donâ€™t feel well. This is how the cherry picking causes problems. People generally only choose to do things that they like when it comes to food. When I delve a bit further, these are the type of people that still have 3 slices of toast with their â€œlashingsâ€ of butter and they choose to buy meats which have added sugars and refined oils. The types of fat you eat and the amount of fat you eat is completely dependent on whether or not you have covered all the other bases. The other bases are related to reducing your carbohydrate intake, eliminating gluten, getting your timing right, reducing inflammation, avoiding any added sugars, eating the right fats, eating the right proteins, and getting the best sources of these foods (to name but a few). Therefore, if you think simply eating more butter and eggs is the solution to all your problems, and then you are completely missing the point. If you still eat the foods that are causing the problems and still have the metabolic impairment (i.e. you have spent your life eating metabolically damaging foods like refined grains, oils and sugars) , then simply adding more eggs and butter to your general diet is not going to do much, in fact, it is likely to cause more problems.

My aim here is not to cause confusion and fear. It might sound like I am trying to make nutrition sound complicated. Iâ€™m not. I am simply trying to emphasize the importance of taking a collective approach to the way you eat. Itâ€™s not about making one or two changes and just adding the foods you like back into your diet while keeping all the bad stuff there. Thatâ€™s equivalent to putting in a few traffic wardens in to a completely grid locked city of traffic jams. The solution to this is to build new roads and bridges. Itâ€™s the same with your health. Address the cause instead of just patching up the symptoms. Â 

The right nutrition for improved health and fitness is about making wholesale changes to what, when and how much you eat. Itâ€™s about creating a new normal. Itâ€™s about educating yourself and learning about all the facets of good nutrition and then applying it to yourself.Â  Look to improve all aspects of the food you eat and the way you eat. How detailed you want that to be is down to the person. And like I said before, everything has to be put into context and individualised. The right guidelines are there if you look in the right places, the right foods are easily available and Ireland happens to be blessed with really good quality sources. The whole is only as good as the sum of its parts. Learn what those parts are and gather as many of them as you can. Make it a long term continuous project so that it becomes habitual. Health and wellness should not be a temporary state, so why should eating healthily be something you do temporarily??

Â 

Furthermore, remember that there are still several other factors of the equation that need to be put in place like good sleep, reduced stress and proper exercise. Each of these things is a separate discussion but itâ€™s important to mention them. Health is a multifactorial equation and nothing works in isolation. So you might have the best nutrition in the world and eat the â€œBarryâ€ way. Great. But if you suffer from stress and anxiety and sleep poorly, then you are likely to still suffer from health problems. Â 

So the best advice I have for anyone trying to improve their health is this: do not look for magic bullets, they don't exist. Rather, look to improve every aspect of your life from nutrition to thoughts to exercise no matter how big or small they may seem. Everything counts. Nothing is more important than the other. You are only as good as the sum of your parts.

Â 

"Beyond the walls of intelligence, life is defined"

### anatoli@bythebay.com.au — 2012-11-26T07:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-newbie-questions.4980/post-65613>

HI All

I am a newbie to this forum and to paleo. Just wondering if you can help with timing of breakfast. Is it 1/2 hour of waking or 1/2 hour rising? I often wake early and so lie in bed and meditate for a while. Should I be getting up and eating straight away?

Thanks

Antonietta

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-26T08:28:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-newbie-questions.4980/post-65616>

it is waking.......this is when your cortisol level rises to wake up the cerebral cortex so we want to use it to yolk the correct response.

Â 

**WHY DO I DARE TO DISAGREE with Paleo and modern healthcare, like the thought of Obamacare?

If our goal is to really understand our world better, our**Â lack of scientific diversity**Â is what really makes it a more difficult task. Â I am no exercise guru, gym owner, or research scientist. Â But they are no experts on neural biology. Â And humans have big brains, and this is what separates us from all eutherian mammals. Â I am a clinician who thinks best outside the box and lets the science guide me. Â Paleo can be described as a team or a tribe today. Â Team building requires you to limit diversity of thought, and subjugate it for a common goal. Â It sounds counterintuitive at first, Â but this is what the concept of â€œ[group think](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groupthink)â€ is all about. Â There is an entire social science built upon this subject and concept. Â When people begin to all share values and scientific beliefs, they become a sort of â€œteamâ€. Moreover, once you engage the â€œscience of teamâ€, it shuts down open minded thinking that goes against what the tribe believes to be true. Â Normally this is not a big issue, if things are truly correct scientifically, for those values or beliefs the group holds sacred.

_But what if they are not correct?_**Â  In my view, that is my role on the team. Â Someone has to challenge conventional thought even in an unconventional group of people. Â I am that guy. Â It makes me a target. Â I am OK with it, because I am looking out for our optimal health, and nothing else. Â This path got my own health back. Â I think it is worth sharing with you too.

What if you are not aware of something you never considered? Â  What my blog represents, is that guy, who dares to disagree with the current beliefs. Â I tell all my members and readers to question everything, including what I tell them, or write about. Â I already know I donâ€™t know everything, even though many of my critics will put words in my mouth that suits their agenda best.

One thing I do know about, is how this information help fix me and my family. Â It then helped me fix many patients. Â We did not get the results found on most paleo sites doing it their way. Â We used a different method. Â Because we did it differently does that mean they were wrong and we were right? Â No. Â It means we have more to learn about each method. Â When you can not get the results you see from the public testamonials we are all accustomed to seeing weekly, I want you to know why you canâ€™t. Â I do not want you to be depressed or shell shocked because the advice given elsewhere just does not work for you. Â I do not want you thinking you are broken deeply somehow. Â I rather enjoy a collaborative model of scientific exploration, that requires my thinking brothers and sisters, not to become echo chambers of the same beliefs the tribe currently has.**

In order to get different results we need to think differently about things at times. Â The science of DHA alone, tells you something is very off in the current belief structure. Â I want to beÂ _**that lone nut**_**, that gets you to look at something the whole tribe believes to be true for yourself. Â  But do it without having to worry about what the tribe thinks of you or your methods. Â That is what the comment section of my blog is for. Â Itâ€™s for you to exert your ability to think differently than you usually might given the tribeâ€™s beliefs. Â I want everyone who is sick and tired of being sick and tired to come here and read. Â I want the weary who have failed the Rx found in the medical books we have all read to come find out why. Â My blog is not for those of you who have found success easily. Â **It is for the people who have not found success for a reason.Â Â I want you to know why everyoneâ€™s mileage varies in this journey to optimal. Â I do not want you thinking there is something wrong with you. Â I want to teach how our â€˜Ferrariâ€™ really works, and for you to discover that you too can reclaim your health when you understand the owners manual you came with. Â I agree with most of what they say, but on brain biochemistry they do not have it all together. Â That is their puzzle. Â As a clinician my job is to make sense of our present biochemistry puzzle. Â Everyone of us is at a different point on the same continuum of human biochemistry. Â Deciphering where you are at now is my domain. Â There is a scientific epistemologic basis of how the laws of biochemistry apply to our species, and that is universal for our species. I am trying to show you how it works in an evolutionary context, and not how we think it works.

The concept of â€œteamâ€ requires confirmation in the face of adversity. Â I want to you embrace that discomfort with this information, and realize that all of what we believe may not be what we all thought. Â Will, you allow yourself to examine things for yourself andÂ dare to disagree**Â with your perceptions of your current truth?

Victory is had by opening eyes on both sides. Thereâ€™s room for many opinions, as long as we all warn the readers that with or without degrees, they have to take their own lives in their own hands**

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T16:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-prevention-pushed-by-doctors-female-breast-removal-before-age-35.4981/post-65868>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> When you limit options due to cost you are forcing........and this is surely happening daily in hospitals across america whether Darleen believes it or not. Â What it will foster is a second tier system for people who reject Darleen's beliefs ( not meant to piss ya off Darleen, but used to illustrate a point) for what they want to create in their own life.......and that choice will require a lot more wisdom smarts and money. Â Healthcare will be available to all but good care will become quite rare for the illness at hand. Â Evidence based medicine is medicine that gives the best smallest improvement for the lowest cost possible........regardless of the longterm outcome. Â the short term outcome is now how medicine will be practiced.......and most who advocate for this system do not understand the implications of this algorithm.........I do because I play in this sand box daily.

The issue has been for over 30 years health care COSTS which went completely out of control in the 1980's AFTER deregulation. It allowed FOR-PROFITS to buy up hospitals, doctor's private practices and insurance companies. Â It has become a system run by racketeers. Â It's no better than the mob.

Â 

When I was in my 20's we had the best health care in the world and the it was affordable for all. Â Business were happy to offer insurance coverage as an incentive to hire the best employees because it was affordable.

Â 

I have stated this all before and will now do so again. Â Just to make my point. At 22 I had an appendectomy. Â I was working for Retail Credit Corp as a secretary/investigator and they paid my health insurance premiums in full. Â When I came out of the hospital I didn't owe ANYone a dime. Â My insurance covered it all.

Â 

At 27 I tore the cartilage in my right knee. Â I was working on the assembly line at Teledyne Aqua Tec and as part of my wages they paid my insurance premiums. Â When I limped out of the hospital after having the cartilage removed I owed NOTHING.

Â 

then the 80's and deregulation hit. Â My right knee collapsed and it was grinding bone on bone. Â More surgery. Â THIS time the insurance company told me "too bad so sad" we're only going to ALLOW half your bill of which we will pay 80% and you can pay the other half PLUS 20% of the remainder. Now mind you, by this time DH was working for the BNRR and we had supposedly the best coverage OUR money could buy. Â That's right. Â Now it was coming out of our pocket as part of the wages contract. Â And it was well over $500/month (remember this was 30 years ago).

Â 

Then I had to have my gall bladder removed. Â I had to go to Billings to have it done because the local hospital didn't yet have the equipment to do the "laser" method which is the least invasive. Â AGAIN the blankety blank blank insurance company DISALLOWED a HUGE portion of the bill and then only paid 80% of the lesser amount. Â When I filed a complaint they said too bad so sad ask your surgeon the cut his bill. Â Now seriously Jack ... if you operated on someone and they then came back and said the insurance company said you charged too much would you lower your bill? Â didn't think so.

Â 

Ditto with my hysterectomy. Â Same song, same verse, different day.

Â 

The CEO of that insurance company, btw, retired with a near BILLION DOLLAR PAYDAY. Â Where'd the money come from? Â People like me who were denied reimbursement "just because" so the company could grow fat. Â Just like a blood sucking tick.

Â 

I hate insurance companies and what they have done. Â I hate the fact that Congress wrote this stupid assed bill (The Affordable Health Care Act) when they could have done one of two things to permanently fix this mess. Â They could have put the regulations back in place that kept the greed mongers at bay OR they could have opened Medicare to everyone. Â Single payer or regs. Â Either way would fix the problem.

Â 

that's MY opinion and until someone can show me otherwise I'm stickin' to it.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-30T12:02:29+0200

I reply, it is yoru insurance and you have the duty to know what is covered and not. Â My job as the surgeon is take real good care of you. Â When this has happened to my patients I have worked with them........why did it not in your case? Â Your choice to live in a place where there is no competition and you did not realize what precarious situation this could put you in when something went surgical in your case. Â It is not our fault you did not read it.............now look in the mirror and see who was responsible for it. Â Think learn then react. Â

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-12-01T18:01:29+0200

> The point I was making is that medical and insurance costs have soared out of control because of GREED.

Yes, I think socialized medicine is possibly better than letting people die. That's the case here in Spain. Nevertheless I think very different now of the medical establishment than two years ago. There is no effort to try to explore the causes of disease in order to prevent it. I am certainly guilty of having been gullible, thinking that the dietary advice was based on science. Certainly I contributed to rot the traditional diet of my family: margarine, low fat milk and everything else. I think that had quite a weight on the early demise of my mother (cancer) and my grandmother (heart attack). So I don't delegate our health in them now.

Â 

Going back to my point. Appendicitis is a western diet disease: [see minute 20 of the video posted by Dr. Kruse](http:///forum-2/educating-doctors/see-how-a-paleo-doc-has-to-navigate-the-waters-of-his-colleagues-to-get-the-message-thru/). As is commented on the iodine thread, fibroids and uterus problems may be due to iodine deficiency. Certainly orthopedic medicine will be still needed, but once all of the preventable disease are prevented the lighter burden on the system would do it affordable, both socialized or not.

And yes, I agree on [the GREED problem](http://http://www.honestmedicine.com/2009/03/burt-berkson-md-phd-talks-with-honest-medicine-about-his-work-and-our-medical-system-the-interview-t.html).

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T00:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-prevention-pushed-by-doctors-female-breast-removal-before-age-35.4981/post-66109>

> **Jack Kruse said
> You said, "Then I had to have my gall bladder removed. Â I had to go to Billings to have it done because the local hospital didn't yet have the equipment to do the "laser" method which is the least invasive. Â AGAIN the blankety blank blank insurance company DISALLOWED a HUGE portion of the bill and then only paid 80% of the lesser amount. Â When I filed a complaint they said too bad so sad ask your surgeon the cut his bill. Â Now seriously Jack â€¦ if you operated on someone and they then came back and said the insurance company said you charged too much would you lower your bill? Â didn't think so."
>
> Â 
>
> I reply, it is yoru insurance and you have the duty to know what is covered and not. Â My job as the surgeon is take real good care of you. Â When this has happened to my patients I have worked with them........why did it not in your case? Â Your choice to live in a place where there is no competition and you did not realize what precarious situation this could put you in when something went surgical in your case. Â Again, it may not seem right to you but you have the duty to know what is in your coverage not the doctor.........My job is to get you well.
>
> Everybody is a victim but it is hard to look in the mirror when the rules of the game are clear to those who see them........to those who dont and let the system react to their inaction the system seems bad..........Well sorry to pop your bubble. Â The surgeon should not enable your inaction in this case and maybe just maybe this is an excellent point to see where you choose to live might effect your healthcare. Â That point is going to be even bigger in Obama care. Â Be fair warned. Â You have already fallen prey once to bad thinking..........will you do it once again? Â Food for thought even thought the medicine here might be a bit bitter for ya. It is said with love but you need some windex on your glass eyes on this. Â Your emergency is not my problem.........especially when the rules were clearly laid out in a policy you signed up for and the surgeon did not. Â It is not our fault you did not read it.............now look in the mirror and see who was responsible for it. Â Think learn then react. Â** 

**
But Jack, you are once again missing my point. Â so let me back the truck up and take another run at it.

Â 

Insurance was initially formed to protect the insured. Â The way it is SUPPOSED to work is this. Â Let's use fire insurance as an example. Â 100 families form an insurance coop. Â They each pay in a set amount each month, let's make the arithmetic easy ... $10 per family. Â so their little coop is socking away $1000 a month or $12,000 a year. Â 5 years in, Farmer Jone's barn burns down. Â the insurance cooperative pays to put up Â a new barn.

All goes well until the coop account starts to get too big. Â Carl Carpetbagger gets wind of it and offers to "manage' their fund for a small fee. Â Pretty soon CC needs a bigger office and a secretary and a salesman and a new car and a bigger salary and pretty soon the expenses he's running up outstrip the money he feels he can pay out to the insureds. Â So now we're going to start putting conditions on how much an insured can have back of _THEIR MONEY._**

Â 

It's got nothing to do with where the farmers are living and everything to do with what has now become a protection racket because Carl Carpetbagger now has the Congress Critters in his back pocket and he sees to it (by writing the legislation) that everyone HAS TO BUY INSURANCE. Â Protection racket.

Why should ANYone have to live in a big city just so they can get decent health care at a reasonable cost? Â There are a LOT of doctors living here because this is a great area. Â the local hospital has become a PROFIT center which is BS in my book. Â they are not there to make a profit but to care for the sick. Â At least that's how it used to be. Â I am, unfortunately, old enough to remember how things used to work and they worked VERY WELL.

I will repeat ... insurance should NOT BE FOR PROFIT. Â It is money pooled by the INSUREDS to help defray costs in emergencies. Â for a CEO to STEAL a billion dollars from an insurance company as his "just compensation" while denying the insureds who put the money there to begin with!! is a crime that just takes my breath away.

I do NOT get why no one else seems to get this.

As to the OP ... it does not help that "celebrities" are now lopping off their breasts because they have the "cancer gene and OMG get rid of them quick!" Â People are not only sheep but they refuse to think for themselves. Â And then there is big pharma and (sorry Jack) the AMA not to mention the insurance companies and FOR PROFIT hospitals ... Once again I say FOLLOW THE (expletive deleted) MONEY. Â 

Â 

ETA, Jack I never said the surgeon was responsible. Â I was absolutely AGHAST that the insurance company would try to dump their share of the bill by telling ME to ask the surgeon to lower his bill. Â I REFUSED. Â You know why? Â Because the surgeon had to go learn new techniques so why should I ask him to cut his bill? I would not! Â It wasn't that he charged more than other places either. Â The insurance company had a responsibility to PAY THEIR FAIR SHARE. Â I was NOT the only person who went through this. Â It was NOT a matter of know what was in my policy. Â The point was they simply, as a matter of policy, DENIED large chunks of legitimate claims because they wanted to keep the money. Â Remember I have said it twice now ... the ceo of that insurance company retired a few years ago with a BILLION DOLLAR PAYDAY. Â Where do you think that money came from?? Â It came from the pockets of the insured and from denying every single claim that came across the claims desk.

# Thread: CTing in the shower, which BAT to focus spray on?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-27T18:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65723>

I have trouble getting in and out of my tub. It is perfect for CTing. Due to my stroke two years ago, I have trouble lifting my legs over the side to both get in and out.

So I continue to shower my CT. At my old house, the shower had a fixed shower head as well as a hand held. I was able to have spray from both because it was worn. Where I live now, I have both, but it is one or the other.

My question is should I let the water hit my back or chest? Is the BAT around the spine bigger than that around the sternum?

When I use the hand held, I can move it around, but I grow weary of this and actually can sit under the fixed shower head for longer periods of time.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-28T09:10:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65762>

Cold water on neck (carotid arteries) and upper back and chest is the best choice with this method

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T10:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65828>

Thank you Dr. Kruse.

I have found that sitting on my shower chair with the shower focused on my shoulders is easy to do.

Since I moved the chair in the shower to make this easier, I have found that I can spend more time in the shower and consequently get the water colder.

Now it will be perfect once I get to hear the radio or music as I CT.

# Thread: Jack Missed This About Leptin Rx.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-missed-this-about-leptin-rx.4992/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2

### Thor — 2012-11-28T02:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-missed-this-about-leptin-rx.4992/post-65749>

.

you can tellÂ me I'mÂ wrong go ahead but....Â  roughly 60% of the US population has atleast one genetic error on MTHFR C677T or 1298C.

Â 

Only two doctors in the entire US have a clue about the real problem this has on your health - Dr Ben Lynch out of Seattle and Dr Kendal Stewart out of Houston.Â  More than 70 disorders have the main cause rooted in MTHFR snp.Â  see [www.mthfr.net](http:///www.mthfr.net")Â 

Â 

Here's where things really get interesting.... We know the impact of Choline (B4)Â has with insulin resistance & leptin resistance plus fatty liver issues.Â  Choline main role is in what's called theÂ **Dawn Phenomenon (_similar disorder:Â Somogyi Effect).Â Â  And, choline has dozens of other areas it impacts ranging from fertility to immunity.

Â 

If you have the genetic hickupÂ MTHFR C677TÂ and/orÂ 1298CÂ - guess what.... you do not have any Choline in your system!Â  Due to how MTHFR works it "sucks" up any Choline you may have taken in from food.Â  With zero Choline available you're in HUGE trouble - that leads to Leptin and Insulin resistance on the fact track!Â 

Â 

Here's a brief geek breakdown:_**_

Choline and folate are interrelated in 1-carbon metabolism, mostly because of their shared function as methyl donors for homocysteine remethylation. Folate deficiency and mutations of methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) reduce the availability of a major methyl donor, 5-methyltetrahydrofolate, which in turn lead to compensatory changes in choline metabolism.

Choline generally refers to the various [quaternary ammonium salts](http:///forum-2/leptin-rx/"/wiki/Quaternary_ammonium_salts") containing the _N,N_,N_\-trimethylethanolammonium [cation](http:///forum-2/leptin-rx/"/wiki/Cation")

Â 

[http://www.annualreviews.org/doi/abs/10.1146/annurev.nutr.26.061505.111156](http:///www.annualreviews.org/doi/abs/10.1146/annurev.nutr.26.061505.111156")

Â 

Bottom line - get tested for MTFHR snp.Â  If you have the genetic hickup you'll need a larger dose of Choline than the average person. In addition you'll need a Doctor who can properly Tx the MTHFR defect.Â  You can never heal properly until the MTHFR issues is addressed.

Â 

I know first hand the hell this disorder causes - I nearly died from this!Â  /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_sad.gif Â Â Â Â  I'd love to chat with Jack about this sometime but I know he's super busy.Â 

Â

# Thread: Loss of Ask Jack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T10:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65826>

I particularly morn the loss of Ask Jack. Since my last visit to the thread before the conversion, there have been several threads started that pertain to me. I would love to read them.

It is extremely frustrating to be able to read the topics and not the actual threads.

In the past, I was able to afford the $48/month. Now I struggle to keep my family together and fed. That is a considerable sum to be able to read questions and answers. Some answers were merely directional signs to where to find the answer. A nearly $600/yr for the privilege to view the thread and maybe get an easy answer if you post a question.

When my business was open, I found that as I explained the dynamics of my product, I sold more. Because, I found with the better knowledge of how my product wore out and why, my customers did better, even if it was with one of my competitors.

May I suggest letting the thread be read, And as an enticement to be able to post a question in the thread, a much more moderate price for those of us who struggling in this economy. That way you can stair step people up to the more expensive memberships.

Thank you

John

### SueC — 2012-11-29T15:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65859>

I, too, lament the loss of Ask Jack, especially because so often questions by others would clarify points that I found vague in the blogs. I also cannot afford to upgrade at this time as my hubby was recently part of a massive layoff (102 of a total staff of 149!). Yet I'm really grateful for all the incredible information here that's available for free, including the months I got to read Ask Jack. It still astounds me.

I also wonder how on earth Jack could answer the many questions that appeared in Ask Jack along with all the other things he does to live his life. I don't know how the decision came about, but it seems reasonable to me to put a price tag on the thread simply to limit a little one of the many things he has to do in any given day...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-29T20:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65896>

Sue that is precisely why it had to happen.......my time is valuable to my health. Â It is not a $ decision it is a time management issue. Â I think I answer most questions on the blog........ask Jack is for the members who want and value my time. Â If this is not acceptable, please accept my apologies. It was my decision and no one else's. Â I try to give 150% of my abilities when I can. Â

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T20:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65902>

Dr. Kruse that is a clear and acceptable answer. I apologize for the BMG post. I had imagined that you were dictating the answers so they could be typed in.

And in truth you responded to my question on CTing when I was hoping to get a reply from AKman. It was his thread in MDA that turned me on to the process.

I look forward to bumping into you in the other threads as I bump along to optimal health and optimal earnings.

Thank You

John Downey

### SueC — 2012-11-30T16:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65991>

Jack,

You already give away an amazing amount for free and I'm glad to hear you're taking care of yourself. Thinking about the amount you do, I've been wondering for awhile if you'd burn out and ALL this would go away. None of us would want that. Thanks again for all you do.

The real power of Factor X is the survival power laws it gave to all eutherian mammals.......with their ability to adapt by altering the speed of the epigenetic programs on their genome. Â Mother Nature reached for greatness in the post K-T world.........today we are still feeling that ripple in disease generation cause by mismatches due to epigenetics........our ancestors survival was guaranteed by this maneuver......and now we are headed to extinction because we fail to see the other side of the sword in how this science could subjugate us.

Â 

Squeeze the juice of life from your grape every moment with intention and purpose. Â If you don't...... mediocre and moderate never produce exceptional or extraordinary. Â Second rate and substandard don't result in significant or substantial. Â Slightly or somewhat never yield passion or purpose Live fully and because living half heartedly is just a waste of time and energy. Â Today, should be the day you leave average at the alter.

Â 

This is probably the most important concept to get down from this website.........it affects everything.Â

# Thread: Newb Question about Leptin Reset and Obesity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-03T17:58:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/post-66254>

First off, I apologize if this is a stupid question.

Â 

I am rather obese, but still try to be fairly active. I am 54, and have type 2 diabetes, but my blood sugars are well in control. I am also on TRT.

Â 

Anyway, I am currently Leptin Resistant, and would like to become Leptin Sensitive, so I would like to begin the Leptin Reset.

Â 

I've read the outline as far as the foods to eat, eating within 30 mins of waking, limit carbs to under 50 grams per day...preferably 25-30 if wanting to lose weight, etc.

Â 

Suggestions that we follow a Paleo diet as outlined by Rob Wolff etc.

Â 

I notice that they recommend limiting nuts to 1-2 oz or even eliminating them during the reset due to their high fat content I presume.Â  Yet the Leptin Reset calls for ingesting a large amount of fat from what we've been lead to believe were unhealthy types/sources, such as Bacon fat.

Â 

So my question...is this Leptin Reset eating plan with all the fat for just those who don't need/want to lose weight/bodyfat? Or is it for everyone who wants to reset their Leptin sensitivity including those who are obese, and want to lose bodyfat?

Â 

Do those of us who are obese, still eat all that fat, bacon, pasture butter, cream etc?

Â 

Also if so, why are nuts "frowned upon" at the beginning, even though we're supposed to still consume all that other types of fat?

Â 

Finally, I see that coconut oil...which I love BTW, is suggested as a "spring & summer" fat, but not recommended for fall & winter. Why is that?

Â 

Again I apologize if these are stupid questions, but I want to find out before I do/eat something wrong.

Â 

Thanks in advance. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### nonchalant — 2012-12-03T18:17:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/post-66255>

Hi furface, welcome!

It definitely isn't the fat content of the nuts.Â  Jack loves fat, for everyone. Yes, you should eat lots of good animal fats, so that your body becomes a fat burner.

Nuts have a fair amount of carbs, and they also have LOTS of phytates and lectins, which aggravate a leaky gut and bind to minerals so you can't use them.

About coconut oil... If you are healthy, you should only eat coconut oil in the warmer months.Â  However, since you have weight to lose, the benefits of coconut oil in the winter outweigh any inflammatory effects from a circadian mismatch.Â  Knock yourself out.Â

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-03T18:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/post-66259>

First off no questions are stupid here in this site. Â Welcome. Â I love fats and protein for obesity reversal. Â The key thing is limiting carbs to the appropriate season ........when Obese I would even cut them back in summer until your brain is able to partition and account for nutrients as LS becomes natural. Â

### furface — 2012-12-03T18:53:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/post-66262>

First off, thank you Jack, and Nonchalant for the warm welcome. It's a pleasure to join your wonderful site/forum.

Â 

Next, thankÂ  you both for answering my questions, and providing me with the valuable information that I required.

Will try my best to eliminate Macadamia nuts, Pistachios, and Cashews (The only nuts I really like) from my diet.

Â 

I look forward to learning a lot more, and to turning my life/health around.

Â 

I just visited the U.S. Wellness Meats website, but unfortunately they don't ship to Canada. (Vancouver, B.C.) However we do have some Whole Foods outlets here, and other stores that sell grass-fed meats.

Â 

Finally, I also notice that chicken or turkey is not recommended. This is also going to be tough to give up, but I am willing to make the sacrifice.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-03T19:02:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newb-question-about-leptin-reset-and-obesity.5036/post-66264>

Sounds great.Â  Jack doesn't recommend cashews for anyoneÂ 

Â  so you might want to eliminate those first.

I forget why.

You might want to read about Jack's Epi-Paleo diet.Â  Or just follow the Paleo dietÂ but add lots of seafood.

Â 

[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http:///brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)

### furface — 2012-12-03T19:10:04+0200

Thanks again, Nonchalant.

Â

Â

An unexpected benefit (to Cting in holiday mode?)Â has been that my kids are now CTing too. Â Â SO proud of them!! It's inspiring to see tham adapting 1/soÂ quickly and 2/ so happily!

SoÂ we're going back to summertime and becauseÂ I still have work do do on my healthÂ I'm determined to keep the baths going as long as I can. I have a few ideas for getting more ice so I can stay close to 50F for a while anyway...

So, my questions are - **what have other people found good to keep CTingÂ over summer? Any tips with icing? Was someone hereÂ  - ealachan? Where is she lately? I miss her smiling face! - sleeping with an icepack on her neck at some point? Any other DIY icing gear intelligence anyone can share?**

### Eric — 2012-12-04T13:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-on-into-summer-few-s-for-those-who-have-gone-before.5037/post-66298>

I was able to go up to and maintain a **full shiver state** for 1-2hrs every day this last record-breaking hot summer. (in Pennsylvania, so it was also humid!)

Â 

Even with the A/C cranked to max, the lowest I could get the room was 69'.Â  Along with drinking some ice-water and wearing the CFB with only shorts, socks, and gloves, it didn't hinder my CT at all.Â  (I didn't go to full shiver every day, just when I felt like it; usually I just go to strong goosebumps/pre-shiver, especially since I'm at my target physique)

Â 

In fact, if you watch the "[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fatBDfjuWiQ\[/video\]\]How I eat junk food](http://[video=youtube_share;fatBDfjuWiQ)" video, I was able to drop over an inch per month off my waistline from early spring, into the summer.Â  (probably around April through July).

Â 

No, I'm not advising or celebrating eating like that -- that was just an amusing "experiment" I ran on myself, to see if I could "overcome" even garbage food with the combo of CT, power-endurance exercise, and except for that huge junk-food meal, a low/no carb meal for the rest of the day.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-04T14:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-on-into-summer-few-s-for-those-who-have-gone-before.5037/post-66313>

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-03T23:27:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flexible-seafood-soup.5039/post-66276>

There are no fixed amounts or ratios for the following recipe. It always seems to work out well.

This is actually a breakfast favorite of mine.

Sautee sliced mushrooms (any kind), shallots, minced garlic in generous amount of olive or avocado oil. (Don't be afraid of the fat)

Add curry powder and black pepper (I use obscene amounts of these. You do what you like.)

Heat in separate pot lobster or seafood stock. (Made with some seaweed and shells and bodies and heads of last seafood meal)

Deglaze sautee pan (pour hot stock in slowly while scraping all the yummies off the bottom of the pan) with stock and stir until sauce consistency.

Bring to low simmer.

Stir in lobster roe or fish roe (1/4 lb per person)

Now you have a base to add any or all of the following: head on shrimp, scallops,oysters,clams, squid,lobster or whatever creature of the sea you have on hand.

Simmer till seafood is cooked which is no more than 5 min or so for all the above.

This is a versatile dish open to modification. It is always delicious and amazingly filling. You can add some chopped greens with the seafood.

### caroline — 2012-12-04T03:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flexible-seafood-soup.5039/post-66281>

Thanks for posting the recipe - sounds very yummy!

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-04T14:44:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flexible-seafood-soup.5039/post-66315>

I'm making something similar tonight! Â I use clam juice I've saved. Â I'll saute some onions & garlic in bacon fat, toss in mixed seafood (mussels, squid, clams, cuttle fish, shrimp, and some mahi mahi, throw in the clam juice, lime juice and cilanto & tomatoes... season w/ old bayÂ 

and serve left over butternut squash... dinner is servered

### lee — 2013-01-22T03:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flexible-seafood-soup.5039/post-72262>

Learned my version of this in New England. It's a two night event.

First night, cook mussels in dry white wine. Throw in tied bunches of cilantro, whole peppercorns and whole garlic cloves.

Reserve most of the stock and some of the mussels for the next night, when you add in catfish (the only fish that will hold up) scallops, shrimp, etc..

Next time I'm going to try adding it to the pan like you described with the garlic and yummies!

The stock is wonderful. I use more fresh cilantro the second day.

Cheers,

Lee

### Braden — 2013-01-26T08:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flexible-seafood-soup.5039/post-72708>

I'm creating something identical tonight! \*I use clam juice I've stored. \*I'll saute some vegetables & beans in bread fat, throw in combined seafood (mussels, squid, clams, cuttle seafood, shrimp, and some mahi and serve with little garnish of coriander......

### Rachelle — 2012-12-05T03:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-66421>

Mayo is basicallly this recipe from Nom Nom but I use avocado oil and MTC oilÂ http://nomnompaleo.com/post/3440774534/macadamia-nut-oil-mayonnaise

Â 

For me it makes tartare more easier to swollow..lol if you know what I mean

Â 

The ceviche I love is made with Mango, jalapeno pepper, red onion, cilantro, lime juice and a bit of honey. You can use any type of white fish but I find bay scallops are really the good in this recipe. Lots of options like tomato, orange juice, red pepper, celery..you can use what you like. Let marinate a few hours or more.

Just do it.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-05T22:28:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-66525>

OK it is:
Â 
Salmon Tartar
3/4 # Salmon cut into 1/4" dice
1/4 cup capers, drained but not rinsed
1/4 cup green onions, finely chopped (both white and green parts)
2 Tbsp Dijon mustard (can use a little less)
2 tsp pureed Chipotles (come in a little can in Mex food section- I just chop mine)
2Â Tbsp Olive Oil
3 Tbsp Cilantro choppedÂ 
Â 
Mix salmon, capers and onions.
Combine mustard and chipotles.Â  Drizzle mixture over salmon.
Drizzle olive oil and mix thoroughly.
Add cilantro and mix thoroughly
Â 
To add a punch to it you can drizzle a little Tequila over it after all mixed up and ready to go.
Â 
It should refrigerated for a couple of hours if you have time. Yummy!

### Rachelle — 2012-12-08T16:52:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-66844>

It's pretty hard to get fresh salmon where I live so I always use the sushi grade I get frozen from Alaska. Sushi grade is determined by how the salmon was handled from catching it to it's packaging Â Certainly, I wouldn't expect it to go to China first, Â like many of the fish is handled these days. I will buy Whole Paychecks if I, absolutely, have to but I highly doubt the freshness. The Alaskan frozen salmon I buy from a legit source is much better than theirs. The best way to tell a fish is fresh is to touch it...should be NO slime and only smell of the ocean. Whole Foods tried to say it was the oil in the fish that made it slimy to the touch, but that's just another lie I caught them at.

Â 

Can you tell I am not a Whole Foods fan? I tolerate them just to get what I cannot get elsewhere.

### jayjha1@gmail.com — 2012-12-10T13:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-67013>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> i have eaten raw fish every night for the last three weeks.......results have been nothing short of wow. Â 

I have done it the last 2 nights only and am pretty freakin' stunned as to how good I feel.

Yesterday I chopped up 5oz of raw wild salmon and 3 oz of wild dover sole.Â  To that I added 1lb (in shell) of steamed mussels that I removed from the shell.Â  I tossed the whole thing with fish sauce, lime, tons of fresh ginger, serrano pepper, cilantro, a teaspoon of unheated honey and a tablespoon of organic evoo.Â 

Â 

It was delicious and I was just in the best mood all evening and this morning.Â  Gonna try this again today for dinner - I ran out of salmon but I got about 13oz of wild dover sole that I'll be chopping up tonight.

### furface — 2012-12-10T15:13:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-67022>

Not sure if this would qualify as a raw salmon recipe, but it does use raw salmon, and I've actually tried it while at a luau in Hawaii, and it was very refreshing. Also not sure if it would be Epi-Paleo approved, but I'll post it anyway.

Â 

Lomi Lomi Salmon

Â 

**Ingredients

-   24 oz Salmon (or However Much Salmon You Desire)
    -   Salt
        -   7-10 medium size tomatoes OR 3 large ones1 1/2 cup green onions1 large sweet yellow onion (Maui Onion, Sweet Walla Walla Onion etc.)

Â 

Directions**

Makes about 10 servings

1\. Wash off salmon. Rub salt all over the fish and wrap in saran wrap. (clear wrapper). Place in fridge for 8 hours or overnight.

2\. Remove from fridge and place in a bowl with ice cold water. Replacing water every 30 minutes for a total of 1 hour.

3\. Wash off well and remove skin.

4\. Cut into small cube pieces.

5\. Cut tomatoes in halves, remove seeds, and dice. Dice onion as well. Chop green onions.

6\. In a new clean bowl, add salmon, tomatoes, onion, and green onions. Mix it well.

\*\*\*\*Lomi Lomi in hawaiian means to massage so make sure your hands are clean and lomi away /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_smile.gif

7\. Place in fridge, let it chill and serve when ready.

Number of Servings: 10

Â

### furface — 2012-12-10T16:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-salmon.5041/post-67034>

I'm sure real salt would be good.

Â 

**Kalua Pig in a Slow Cooker

**Â 

**Ingredients:

Â 

-

    1 (6 pound) pork butt roast

-   1 1/2 tablespoons Hawaiian sea salt

    -   1 tablespoon liquid smoke flavoring

    **Directions

    \[list type=decimal\]
    \[\*\]Pierce pork all over with a carving fork. Rub salt then liquid smoke over meat. Place roast in a slow cooker.
    \[\*\]Cover, and cook on Low for 16 to 20 hours, turning once during cooking time.
    Remove meat from slow cooker, and shred, adding drippings as needed to moisten.

    Â 

    Or...

    Â 

    **Oven Kalua Pork

    **Â 

    **Ingredients:

    **Â** 

    5 pounds pork butt roast

    1 tablespoon liquid smoke flavoring
    ********

********

Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  2 1/2 tablespoons Hawaiian sea salt, divided

Â 

Directions**

Â** 

Preheat oven to 325 degrees F (165 degrees C). Rub liquid smoke and 1 1/2 tablespoons of the salt into the skin of the pork. Wrap well in foil, and seal completely. Place in a roasting pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until an internal temperature of 145 degrees F (63 degrees C) is reached, about 5 hours. Remove from oven and let cool before shredding. Sprinkle the shredded meat with the remaining 1 tablespoon of salt.
\[/list type=decimal\]
Â 

Best eaten with your fingers after it's cooled down a bit.

Â

Â** 

Â** 

Â** 

Â 

Â**

# Thread: hs CRP vs wide range wr CRP
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hs-crp-vs-wide-range-wr-crp.5047/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2012-12-04T14:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hs-crp-vs-wide-range-wr-crp.5047/post-66316>

My hs-CRP result from August was 1.84 mg/L (through Directlabs).

Last week I requested an hs-CRP test from Kaiser. The result (0.8 mg/L) came back specified instead as Wide Range CRP (wr-CRP) measurement. I had not heard of this, but just found this paper:

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16018882](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16018882)

Â 

The paper claims little difference in accuracy, but I was wondering if anyone has used this measurement method? Do I have two data points I can use or should I retest with hs-CRP?

Â 

thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-04T20:28:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hs-crp-vs-wide-range-wr-crp.5047/post-66359>

one test alone is never something to base treatment on but a battery of test that all point the same direction is actionable just sayin'

### JoeBranca — 2012-12-04T20:52:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hs-crp-vs-wide-range-wr-crp.5047/post-66375>

Yep, I certainly intend to follow on with further tests. I'm just trying to determine if this recent wr-CRP result can be used to compare with the previous hs-CRP result from August to begin to show a trend, or do I throw it out since a different method of measurement was used? I'm done with Kaiser after 2012, presumably future tests will again be hs-CRP.

# Thread: Few more questions about Leptin Reset
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-04T16:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66318>

I have a few more questions regarding the Leptin Reset.

Â 

1) If I'm already obese, won't eating all of this extra fat, even in lieu of carbs still make me gain weight/bodyfat until my body becomes Leptin sensitive, and begin to lose weight/fat? (I don't want my clothes to stop fitting before I begin to lose weight.)

Â 

2) How long before our stomachs/system gets used to eating all of this extra fat? (May not always have a washroom handy.)

Â 

3) How does one know if they have a "leaky gut"?

Â 

4) Is type 2 diabetes an AI disease, and should tomatoes, bell peppers, and "hot" spices such as cayenne, chili powder etc be avoided?

Â 

Thanks again.

### furface — 2012-12-05T02:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66416>

I guess no one knows the answers, or else I've asked in the wrong forum.

Â 

Maybe I should just delete the questions.

### Destiny — 2012-12-05T07:51:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66426>

Furnace,

I'll give it a try.

Â 

You might gain some weight at the beginning but if you stay at it, you will lose. I think the change is a change and it doesn't happen overnight.

Â 

The stomach/system issue is very individual. I had a lot of problems for almost 8 months but I have no gallbladder. What helped me was Ox bile and Digestive enzymes.

Â 

For a leaky gut, I would go back to the older blogs from Dr. Kruse. It is not an explanation on a couple of lines but you could start with the labs.

Â 

I don't know about the diabetes/AI question.

Â 

Good luck!

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-05T09:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66434>

Welcome, you wil most likely have no issues as you start to eat lots of fat. If you eat lot's of fat, which I recommend, make sure you reduce carbs dramatically or you will have issues. Eating lots of fat is a challenge because you ave been told it's bad. Eat fatty meat, eat seafood, drown your green veg (the only carbs you should eat now) in fat; butter, olive oil, bacon fat, lard. You will feel full and satisfied and as the carbs leave your system you will be calmer with less gas and bloating. As with many things addictive I find the cold turkey approach to carbs the best. The sooner you have it out of your system the easier it is. Try to find some raw cold pressed coconut oil and start using it liberally. Many people have constipation issues initially. The remedy is.....more fat. Don't just fill up on lean protein like steak or chicken. Do not eat anything labeled lo-fat. Read food labels sugar is in almost every food product.

I am T2D with Hashimoto's Thyroiditis. I don't think T2D is AI, but Hashi's is for sure. If you are T2D you may unfortunately find out you have other issues. This disease rarely travels alone. Thyroid issues are common. You will need further testing to determine if the solanacea family( tomatoes,peppers, potatoes) are a problem for you.

Start reading the Quilt and the blogs. It's all in there and the learning is important. When YOU learn it YOU own it. That's where the power to make these changes come from. Good luck.

### Linz — 2012-12-05T11:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66444>

Furface,

I'm not sure I can give you the best answers either but don't worry about eating fat at all. Just make sure you use the right ones.

I was only a bit overweight when I changed but had quite severe digestive problems and I had too much weight loss and very up and down digestive symptoms for several months, certainly not constipation! I think it does take some time to adapt to fat burning. I would change the fats and eliminate gluten and all grains immediately as well as sugar and sweet starches but perhaps keep some of the starchy vegetables for a while - just not too many. Replace starches with extra green vegetables in the beginning and eat lots of fish and shellfish.

After a lifetime of hearing fat is bad it is not an easy change but add fat to all your green veg as Dali says, don't skim soup stocks and pour the bacon fat onto your vegetables. Hindsight is the best way to learn for your own circumstances as we are all so different. It's so much easier to look back and see the changes andÂ  once you start to see real differences for yourself there is an incredible amount of knowledge here to interpret them.

I think most who have lived a lifetime of SAD have gut problems to sort out.

Best of luck

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-05T12:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66447>

great advice right there........

### freesia — 2012-12-05T16:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66479>

I just want to echo what above posters said.
...Becoming fat adapted painlessly seems to be a naturalÂ process asÂ you cut carbs. Coming into winter where you are this should suit your body well { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### furface — 2012-12-05T18:45:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66496>

Thanks everyone for your wonderful and very helpful answers.

Â 

I know it's hard toÂ  "erase" what we've been taught for all these years about fat being bad for us, and carbs being the best source of energy etc.

Â 

I love coconut oil, and just bought myself a jar of Nutiva. (It was only a small jar...that's all they had in stock, but that should suffice me for a couple of weeks until I get paid.)

Â 

I've severely reduced my carb intake, to where my only carbs have been either a few spears of asparagus, some brocolli, a few pieces of 99% Dark Chocolate...not bad actually, and some baby bok choy which I had for dinner last night along with some bacon, and a grass-fed ground beef burger. The baby bok choy in the bacon grease was delicious!

Â 

I've even begun taking cold water showers after my regular shower in the morning...a great waker upper. So I'm beginning to get more and more "into" this lifestyle. I already have a lot more energy, and I hope it just continues.

### caroline — 2012-12-05T19:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66503>

Everyone has given you so much great advice. I would just add - once you adapt to all the fat etc. your cravings completely go away and your appetite decreases. Now I use food strictly as fuel for my body and I want to use the best fuel possible. It has been such a revelation to not always be thinking of food. I only eat when hungry and then only the best stuff. Really liberating actually! I do sometimes think I would like an ice cream cone but I doubt I could eat one!

### furface — 2012-12-05T19:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66505>

Good points, Caroline. I think I'm already beginning to feel the "feeling full" effects of eating "extra" fats.

As far as the ice cream cone goes, I don't think one cone would hurt you, and in fact, you...or people who have their LS in tact, and their Paleo/Epi-Paleo diet down pat, could probably enjoy it WITHOUT any "fear" or "regrets" afterward (i.e. "Damn I shouldn't have done that!") as a "reward" to themselves BECAUSE they have control of their diet. IOW, YOU/THEY control their food...food DOESN"T control them.

Â 

Just a thought.

Â 

Nothing wrong with enjoying something, or "treating" one's self once in a while.

### caroline — 2012-12-05T20:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66507>

Good points, Caroline. I think I'm already beginning to feel the "feeling full" effects of eating "extra" fats.

As far as the ice cream cone goes, I don't think one cone would hurt you, and in fact, you...or people who have their LS in tact, and their Paleo/Epi-Paleo diet down pat, could probably enjoy it WITHOUT any "fear" or "regrets" afterward (i.e. "Damn I shouldn't have done that!") as a "reward" to themselves BECAUSE they have control of their diet. IOW, YOU/THEY control their food...food DOESN"T control them.

Â 

Just a thought.

Â 

Nothing wrong with enjoying something, or "treating" one's self once in a while.

I definitely don't think there isn't anything wrong with enjoying yourself - but my thinking has changed. I wouldn't feel guilty about having an ice cream cone but I don't think I would really like it now. It would be way to sweet etc. In time your thinking will change too. It isn't that you can't have something - it is more that if you do you will feel physically not well and not enjoy it either. I am sure all of us have been there and tried something we used to love and the attraction just isn't there anymore. Now I love dark chocolate and a glass of wine and some really great blue cheese. You are thinking - I think - that we are all suffering and missing the pleasures of life. That is so not the case. I feel fantastic .....and I a not giving that up for an ice cream cone!!

### caroline — 2012-12-05T20:30:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66510>

Â A friend who is an amazing professional baker always makes me an enormous Pavlova with all kinds of fresh cream and fresh fruit. Â This has always been my favorite and I usually eat huge amounts this one time - at Christmas. Â I think I might just eat the cream and fruit this year!!

### furface — 2012-12-05T20:55:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66516>

Good points again.

Â 

I just don't think we should let food, or ANY type of eating plan "control" us. We should still be the one in control, and not think or feel that having something we've been told is "bad" for us once in a blue moon is going to make us feel awful, or ruin all of our progress so we become "scared" or even "brainwashed" to think that we can never have it, or if we do we "won't" enjoy it because we've been "programmed" to ONLY enjoy eating this "new" way. Not saying that there's anything wrong with eating this way...I'm fully committed, but that doesn't, or better yet, SHOULDN'T mean that we shouldn't be allowed to enjoy something that we used to enjoy, once in a blue moon. IOW I still want to be in control of my life, and my enjoyment. I still want to be in control of myself...not some diet plan. I'm still a human, NOT a robot.

Â 

Besides, I don't think ONE ice cream cone once in a blue moon would KILL anyone. (Unless of course they have specific health issues related to the ingredients.)

Â 

But hey, in a few months, I may feel exactly the same way that you do.

Â 

I just HATE to be told WHAT TO DO, and have a proverbial GUN held to my head, and think I don't have any say in the matter.

Â 

I realize that this may be overreacting, and again, not trying to knock anything, just expressing my current opinion. I love my freedom. I LOVE my life, and I want to be able to enjoy it the way I want to enjoy it.

### furface — 2012-12-06T00:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66531>

I completely concur with the fact that eating extra fat will make you feel fuller.

Â 

For dinner I only had 2 slices of bacon, a small boneless pork chop, and 6 thin spears of asparagus cooked in the bacon fat, along with one tablespoon of Nutiva coconut oil, and that was nearly 3 hours ago, and I still feel SO FULL!!

Â 

I can see how this way of eating would clearly lower your total food intake on a daily basis.

### caroline — 2012-12-06T03:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66536>

Good points again.

Â 

I just don't think we should let food, or ANY type of eating plan "control" us. We should still be the one in control, and not think or feel that having something we've been told is "bad" for us once in a blue moon is going to make us feel awful, or ruin all of our progress so we become "scared" or even "brainwashed" to think that we can never have it, or if we do we "won't" enjoy it because we've been "programmed" to ONLY enjoy eating this "new" way. Not saying that there's anything wrong with eating this way...I'm fully committed, but that doesn't, or better yet, SHOULDN'T mean that we shouldn't be allowed to enjoy something that we used to enjoy, once in a blue moon. IOW I still want to be in control of my life, and my enjoyment. I still want to be in control of myself...not some diet plan. I'm still a human, NOT a robot.

Â 

Besides, I don't think ONE ice cream cone once in a blue moon would KILL anyone. (Unless of course they have specific health issues related to the ingredients.)

Â 

But hey, in a few months, I may feel exactly the same way that you do.

Â 

I just HATE to be told WHAT TO DO, and have a proverbial GUN held to my head, and think I don't have any say in the matter.

Â 

I realize that this may be overreacting, and again, not trying to knock anything, just expressing my current opinion. I love my freedom. I LOVE my life, and I want to be able to enjoy it the way I want to enjoy it.

Maybe I will try a different approach - Dr. Kruse has said many, many times that he is just shining a light on stuff. This is my road to travel and I want to be as healthy as possible. No one is holding a gun to my head and no one is making me do this. I love my life too and that is why I am doing it. Everyone's journey is very different.
He also has said many times that no one cares about our health as much as we do - so it is up to us to read and learn and ask questions and do whatever we need to - to get where we want to go. A lot of us here have been on an incredible ride with Dr. K the last eighteen months. It hasn't been easy especially for me - I don't have a scientific bone in my body and so I find a lot of the language difficult but I keep trying and there are so many really smart, helpful, caring people on this forum. Dr. Kruse wants us to be excited about our life and live every second to the fullest and he is giving us the tools to do it if we so choose. I know - for me- I keep reading and trying different things and finally a lot of things have come together - a light bulb has finally gone on.
For me the most important piece in the puzzle was my circadian rhythm and getting the proper testing. That has made the biggest impact.
sorry about the soapbox!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-06T09:30:30+0200

That post is likely going to help more people than anything I write. Â We need more people to pour their words into this community to create more starfish. Â

### kzimm — 2012-12-06T13:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-more-questions-about-leptin-reset.5048/post-66556>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> That post is likely going to help more people than anything I write. Â We need more people to pour their words into this community to create more starfish. Â 

Since October 2011, I've been here at the school of Jack.Â  I haven't posted, as Caroline saidÂ Â  "I don't have a scienticfic bone in my body".Â  I am, however, able to take the information and make it actionable.

I've watered it down to a 3 prong approach.Â  One - Live within circadian/seasonal cycles - be it food or exposure to light.Â  Two - eat paleo with an emphasis on seafood, sea veggies, fat - veggies cooked and meats as raw as possible.Â  (Under the food umbrella, timing is key.Â  No eating 4-5 hours before sleep, etc.)Â  Three - your cells need to experience cold.

Â 

All of the posts interweave information that you need to read.Â  We are all different.

Paraphrasing Jack - years of living in a modern world eating a SAD diet are not undone overnight.

You may have to start with labs so you know what to address first.Â  It takes time.Â  It's a choice to change.Â  Keep a journal if that helps you.

Â 

My own experience has been a gradual,Â plotted course.Â  It's been drilled here over and over - food is information for your hormones not metabolic fuel.Â  Once one wraps one's head around the fact "just because you eat it, doesn't mean it's food"Â  it starts to fall into place.Â  This is coming from someone who used Ben and Jerry as counselors.

### shilohman — 2012-12-04T22:10:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-members-i-need-your-input.5049/post-66386>

Podcasts - Yes, they add a different perspective to your answers sometimes. Â I have noticed that on the podcasts you are limited in time with your answers so you tend to be more concise and simplified with your answers, very helpful.

Evolution - For me, I have a hard enough time trying to understand the technical science stuff, the evolution stuff just gets in the way.

Philosophy - Yes, this is good. Â Often times you have to put a phiosophical view point to explain alot of what is covered in this forum.

Bio Hack - Yes, Yes, Yes. Â I find this very interesting. Â Even without the data, would like a better understanding of why the biohack. For example the biohack you did early this year with the liposuction and then MRSA injections, why was the liposuction necessary? Â Was there a reason why you picked a bacteria (MRSA) and not a virus? Â  Â 

Q&A worth it - Personally, I have gotten a lot out the Q&As. Â Have only attended one live, but I always go through the recorded ones. Â The Q&As ask more specific and directed questions than the podcasts. Â The podcast questions are more general.

One thing that I would like to see added is a monthly case study/hypothetical consult. Â For example, I think it is in the T3 forum Destiny discusses how she felt that she had cleared the rT3 stored in the cells of her body only to find that she after doing so, she feels worse now than before. Â So how about look at applying Destiny's situation to a hypothetical consult where you cover things like: Â How to determine that your body has truly cleared rT3 from your cells (testing?). Â What type things can happen once the rT3 is cleared to increase rT3 and what things can be done with the diet, supplements etc. to overcome this type of problem. Â In other words, present a specific situation and then discuss resolutions along with implications of fixing the leaky gut, balancing the HPA axis, getting the omega 6/3 ratio optimal, that kind of thing.

That's my two cents.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-04T22:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-members-i-need-your-input.5049/post-66387>

Great comments.Â  ++Podcasts!

I would like someone to make some short videos explaining, for the general public, some of the concepts you have taught us.

Like the part of the Hormone101 blog post explaining the conversion of cholesterol to steroid hormones.Â  Or perhaps the circadian cycle of hormones.Â  Or maybe avoiding light at night and seeking it in daytime.

They could cover the subject lightly, to peak curiosity, then refer people to this website for more info.

Maybe a contest for best video idea?

### Awainer1 — 2012-12-04T22:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-members-i-need-your-input.5049/post-66390>

Yes on the biohacks. Like the wbinar over podcast. There are too many podcasts out there as it is and I look forward to the webinar every month. More personal than a podcast. Dont really feel the Q & A is all that necessary as it gets sidetracked with other questions and there is so much info in the webinars that its enough to take in. Really excited and a little bit nervous to hear about the recent biohack. But when you know better you do better. Love the evolution and philosophy would like to hear more about lifestyle and what to do with all this info. Would love to hear your views on how to apply this to where you live/type of work/daily stressors. A lot of people dont have a way to make passive income and spend time living perfectly optimal. Since getting into paleo I quit my job in an office and work outside at a golf course making less money but living minimally and with much less acute stress. Would like to hear you expand on stuff like that.

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-04T22:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-members-i-need-your-input.5049/post-66391>

Hi Dr. K

Â 

Here are some of my thoughts...the webinars have been great. Podcasts would be great too. I like listening to the information. It compliments reading the blogs. The Q and A's have been a mixed bag. When there aren't a lot of people involved it seems to go off track pretty easily...then becomes more of a social gathering. That's not a bad thing, but it may not be worth your time and effort if there are only a few people. Perhaps something to commit to, or sign up for, maybe schedule one every two months? Not sure what the answer is.Â 

Â 

I like a separation between evolutionary mind-blowing biology and your philosophical musings in the blogs. The education we are receiving is fantastic. I really appreciate the evolution stuff when it is written and edited carefully. It makes it so much easier to study and retain. When it comes to your philosophy...well, let 'er rip. I'd rather read the stream of conscious writing that comes straight from your heart...who cares about the editing then! I think the balance between the two has been good... I like it.

Â 

All the bio-hack stuff is just really interesting to know...and even without data, the information will be welcome.

Â 

I think the site has improved consistently. Certainly an opportunity to examine how we all deal with change! I'm curious as to how the different levels of membership have changed the numbers of people reading and using the site.Â 

Â 

As I said awhile ago...I've stopped posting for the most part and just check in to read and keep up with new stuff...but that is because I'm feeling SO MUCH BETTER!!! I finally have some energy (manufactured by me, not outside stimulants) Â to go out in the world and do things....it's an amazing feeling. I'm still not where I want to be, but I'm on my way. Without the support and information you and this forum provide, this wouldn't have happened for me.Â 

Â 

Hope all the changes in your life lead to great things too. SeaHorse

I grew up in Northern Colorado (for those of you who missed it). Â Summers in my most of my childhood were spent up in the Rockies at a cabin my Mom and Grandmom bought just outside Allenspark. Â I slept wonderfully there, was more relaxed, enjoyed the quiet and the sound of the wind in the pines, the scents, everything about it. Â 

Since most of my ancestry is Northern European (and hazarding a guess here until I can spring for the 23andme test ... a healthy dollop of Asian) I am not at all surprised that I love the woods and the altitude. Â There is a peace there that I simply can't define. Â And just let me add it has been ruined. Â The last outing we took to the woods the morons who were there were playing their boomboxes at an ear splitting volume, there were campers with satellite dishes on them, no one was walking anywhere they were tearing up the terrain with their atv's. Â god I sometimes really hate "civilization."

Moving North to Wyoming put us at a slightly lower altitude which took me a long time to adjust to. Â Whenever I would return from a visit to Fort Collins I would feel a sense of dread steal over me the closer I got to Sheridan. Â Some people claim it's because we're located in close proximity to an energy center (the Medicine Wheel up on the mountain) but I'm not really a crystals and chanting kinda gal so I never fully bought into that. Â The change in elevation is only about 1500 feet but it apparently is enough of a difference.
