# Thread: When starting out on the Leptin Rx is it MANDATORY to have lots of fat every meal?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-07T00:25:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66639>

Not sure if this should be in the Leptin Rx forum, or the Optimal Nutrition forum so if it should be moved, please feel free to do so and I apologize for posting it in the wrong forum.

Â 

Just wondering when a person is just starting out on the Leptin Reset, is it MANDATORY to have plenty of fat with EVERY meal? I realize that this is one of the major keys/components to making this all work, but would it be truly detrimental if a person were to have one or two meals a week if they wanted, where they didn't have a large amount of fat with the meal? Just to give their stomach/system a little break?

Â 

Then once they've become more adapted to eating this way, they can eat plenty of fat with every meal.

Â 

I'm speaking mainly of the "added" fat such as bacon grease/fat, added coconut oil etc. Not the fat that is included in the actual food that they are eating. Even if cooking bacon, instead of eating all the fat drippings during that meal for instance, could they put it in a container, and use it later?

### vkiernan — 2012-12-07T08:59:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66658>

> **furface said**
> Not sure if this should be in the Leptin Rx forum, or the Optimal Nutrition forum so if it should be moved, please feel free to do so and I apologize for posting it in the wrong forum.
>
> Just wondering when a person is just starting out on the Leptin Reset, is it MANDATORY to have plenty of fat with EVERY meal? I realize that this is one of the major keys/components to making this all work, but would it be truly detrimental if a person were to have one or two meals a week if they wanted, where they didn't have a large amount of fat with the meal? Just to give their stomach/system a little break?
>
> Then once they've become more adapted to eating this way, they can eat plenty of fat with every meal.

I'm speaking mainly of the "added" fat such as bacon grease/fat, added coconut oil etc. Not the fat that is included in the actual food that they are eating. Even if cooking bacon, instead of eating all the fat drippings during that meal for instance, could they put it in a container, and use it later?

You don't need added fat at every meal and not quite sure where you got that from.Â  And you certainly don't need to eat all the bacon fat when cooking it.Â  I always saved my bacon grease for cooking in.Â  Fat helps satiate.Â  So if you get hungry before your next meal, then eat more fat to help tide you over.Â  Otherwise, eat the right things and don't worry about it.Â  With the Leptin Rx, the main idea is the BAB with at least 50 gr of protein.Â  Then of course eat as much epi-paleo as you can.Â  You'll be fine.Â  Throw any questions out here that you like!Â  We will all help you however needed.

### furface — 2012-12-07T11:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66681>

Thanks vkiernan. That's good to know about the fat. I do feel so much fuller when I do consume a lot of the fat, but at times it makes me feel "too full." Which I guess is the idea behind it. The fuller you feel, the less you eat. DUH!! lol

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-08T10:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66801>

> I do eat fat with every meal, but I don't believe it's a "requirement".Â  Yes, the fat makes you feel very full, which is a great thing!Â  Remember, your brain is >60% fat, so you need fat to help your brain make these improvements.Â  So the more fat you eat, actually, the better!Â  But if you eat a certain meal that doesn't contain any fat and you just don't feel like it, well don't worry about it.Â 
>
> A key component is the BABÂ plus the fact that you will only eat once or twice more each day.Â  We are a society so accustomed to snacking every 2-3 hours to "stoke" our metabolism, which is just so false.Â 
>
>
>
> The funniest thing I've noticed ever since going on the LR in Jan. 2012 is watching how much, and how often, other people eat.Â  I've been at airports waiting for a flight, and about 80% of the people can't seem to wait 1 hour for a flight without eating something.Â  They are ALL snacking.Â  Once, the crunching and the sounds of the plastic bags they were eating from annoyed me so much I had to change to another waiting area.Â  It occurred to me that that used to be me:Â  I'd be first in line at those little airport convenience stores, stocking up on snacks for the waiting area + the flight.Â  Heaven forbid I go 4-5 hours without food.Â  These days, it doesn't even OCCUR to me to eat for 5-7 hours after the last time I ate.
>
> Furface, it sounds like you're doing great here and asking all these questions is terrific.Â  We've ALL been there, just finding Dr. K., and we ALL know how overwhelming it can be.Â  So please, ask away.
>
> Â 
>
> Â 
>
> Â 
>
> Â

### furface — 2012-12-08T11:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66804>

Thanks Pam, very much appreciated. I also appreciate the encouragement. I think the biggest obstacle to overcome is being able to break "bad" habits, and getting out of an old routine, and starting a new, much healthier one.

Â 

As far as asking so many questions, I'm sure a lot of the answers are in the blogs, but to be totally honest, a lot of the techno babble jargon can get confusing, esp if the answer a person is looking for is somewhere in the middle of all of that scientific mumbo jumbo. I've always been a straight to the point, drive or get off the road, no lollygagging type of person, so while at times knowing how, or why something works is interesting, or something that I'd like to know, more often than not, I just want to know WHAT works.

Â 

It's like if you've ever seen the old TV show, "All In The Family", I'm sure you'll remember scenes when Edith would be telling Archie a story, she'd be going on, and on, and on, putting in endless, mind numbing, at times totally irrelevant details, and Archie would be sitting there twirling his hand for Edith to "hurry up and get to the point", while rolling his eyes, sometimes even pretending to put a bullet in a gun, spin the cylinder, aim it at his head, pretend to pull the trigger, "miss", spin the cylinder again, aim, fire, then shake his head as if he'd been shot...only to have Edith still not have gotten to the point. That's how I feel at times when I'm reading through all of that techno jargon trying to find an answer to my question.

So at times I just think it's faster asking it on the forum.

Â 

No offense meant to anyone whatsoever about the long, drawn out explanations, or techno jargon, it is very impressive, and does show just how intelligent whoever wrote it is, but as I said, most often I, and I'm sure a few others would just like a simple answer to their question if it is at all possible.

Â 

As far as eating only 2-3 times per day, with no snacking in between, I think I'm already getting used to that, and the BAB is definitely key. And I've been one of those who have been on the "eat 5-6 small meals, one every 2-3 hours each day" eating regimen for a while. Esp when I was going to the gym 6 days per week.

Â 

So thanks again for the help, I apologize for all the rambling, and look forward to a new, happy, healthier, me.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-08T21:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66896>

furnace you would do well to listen to the Dec Webinar when it goes live to explain to you why the Leptin Rx is critical..........

### furface — 2012-12-08T22:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66906>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> furnace you would do well to listen to the Dec Webinar when it goes live to explain to you why the Leptin Rx is critical..........

Thanks Dr. Kruse, I will.

Â 

And again, I meant no disrespect whatsoever with all the "techno babble" talk. It's just that reading it all can get confusing, and like I said, I'm a "get to the point" type of person. So I apologize if I've offended you, or anyone else, in any way whatsoever.

Â 

I've been on the Leptin Rx since Monday, and I'm doing my utmost to follow it as best I can. I haven't "cheated" on my diet, I've eaten as much fat as I can...healthy fats of course, and have not snacked once between meals. I realize it's a long process, but I am determined to change my life, and change my health.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-09T09:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66921>

You really don't need the techno babble. Some of us need it because we need to unerstand why. If you don't care about why here is some simple: There are three macronutrients -Fat, protein and carbs. If humans have access to animal fat and protein they do not need carbs. so assuming you have eliminated carbs that leaves protein and fat. You should eat more fat than protein. That's the basic program. As you put this in practice it gets easier with each passing day. I have been doing this for 18 mo. with great results, but I am still changing and adapting. It takes time, it gets better every day.

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-09T10:22:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66927>

Re:Â  The "techno-babble".Â  Furface, I have to read every blog post like, 4 times.Â  First, I skim it, then I go back and read thoroughly, then I wait for some comments to come in, because they help me further understand.Â  Then about a month or so later, I read again.Â  Hey, I have a business degree from a party school; I'm not a scientist.Â  That frog we dissected in HS Biology was as far as I got.Â  Here's something I do regularly though, that I think could help: Go to the archives and pick a blog.Â  Any blog, doesn't matter the subject.Â  Read that blog completely, thoroughly, until your brain literally hurts.Â  Google words you don't understand.Â  Really spend time on the comments.Â  Print it out if you have to.Â  Do this about once a week, and they will start falling together like puzzle pieces.Â Â What starts happening is you start saying, oh....he said that same thing, in a different way, in that blog, months ago.Â  And here's another puzzle piece...wow.Â  And it starts making sense in a big way.Â  Of course, that could be also because I've dramatically upped my seafood content (and thereby my DHA, which I now understand! LOL) which makes my brain work better.

Also, regarding the fats, are you eating seafood every day, or almost daily?Â  Remember, seafood (esp. shellfish) contains a ton of DHA, which is a fat.Â  So it's not like you have to "add" fats.Â  In fact, Dr. K. recommends not adding sat. fat to your seafood meals, as it doesn't allow as much of the DHA uptake.Â

### furface — 2012-12-09T12:40:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66937>

Very helpful information, Pam. Both about the blogs, and the seafood and fat.

I might start to reread the blogs, and as far as seafood goes, right now all I have in my fridge/freezer/cupboards are about 3 pieces of wild salmon, 3 pieces of tilapia, 30 prawns, and 8 cans of tuna. I don't get paid for another 11 days when I plan on jamming my fridge, and freezer with all types of seafood.

Â 

Still have to acquire a taste for oysters...tried a can of smoked oysters last week, and to be totally honest, they tasted like dirt, very gritty. But I finished them. But I'll try some fresh ones, along with some fresh clams (Afraid to try canned ones after my canned oysters experience.), some more wild salmon, tilapia, basa, shrimp/prawns, maybe even some dungeness crab and lobster if I can afford it. Also some grass-fed beef, and grass-fed lamb. Oh and of course bacon and free run pastured eggs.

Â 

That's good to know that you don't need extra added fat with seafood because I think that's where I have my biggest adjustment...consuming the extra fat on it's own.

Will also be adding some avocado, continue with the coconut oil, will try and find some coconut manna, possibly some heavy whipping cream from Jersey cows (so it's A2), as well as pasture butter, and hopefully things will be in full swing.

### Kiera2 — 2012-12-09T16:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66946>

Hi Furface, be careful with the dairy. Just because it's a Jersey, doesn't mean it's A2.

### furface — 2012-12-09T19:52:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx-is-it-mandatory-to-have-lots-of-fat-every-meal.5075/post-66960>

> **Coriander said**
> Hi Furface, be careful with the dairy. Just because it's a Jersey, doesn't mean it's A2.

Oh I will. I'm not going to go way overboard, but I'm also not going to be so overly paranoid and worry whether or not it is A1 or A2 over 1/2 a cup of cream once or twice a week...if that. At times I think we can OVER think things at times, and become TOO cautious, and over worry when it's not really necessary. At times we must let common sense prevail. JMHO.

# Thread: 50-55 degree temp basis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/50-55-degree-temp-basis.5076/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-12-07T02:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/50-55-degree-temp-basis.5076/post-66641>

Is this 50-55 degree temperature basis partly because this is part of adapting?Â  As you adapt to the cold you can stay in this range longer?Â  I was in the pool for 35 minutes and my temperature would not go below 55 degrees.Â  I was thinking about waiting till it went to 50...but it wasn't going down so I went ahead and stopped at 35 minutes.Â  A minute after getting out of the pool and the temp was already up to 58.

Â 

The temp of the water was 53.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-07T09:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/50-55-degree-temp-basis.5076/post-66660>

No.....its due to how the cold receptors in mammals are set and wired into their central and peripheral nervous system. CT 4-6 has that info.

# Thread: Optimal latitude?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-latitude.5089/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2012-12-07T19:04:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-latitude.5089/post-66738>

I've been thinking sinceÂ Jack's last blog how I had a near remission from my illness about 18 years ago when I visited the Greek Islands. I mentioned it a few months later to a neurologist in London who wrote it off as "well, you were in holiday mode and everyone feels better on holiday".
I didn't push the point....you expect specialists to know better than you right? /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_biggrin.gif But....IÂ did wonder about the fact I was on holiday in northern Italy just prior to Greece, and elsewhere in Europe afterwards and I don't think IÂ had the same improvement anywhere but this one place. **The most notable improvement was in cognitive function.**Â  (Happy to expand on that by PMÂ if anyone is interested.)

The latitude was 36.52 N.

...It was in line with where I can trace my maternal gentic heritage back to....though I'm not sure if/howÂ that bears relevance.

Curiouser and curiouser, in the 28 years since I became unwell I've had just one other time when I had a sharpÂ improvement in cognitive function and at that time I was (on holiday again) staying at 36.2 S.

So, is there anyone here willing to speculate? Magnetic fields at play? The same North and South of the equator? DoÂ humans have an optimal latitude? Is this a critical_Â_ factor for some? Are weÂ going to be hit with an even bigger inconvenient truth bomb?

Can NASA come up with some latitude simulating device?Â For Christmas would be excellent! Thanks in advance /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_biggrin.gif

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-07T19:18:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-latitude.5089/post-66746>

I use a GPS device on my cell phone......to always check where i am. Had labs today drawn at my coordinates.......then did my talk.

### freesia — 2012-12-07T19:35:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-latitude.5089/post-66749>

Drawing labs at your coordinates!

I'm just about chewing a piece out of my laptop here wanting to know what biomarkers you're monitoring. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: I'm learning...by diane
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 390
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2012-12-08T12:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-66812>

So after almost a year of reading the blog and forum, I decided to start a journal. Not sure if I will post every day as I have a habit of thinking something is a good idea and then not following through LOL. But what I really wanted was a place to put down some of the things I'm learning and finding, and it doesn't always fit well in the other threads.

Â 

So here we go.

Â 

I've been on a "quest" to feel better over the last couple of years, and while I feel there's been pretty constant forward momentum, it's still a journey. I started out because of weight gain (especially after 40 and around my middle), brain fog, fatigue (where I almost needed to sleep under my desk every day), joint pain, heartburn, lack of energy, grumpiness, terrible cravings and night eating (and I guess what's called bingeing - but not sure) and not feeling like I was living to my potential. Add to that constant congestion and ringing ears which drives me crazy.

Â 

I found pretty early on that I have gluten, sugar and dairy issues. Then found I have many other food issues, none of which was really a surprise to me. About 3 years ago I found paleo, which was pretty much how I found that I needed to eat before I knew what it was called. Except I didn't add as much fat. But I would be able to eat what I knew I should for about 3 months at a time, then fell off pretty much completely. Would feel horrible again, get fed up with feeling that way and back on. I'm tired of that pattern too.

Â 

So far, I think the biggest change I've made has to do with meal timing and light. I notice huge changes if I don't have breakfast - like yesterday. I had to get blood tests in the morning, so had to fast. But then was off to meetings, and didn't bring anything with me. Well, last night I ate most of the night, for the first time in a couple of months. Plus, I feel so much better when I finish eating by 6:30 or so. It's hard for me to eat much earlier with traffic and getting home, and then taking the dogs out. Plus I have a full time job and a business on the side, which can get pretty busy.Â 

Changing to epi-paleo has been a great change as well. I've noticed differences in my skin and my mood. The recently I added more fat, which really has helped with the cravings.Â 

Â 

The weight hasn't started to go down yet - but while sometimes it's the hardest to face (because it's visual), I try not to make it the most important thing. Because I know that there needs to be health in order for the body to respond. However, over the last few days, I've noticed my waist and hips going down a little bit. There's a little more room in my pants - so something is happening.Â 

Â 

I did a consult with Dr. K a couple of months ago and it was amazing. Crazy as it sounds, it's taken me a couple of months to find an anti-aging doctor, schedule an appointment (that took almost 2 months because she's so booked, and then I had to reschedule once. Boo), get to my doctor for blood tests (which was another scheduling nightmare). And then wait for day 20 of my cycle (day 21 is a saturday and labs aren't open) for tests. That was yesterday. So hopefully will have a better idea of what's going on in the next couple of weeks. My last labs were about a year and a half ago. They were pretty thorough thanks to my naturopath who suggested them, and my doctor who went along with them. Although 15 vials of blood later, I almost passed out LOL. Yesterday I needed 8 so they made me lie down.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â 

I love Dr. K's blogs, and as I work my way through them, my understanding increases. That's what I want for myself - to be able to get it.Â 

Â I think what I truly have understood is how important it is to try things for yourself and then see what works. Plus have patience because not everything happens right away. Which can be frustrating, especially with the weight part. But life is definitely a journey. I think it helps because I teach people the same thing, just about dogs, not health. Quick fixes usually aren't, and if something sounds too good to be true, it probably is. What I actually teach is how to gain knowledge - that's more powerful than a quick fix.

So hopefully I can post here with some regularity! LOL.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T16:00:06+0200

I loved reading your post Diane. Â I can relate to so much of it - probably most. Â The biggest change for me too - is figuring it out for myself - but then - Dr. Kruse has given us the tools. Â For the last 8 or 10 years I have been seeking help from Doctors for a lot of stuff you talk about. Â They always ran the run of the mill tests and then said I was okay. Â I look back now and realize my questions were the right ones and I pretty much had nailed the problems - so my intuition was correct - but nobody took me seriously.

Light for me is huge too and adding more fat and raw are the big ones. Â 

If you are anything like me - I have probably every diet book ever written. You get so hopeful that this will be the one and it never was. Â Now I can figure it out for myself - so empowering! Â 

Good luck to all of us! Â We deserve to be all that we can be ......and we need to require that of ourselves. Â It takes a lot of work and thought and purpose - but we are so worth it. Â All of this has been so liberating hasn't it? Â  And Also - the most important thing -- I am having the time of my life......WHO KNEW???? Â  Â ......... Â  actually Dr. K did!

### diane — 2012-12-08T17:28:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-66850>

Thanks! Absolutely Caroline - we definitely deserve to be all that we can be. On one hand, it's too bad that it took me this long to realize that, but on the other, I'm so glad I did it now! I've also been working with a therapist, which has really helped. If nothing more than to help me realize that there is a different perspective out there. And the biggest change has been the change with my self talk - which has been huge!

Â 

Good luck with getting the prescription endless! Fingers crossed for you!

Â 

When I tested my cortisol and melatonin levels in April, they were abysmal. It's like I could barely get out of bed, and according to the chart, I had "phase 2" adrenal fatigue. I felt a bit of despair - like how the hell am I going to fix this. When I spoke to Dr. K, he said I wasn't getting enough quality sleep. So I started wearing the blue blockers, turning the lights out and going to bed earlier. A sleep mask helped so much because in the city, even with light blocking blinds, light still does get in. And in a couple of weeks, I wasn't trying to crawl under the desk for a 2pm nap. So wonder if it was adrenal fatigue at all - or just lack of good sleep. Now I can pretty much tell when my sleep is off. But, I've also noticed that if I do eat a bit late or get too much light in an evening, I don't feel nearly as bad as I did. Now that's progress!Â

### caroline — 2012-12-08T17:47:54+0200

I seem to be on a similar path Diane. Following the light cycles has been so huge in feeling better and sleeping better. I have just had more tests done and awaiting results - can't wait! My am and pm cortisol was very low and progesterin was/is horribly low. Are you on cytomel? trying to track that down here. There is a lot of similarities between Canada and Australia as far as health care goes. Within 3 days of my consult with Dr. K. - I had a great new Doc and scripts in hand and he also listened to my consult. I was incredibly lucky. I think that really empowered me.

### diane — 2012-12-08T18:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-66862>

I'm not on anything right now. I did ask my doctor about Metformin, and she is on board. Just wants to make sure that my kidney's are ok, then will write the prescription. She says she uses it for blood glucose but also for irregular periods. Just so happens that mine have gone wonky since I did the light stuff - really long, heavier, and either shorter cycle or longer cycle. Happy coincidence I think!

Â 

Yes, I think Australia and Canada are very similar. There are many supplements we can't get here as well, like DHEA. We seem more progressive on one side, yet more conservative in many more ways. It's funny - when I got my blood tests, I had to pay to get a Vitamin D panel and IGF-1. Sounds funny that someone has deemed that you have to pay for these yourself! Vitamin D would seem to be one that could be important - especially in Canada!

I had forgotten about that gorgeous mall in Buffalo! We used to often fly out of Buffalo - so would go early and shop! I have the most gorgeous pair of Prada shoes that I paid $40.00 for. You would not believe the prices in Australia - they are mind blowing. A brand of jeans that I like used to be $350.00 -$400.00 here. ### diane — 2012-12-09T17:53:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-66952>

Â A little while back, someone asked me where I'm happiest. At first, I wasn't sure. Then I realized - I'm happiest when I'm outside. I know that sounds a bit broad, but I really am happiest when I'm out in the fresh air. I think that's why I love spending time with my dogs so much - they keep me in touch with nature. It's so important to me that they are treated like dogs and not people. Someone asked me if they were my children. I answered no, they're my dogs. That's healthy was the response. No - that should be normal! LOL

Â 

One of my favourite activities is hiking with the dogs, and we do it regularly. They love it too of course. We just went on a great one today, and I brought along another dog I was looking after. Now they're passed out snoring. That's the sign of a great time! My dogs are amazing off leash - I can walk them anywhere off leash, either in the woods or downtown. So I work hard to make sure that there needs are met and they get lots of interaction with nature.Â 

Â 

Which has made me realize that I'm likely going to have to move soon. All of the hiking trails are 45 minutes to an hour away, and I would love to be closer. Plus, think that I need to get away from the city - it's time. Which would also give the dogs a back yard. Not that I think they need it, because believe me we get out and about every day for an hour ++. But it would just add some additional space to chill and play.

Â 

I've gone to bed pretty late the last couple of nights - actually after midnight. Quite a while since I've done that. And the surprising thing is that I haven't been trashed the next day. I think the D-Rhibose is helping with that to be honest.

Â 

One thing I've noticed is how long my nails have been getting. Holy cow - they grow like weeds! And I'm not sure they've ever been stronger. It's crazy.

Â 

Now, it may just be time for a nap. LOL.

### diane — 2012-12-10T22:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67083>

Oh poo. I had a post all typed out and lost it. So trying again.

Â 

I guess it comes down to not what you're giving up, but what you're gaining. And what the thing you're giving up actually makes you feel worse - doesn't feel so bad.

Â 

I love coffee. About 3 years ago I figured out that it really impacts my energy through the day. I've been off for months at a time, but will then go for a weekend drinking it. Since I got my sleep in order, I have realized that coffee impacts my sleep more than I thought. So it really isn't good for me, but I do love it so. I've been doing bulletproof green tea instead. There's wicked flavoured green tea just down the street from me, so I love trying new flavours. Then put butter and coconut oil in my Vitamix. Yum. There's also been a big difference doing this in how hungry I am later in the day. Yes, fat was definitely helpful. I love finding these things that work - every piece is one step further. Wait, thought that would turn out to be very wise, but just didn't come off right LOL.

Â 

A co-worker has recently lost quite a bit of weight on Weight Watchers. It pains me so hearing her lament about fat, while eating brownies (cause they're only 2 points) and complaining how hungry she is all the time. And how she hates her diet. I lost 25 lbs on WW a few years ago, and was pretty tiny (wore a size 4 pants). But that came back. And more. And I know how it's wreaking havoc for later. But it's still hard to say anything that I'm doing, especially the shape I'm in now. I want to be a success story! Oh crap - I am already a success story. If I compare how I feel now vs. over a year ago, there's a huge difference. There were days I could barely make it through I was so tired. I had a hard time getting out of bed. And while I'm still not back to my "old self", hell, I have a lot more energy and haven't felt drained during the day for quite a while. So that's a major WIN!Â 

Â 

One thing I would still like to fix is the fact that I'm out of breath when I go up stairs. It's not that I'm out of shape, but my throat is closed and it's hard to breathe. Over a year ago, I went to a holistic allergist and found there were a number of foods I was allergic to. When I eliminated them, it was amazing the cardio capacity I had. Almost overnight. But now it's back, and I would like to figure out how to resolve it. On the other side, my eyes have pretty much stopped watering which was an issue. They would run down my face as I drove to work and actually impaired my vision many days. So some things resolved, some things yet to fix. Guess I'm never satisfied, eh. Always want more! LOL. Guess that's why I'm on a journey! Â

Â 

Funny - I used to think that I could drink coffee any time of day and it didn't affect me. But I didn't realize how poor my sleep was! Now I know better! LOL.

### diane — 2012-12-11T23:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67175>

Of course, no sooner did I write that I haven't been tired, and I was in nap mode this afternoon LOL. I think it may have been from the crappy coffee I had Sunday after our hike. See, told ya that coffee affects me.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â 

I was also having a day where everything annoyed me, and realized I hadn't had one of those in a while. So rather than focusing on how annoyed I felt, I was pretty impressed by how different I've been. Amazing.

Â 

Speaking of hikes, that is one of my absolute favourite activities (which I'm sure I've mentioned). I try to get out once a week. We have one scheduled this weekend and another next weekend. So exciting! Tomorrow night I have an event with a bunch of people where we take the dogs and go to see lights in various areas of the city. Then we all hop on the subway and go to another location. It's so much fun - I can't wait!

Â 

In case you haven't seen my various other posts, I have 2 dogs plus a significant other. Although he has recently taken a job a couple of hours away so only comes home weekends, which has been a pretty big adjustment. So for most of the time, it's me and the dogs. And I spend a good part of my life thinking and studying dogs, which is super fascinating. And rewarding. Dogs are such amazing teachers, but so many people don't put their ego aside to see it. Or they see them as human and can't see past that. So many people focus on what their dog gives them, but I focus my life on making their lives better because I want to give them everything they deserve. I've had people ask me how I had patience to work through training my dogs. Well, I had a vision of what I wanted for my dogs, and I never lost sight of that vision. I know it would take time, effort and patience - and I was committed to it. Heck, it's not done yet - there's so much we have to do together! I push myself out of my comfort zone to try new things, and I push them out of theirs too. It's created tremendous trust between us, and helps them deal with new things that come along. I so appreciate what they've brought to my life - because I've made it a way of life. I seek out like minded people, have met many new friends (both human and canine LOL). My life has completely changed because of them. They're just amazing.

People tell me I light up when I talk about them. Well, they are worth lighting up for!

### diane — 2012-12-13T01:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67310>

Interesting day. Somehow have eaten more carbs yesterday and today. I've noticed how bloated i get with carbs. Crazy. It's not all weight - a lot of it is bloating. But at least I was still gluten free.

Â 

Traffic getting home tonight was brutal. The whole downtown core was jammed. Of course I had an event to get to - so I was pretty stressed getting home. I almost didn't make it - and it was an event I organized! Once I got there the stress went away and I had an amazing time. We had 25 dogs do an offleash stay in front of a Christmas tree at the busiest intersection in Toronto. It was amazing! We had tons of people stopping to take pictures of them. It was a really cool sight for sure. And I'm really proud that I teach people how to do this. Many tell me when they start my class that it will never happen - and I prove them wrong. I had a client tonight tell me that her dog did "ok" when doing the stay. I asked her if she was kidding - as anyone if they would even attempt doing what she just did and they would think she's crazy. So her dog did fabulous. The icing was that we raised about $350 for a dog rescue as well - so all in all it was a fabulous night. My dogs loved it - they're snoring now!

Â 

Only issue is that I had a coffee while we were out, so now I'm awake and it's midnight. When will I learn! LOL.

### diane — 2012-12-15T00:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67565>

Thanks Caroline! I'm so proud. Tomorrow - another hike! So excited.

So this week, I have not gotten peanut butter M&M's once. That's pretty big for me. I haven't craved them once. I did have some 90% chocolate - but it wasn't a craving like I've been having. I find the key time for me to have enough fat is for breakfast. Which is sometimes hard because like some other people I've seen, there are many mornings that it's really hard to get much breakfast down. I'm almost queasy trying to finish, which is pretty odd for me.

I find that I haven't been planning my meals very well lately, which then causes me to tend to grab fast and easy things. Although last night I made bacon for dinner. Yes, just bacon (which I've never done before LOL). But I wasn't hungry one bit after dinner - which is great! Small steps.

### caroline — 2012-12-15T00:48:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67567>

Thanks Caroline! I'm so proud. Tomorrow - another hike! So excited.

So this week, I have not gotten peanut butter M&M's once. That's pretty big for me. I haven't craved them once. I did have some 90% chocolate - but it wasn't a craving like I've been having. I find the key time for me to have enough fat is for breakfast. Which is sometimes hard because like some other people I've seen, there are many mornings that it's really hard to get much breakfast down. I'm almost queasy trying to finish, which is pretty odd for me.

I find that I haven't been planning my meals very well lately, which then causes me to tend to grab fast and easy things. Although last night I made bacon for dinner. Yes, just bacon (which I've never done before LOL). But I wasn't hungry one bit after dinner - which is great! Small steps.

I was finding the same thing Diane - I struggled to get that much protein down - even tho I needed it. The best thing I ever did was to have more fat instead. So I have two BPC with loads of butter and coconut oil.....and wait for it.....Cardamom!! It made a huge difference. I just add in some left overs - or now--some raw meat and I am good to go. Not much in the way of cooking and cleaning up either!
As Inger says ..... Perfect for us lazy girls!
I was going to ask you before ...Do you have an Nespresso store in Toronto? you must have I would think. You can go and sample their different coffees to see which ones you like and if they affect you. Just say you are considering buying a machine and you can sample to your hearts content!.

### caroline — 2012-12-15T00:50:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67568>

### caroline — 2012-12-15T16:04:26+0200

Dr. K did say that getting more fat in is easier Â and better if you are struggling with all the protein.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-15T21:43:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67615>

Great journal Diane! It's amazing how so many of us share a lot of the same issues.Â  Thankfully we've all found Jack Kruse!!

Your dogs sound wonderful. I'm an animal lover!

### diane — 2012-12-17T19:00:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67753>

Soooooooo.....

Â 

Today's my birthday! I'm so lucky in that I share it with my significant other. This is our 9th bday together - and it keeps getting more and more fun. We went to New York City the first 3 years we were together. And this year, he was home for the weekend, and I think it was more special because I was so happy to see him. The dogs were too - my female really loves him and is so happy when he's home. My male is a real momma's boy LOL. I had a fabulous day today. And tonight, I'm going to a shopping event to my favourite store. And found both pairs of shoes that I wanted buy one get one 50% off -- things went my way today! LOL.

Â 

We went out for lunch. I'm pretty good at avoiding wheat, etc. But one thing that I am noticing is that I feel full way faster than I used to. Of course, we didn't have breakfast (not good, I know) and i was starting to get a headache. But felt full after I had one piece of the appetizer. I'm taking that as a good sign!

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-17T20:45:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67760>

Happy Birthday Diane!!! Sounds like you had a wonderful day....all the best, SeaHorse

### diane — 2012-12-17T22:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67772>

Thanks so much Seahorse!

### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T08:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67797>

Happy belated birthday!Â  Sorry I missed it yesterday, had to work last night.Â  Glad you had a great day!Â  Birthdays are special and should be celebrated.

### caroline — 2012-12-18T16:35:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67849>

Happy belated birthday Diane! It sounds like you had the perfect weekend with your significant other. Doesn't get any better does it. Altho - New York City is one of my favorite places - I love the energy there.

### diane — 2012-12-18T18:54:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67875>

Thank you both! It was fabulous.

Â 

And although I'm not yet to the place you are Caroline with feeling the joy all the time (but have had flashes of it in the past, so I know what you're talking about), I also know that in the past, I would have thought that the day wasn't perfect so it was horrible. I didn't get this, or didn't get that, or didn't do this - and would feel ripped off. This year - I just felt so appreciative. And that the day was just fabulous. I appreciated what DID happen, not what didn't. That is a pretty big change for me, especially given I was surrounded by negativity when I grew up. Although I realized that, I didn't realize how much and just how it affected me. And once that sunk in, it was so much easier to change!

Â 

Sunday my significant other decided to clean the house. We threw out bags for the garbage and recycling. I realized that I've been here, feeling horrible that the house was in the state it was, but not knowing what to do with it. Even when I had to go through the counter, I felt overwhelmed. (Insert fear of being a hoarder here LOL). It was a bit of a wake up call, and I was a little surprised feeling that resistance. Which I pushed through, and felt better.

Â 

I know that for me, when I feel great, all I want to do is clean LOL. And I want to present myself better - hair, makeup, clothes, etc. I know that I'm not there yet - waiting for that to happen! I have found that some of these cues - the behaviours that I exhibit - are a much better indication of how healthy I am, even before I look better!

### caroline — 2012-12-19T00:46:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67914>

You will get there Diane - absolutely no question about that!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-19T08:26:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67930>

Daring people do. Daring is a mindset and an attitude of action. Daring is the weapon used to defeat FEAR. Daring people go that bit further, they do the unexpected, they achieve the impossible and they live the improbable. If you want to walk on water, you have to get out of the boat. Â Getting out of the boat brings change, sometimes crisis accompanies change, but the rewards overshadow it. Rewards in life are in direct proportion to the level of service we provide and the difficulty in providing that service. Today begin to jump out of your situation, and grow your wings on the way down......Become daring.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-19T10:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-67948>

Diane as I read your journal I see reflections of myself. Your last post about your birthday and not being perfect and your past, etc. Yupâ€¦could have been in my journal! Oh how I used to focus on the negative while knowing that I shouldnâ€™t. For years I focused on changing my thoughts & internal dialogue but I never seemed to get anywhere. A few months ago I started having major glimpses of positive thoughts and general happiness. It lasted for quite some time so I know what it feels like and I look forward to more of that too!!!
I could also relate to the cleaning when youâ€™re feeling good. One noticeable change for me that basically hasnâ€™t gone away is that I am more energetic now. My mood used to be ruined if I had things to do after work. I just wanted to have supper and relax but I used to feel like the â€œto doâ€ list never ended. Now, I donâ€™t mind having things to do, it doesnâ€™t stress me out like it used to. I no longer mind having to do things, donâ€™t get me wrong I still love to relax. Itâ€™s just that now I am able to enjoy the hour of relaxation time instead of being pissed off about the two hours I was doing chores! Iâ€™m rambling but just wanted to share my similar thoughts!

### endless — 2012-12-19T22:49:15+0200

### diane — 2012-12-19T22:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68030>

Thanks for that Dr. K. Funny thing is that I do consider myself to be daring on one hand. But on the other hand, I know that I hold myself back sometimes. That's what I'm working on. Maybe I'm just daring to the edge of my comfort zone LOL.

Â 

Hmmmmm....reading Inger's and Caroline's posts makes me think about going more raw. I do love sushi and sashimi - I have no problem with that. However, just trying to figure out how I stock my fridge for that lol. I've done steak tartare at home and really liked it, but I have huge roasts in the freezer - way too big to thaw for raw. It's all amazing grass fed beef from my friend's dads farm. I also want to try ceviche - haven't yet made it. But I will soon! I love the flavours of lime and cilantro, so I know I will like it.

Â 

Inger and Caroline - if you are reading this, you are both so amazing! Inger, thanks so much for what you've been sharing. And Caroline, it's been so amazing to see your transformation and to feel your support. You're both such inspirations!

Â 

So far, my favourite birthday gift is my Nike Fuelband. It's so cool! It tracks steps and distances (a high tech pedometer I guess), and somehow it's pushing me to walk even further with the dogs. Yesterday I took 10,000 steps, today 12,000. We walk for an hour minimum a day, so I want to track to see how much we can do! Do you think I'm a bit competitive?

### caroline — 2012-12-20T00:36:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68041>

Thanks for the kind words Diane. Once you start eating more raw it is so easy. You can just incorporate by having some raw and some cooked. So cook a big pot roast and have some for breakfast and have a little bit of raw meat or fish as well.
The raw meat is the easy part. I just buy a bunch of packages of grass fed rump steak and then every so often open one and put it on a wooden board in the fridge and let sit for a couple of days. I probably like it best when it has been siting for 3 or 4 days. So I just keep opening packages every couple of days depending on how much I am using. I keep shrimp n the freezer and then I buy fresh fish once a week. It is much easier - honestly. no cooking - no cleaning up - heaven! I swear - I wouldn't lie to you!

### diane — 2012-12-20T21:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68088>

Caroline, that's amazing advice. Thank you!

I was inspired by your advice, so today I made salmon ceviche for lunch. Oh my, so delicious! Can't believe I waited this long to try it! Next I will try it with scallops or shrimp, but the salmon really was outstanding. I also took NY sirloin steak out of the freezer (told ya I have amazing grass fed beef in there!) and will do some sort of raw beef.Â 

I really did have great energy this afternoon after lunch. I also had bulletproof coffee this morning instead of my usual green tea. While I love coffee, I think I actually like the tea better. Hmmm...never thought that would happen.

Again realized today that my reaction to things have changed. A certain situation that I've been in before always leaves me in a panic mode, but this time, not at all. Honestly, there's a calm that I haven't had before. I've realized it over the past few days. So interesting, and such a relief as well!

And thanks to Darleen for posting the autocorrect link! Oh my god - I haven't laughed that hard in such a long time. Every one I read made me laugh harder. The dogs thought I was nuts. Or were trying to figure out the joke. LOL.

And yet another thing that's changed - my cycle has been completely thrown off. So weird. It's so much longer, and my TOM lasts almost double the time. For the past 3 months. This I'm not a fan of, but I'm assuming that this means that more change is coming.

I will know once I get my labs back. Actually they must be back because my doctor called because my zinc is really low. But I have to get a fax number in order for them to send them to me, so will have to work that out first. Everything else is fine, she said. Hmmm...I'll bet I can find more issue!

### diane — 2012-12-20T21:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68089>

And thanks to Darleen for posting the autocorrect link! Oh my god - I haven't laughed that hard in a long time. I was in tears, and my stomach aching. The dogs thought I was nuts. That just makes it all worth it LOL.

Â I usually make a big batch and then have some for breakfast the next day. Â Once you get into the swing it will be so much easier especially since you said you are on your own during the week.Â

### diane — 2012-12-20T22:07:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68095>

I've actually made steak tartare twice! But I definitely couldn't eat it every day - I think I would get tired of it. I'll have to come up with a few different flavours and mix it up.

But I will be having ceviche tomorrow for lunch again!Â

### diane — 2012-12-21T15:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68136>

Had a great breakfast today. Cooked a couple of slices of bacon, then a NY strip steak that I just cooked a bit on each side in the bacon great. It was mostly raw - but wasn't quite there for the raw for breakfast LOL. But it was delicious.

Now my salmon ceviche for lunch. Even better today than it was yesterday. I'm getting more salmon and cilantro when i go shopping! I love both. I used red onion today which tastes way better.

I was hungry last night, which must have been because I skimped on breakfast. So had a small can of tuna. Then, inspired by Darleen's coco-crack (I think that's what she called it LOL), I tried a little concoction of my own. Which wasn't bad. And hey - it's a whole lot better than my trek to the store across the street for peanut butter M&Ms. So a win for me.

So, let's talk about poop. I know it's kind of a crappy topic right before Christmas (oh, I'm so sorry but I could NOT resist). Having dogs, I"m always aware and in tune with what's going on with them because I'm forever picking it up (which is why I like feeding them raw as the poop is way smaller and perfectly formed LOL). I'm sure it's similar with children. But yet we don't pay a lot of attention to our own.

I got the Squatty Potty a couple of weeks back. I have noticed a difference. It's much easier to go, and I can tell it's much cleaner back there - meaning that more is emptying out I'm guessing (sorry, hope this isn't too graphic!). I see a lot of people who are constipated, but I have the other issue. A change in my diet, and I'm very runny. Maybe it's IBS, not too sure. When I eat raw, I get runny too. I'm definitely not a 4 on the bristol stool chart! The other thing I've had over the past 8 to 9 months is a burning in the butt (if it's TMI, please feel free to stop here and move along). It's not hemorrhoids - and it gets worse when I eat certain things, like dairy, or if I get glutened. It can be pretty painful, and I'm guessing that it has to do with digestive issues. But, I find that the Squatty Potty has been helping with that, although not sure why!

I'm thinking of getting one for my parents.

They both had colon cancer within 6 months of each other, and think that it may help!

### diane — 2012-12-21T16:57:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68143>

And Caroline - must be the raw meat, but I was out and about at the grocery store and liquor store (yes, it's actually called the liquor store, the beer store here LOL), and I couldn't believe how many people were smiling at me! Maybe it's the Christmas spirit, but I didn't notice it yesterday. And some guy winked at my dogs when we were out for our walk this morning. That was just creepy.

### diane — 2012-12-22T00:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68162>

Yes, they were both lucky. Early stages. Both of them had it in the same spot, surgery only, no chemo or radiation required. Interesting thing is that my dad is older and in lesser physical condition than my mom (after 3 strokes), but recovered much quicker. The key difference? His attitude.Â

### diane — 2012-12-22T13:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68184>

I find myself today either getting all teary or with bursting with joy. Teary not because I'm sad, but sometimes overwhelmed with feeling. Like telling my older dog that I can't remember life without her and then almost crying. Oh my - I sound CRAZY. LOL! Then within the next few minutes jumping around and dancing to Call me Maybe (I'm more of a hard rock girl, but there's usually one pop song that can get me going). Foo Fighters are on now, just to redeem myself.

I also burst into a spontaneous jog while out with the dogs this morning. That's happened before. But I have to be feeling pretty good for that to happen.

It's actually fun.Â 

Paulalynn - thanks for the tip about bacon wrapped scallops! Found some at Loblaws and they were fabulous! They were Nova Scotia scallops, but I doubt they were as good as yours lol.

I've been waking up at 4am the last few days. I'm wondering if it's because I switched my bulletproof tea for bulletproof coffee. Funny thing is I actually now enjoy the tea more! So back to tea today, see if that helps.

The other thing is that my legs have been aching during my walks - muscle aches. I've had these before, but not in a while. I'm out of magnesium, so haven't had any the last few nights. Wondering if this is what it is, so will be getting some magnesium today. I so enjoy my walks, I don't want to be in pain when I'm walking. Plus, with my new Nike Fuel band, I've upped my walking even more trying to get over 10,000 steps per day. So will see if that helps. that's my guess.

### diane — 2012-12-22T13:28:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68186>

I've also started dreaming more. Well, remembering my dreams more. A couple of nights ago I dreamt that I had this amazing powerhouse singing voice. That was a blast!

I stocked up on salmon yesterday, so ceviche for dinner tonight. And have a steak "aging" in the fridge. Â Oh I'm so excited!

### janagram — 2012-12-23T13:27:56+0200

well, blast out some holiday songs!

### diane — 2012-12-23T15:13:44+0200

### diane — 2012-12-23T23:01:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68272>

A great day today! We went on a huge hike and had about 45 dogs. It was crazy! My guys had so much fun, and i got to see some of my clients and their dogs that I hadn't seen for a while. And see how some of the puppies had grown up. I love when they recognize me and get so excited. But most of all, I love seeing them able to be off leash running in the woods, having the time of their lives. Because there are so few dogs that are allowed to do that, and I'm happy that I have a part in that.

Oh, and I love the snoring that I hear after we get home too!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Went to a pet store that sells raw food for dogs after, and got 2 huge elk bones for them. Probably the size of their heads LOL. That will be their Christmas present. Plus I got duck necks, bison bones, buffalo trip and some venison. I stopped in the vet yesterday to say Merry Christmas, and the vet tech asked if they had just been bathed, they were so soft. Nope, has been about 5 months since their last bath. That's what raw meat and fish with kelp will do! Their coats just shine!

### Inger — 2012-12-24T02:39:35+0200

### diane — 2012-12-24T07:25:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68293>

Thanks Inger! Yes I did salmon ceviche for breakfast and steak tartare for dinner yesterday!

Caroline - I think that we will be shiny both inside and out! At least judging from what I'm seeing from people here.

There's a Petsmart commercial with puppy tips that says that 80%of dogs over 3 have tooth decay. What???? Isn't that a huge clue that the standard diet is just wrong for dogs?? I really hate Petsmart anyway - they have no clue about anything lol.Â

### diane — 2012-12-24T17:53:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68325>

I didn't want to hijack Janagram's thread for her intention to lose 25 lbs (go janagram!), so wanted to continue here.

From Caroline:

"that is something I wish Dr. Kruse would talk more about Â - It seems that he will after his latest bio -hacks. Â When I did HCG I felt like my body was working correctly â€¦.I know that sounds vague. Â I have said this before here Â - I think I did HCG years ago in Canada. Â I didn't understand what it was at the time. Â There are clinics Â - Dr. Bernstein Â - and you get injections every couple of days. Â The only thing they would say was it was B vitamins but everyone used to joke that it was mare's urine Â - you can vaguely see the connection. Â Anyway â€“ I felt amazing and my body leaned right out. I remember that feeling so well â€“ to actually feel so good about yourself and be the person you want to be and that gives you amazing confidence to go out and slay the dragons! Â I know health comes first Â - Â but my bad body image and in fact â€“ my fat body â€“ Â stole my life for quite awhile. Â After reading Dr. K's Christmas R/X Â  â€“ I understand a lot of things about me. Â I was continually ridiculed etc. etc. etc. by my Mother all of my growing up years Â and that does untold and subtle damage to one's self worth. Â The only way for me to fix that was to become the person I knew I was in spite of all the negativity. Â Oddly â€“ I never took all of the horrible stuff personally Â - strange isn't it? Â I continually worked on me and â€¦â€¦TA DA Â â€¦here I am. Â I have been lucky enough to have lots of amazing people in my life especially a beautiful, kind and loving and generous husband â€“ who loved me no matter what and he gave me unlimited self confidence and always told me to go for it Â - no matter what crazy ideas I had. Â That is the most empowering of all â€“ when someone believes in you no matter what and failures just become opportunities to try something else. Â I really believe all the failures and all the bad stuff Â as well as the great stuff of course are part and parcel of the amazing people we have become. Â I guess we can choose to fold up like a deck of cards â€¦but failure never was and never should be an option."

Caroline - what you wrote is so wonderfully powerful. My mother was very similar - although I don't think she realizes she was criticizing, but was incredibly negative about everything. I thought that I was positive (haha) but I did take things personally, and have realized just how much it has affected me all these years. So good for you for not taking it personally. I thought all of her problems were my fault - and it was my fault that I couldn't fix them. Well, I've realized that it wasn't. And that I don't have to think of it that way anymore. It's really been the most liberating feeling. And I think that I'm most proud of accomplishing that this year. I am pretty impressed with how far I've come!

I love reading Dr. K's blog, and do like the inspirational content. But I've read a lot of inspirational content in the past, and haven't realized that what I was doing wasn't following the advice. For example, self talk. I've read over and over how to change your self talk. But I didn't think there was anything wrong with mine - I actually thought I was pushing myself harder. Until someone pointed it out to me, and then I changed it pretty quickly. I think that's what I like about this forum - people who can see you objectively and to point out things you may not realize.Â 

I try to embrace failure - so I can learn from it. A company that I used to work at had a value called "Freedom from failure" - meaning you couldn't be criticized for failing because if you were afraid of failing, you would never try. And sometimes failing meant that you would have bigger success. I like that.

And sometimes failing meant that you would have bigger success. I like that.

I like that Diane! What you write above, to embrace failure. I like that so much. I think it gives good vibes to the ones around us when we have that mindset. Also be open to others advice and "critisism" because there could always be some thruth in it!

I love when Caroline tells about her hubby.

It is always so loving and makes me feel so warm in my heart.

### diane — 2012-12-25T15:00:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68392>

I agree Inger! Caroline has a great way of communicating!

Merry Christmas all! We opened our gifts last night, so had the dogs open theirs today. Ok, I know. Corny. But so fun! I wrapped a couple of reindeer plush toys. Which is purely for my own enjoyment because it's so funny. My kettle broke a couple of days ago, so I bought this kick butt kettle that has different temperature settings for green tea, white tea, coffee, French press, etc. It's brilliant! I wrapped it up and put it from the dogs. Yes, I have a weird sense of humour LOL. But it's mine!

Â 

My partner loved his gifts. He gave me luggage last weekend, and a Nike Fuel band for my birthday. Which I love, love, love.

The best present this year has been what I gave myself. In furthering my steps along my journey and really becoming more aware of my thinking and how to change it. If I look back a year ago, my attitude has changed so much! Even with my partner home, things that would bug me before don't bug me know (and I'm talking about stupid little things). That's what Dr. K is talking about changing your brain I think. It's so much easier not having all that anger and frustration! Although he has been sneaky and turning the heat on. Little bugger! LOL.

Off soon to the in-laws for a little get together! Hope everyone has a wonderful day!

### diane — 2012-12-27T19:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68535>

I guess it's the time of year, but I'm on board with janagram's intention to lose 25 lbs. I can honestly say that I've been putting in a half assed effort lately. Ok, maybe 3/4 assed - a bit more than half.Â 

Â 

I really do feel better eating more protein and fat, folliwng the light - and now I've been eating more raw. But somehow, a couple of times a week, bad habits and old behaviours sneak in. Maybe it's the "weekend" attitude. And it's not that I think that I have to be perfect, but damn, it takes so long to get back to feeling great again. That's what's frustrating. So the end result is, I've really seen no results. Ok, that's not quite true. I've seen results in how I feel, in my attitude, in my thinking. I haven't seen results in my weight.Â 

Ok, what have I learned, since that's my theme. That when I eat sugar, dairy or wheat (and the wheat is really by accident most times), my stomach is bloated. I actually don't think I have as much fat to lose as I think - but there's a lot of bloating.Â 

I'm now figuring out my plan. Yes - there will be execution! LOL.

I was researching HCG the past few days. Apparently you can't get the real stuff in Canada - so I've ordered the homeopathic, and will hopefully get it sometime in the next month (damn customs). Then I"m considering tracking - while I hate the advice of the HCG forums to weigh yourself every day, you probably need some marker of change and/or success.

I don't see this as a quick way to lose, or a short term - but more of a kick start. Please feel free to refute or give me other thoughts!

### diane — 2012-12-27T20:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68547>

Hi jib! There's no such thing as hijack. This is a place for discussion!

I'm with you on the needle thing. I don't really have a big fear of needles if someone else is doing it - but there's not way I'm doing it to myself LOL. I'm not sure where you live, but shipping is a big consideration. I googled where to buy HCG in Canada - and found the HCG forum which actually has a lot of info on various brands. You can google "hcg forum" and find it. I ended up getting a patch from a company that will ship to Canada. There were a number of people who had tried it - although there were people who had tried almost everything lol. It's a homeopathic HCG - but I will give it a try.

What I want to figure out is how to do epi-paleo on HCG. It looks like you can't do a lot of fat - and many people look like they do a lot of chicken. which I haven't done much of lately. But I can do lots of fish!

And I have a really hard time with fruit, which there is a lot of, so not sure how to tackle that part yet!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T04:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68561>

there is a company called Hormony that ship hCG as drops. I do not know if homeopathic works. Some say it does, some say it doesn't. There is absolutely NO FAT what so ever on hCG. I do my own version re diet, because I do not want the fruit either. I do not like chicken also, so I do low fat fish, beef and I do a lot of it raw. I make carpacchio, mince my steak and eat as tartare etc.

I just read the protocol yesterday again (you can download from the internet, just google pounds and inches) and you can also do a protein shake with one egg and some egg whites added. There definitely is not a lot of variety, but when the hCG works you do not want to eat most of the time anyway. And I think it is a good idea that you do it only 6 weeks anyway, as then the effect wears off. And I am not craving carbs at all, but I am dreaming of a greasy omlette with bacon. And it is winter and you need good fats. How good your own fat is I am not sure. But you definitely release a lot of it every day which you can see by the inches you lose.

just forget the fruit, I don't eat it, except for some berries, nor the bread stick. So I am epi-paleo, except for the fat and the fat fish.

### diane — 2012-12-30T19:01:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68779>

Hmmm...well, high meat it is!

Definitely more half-assery yesterday. I think I've mentioned that my significant other took a job a couple of hours away few months ago, so it's been almost like living on my own. He's been home for the holidays, and being a very thin person who can eat whatever and not gain an ounce, well his eating habits are pretty horrific to say the least. Doesn't mean he's healthy of course, but most people look at the body and that tells them if they're healthy or not. So a trip to McDonalds and pizza for dinner. At 10. And then I stayed up until after 1, which I rarely do.

I'm not even going to say that I'm disgusted with myself, because I'm not. I'm out of that "judgy" phase - it doesn't do me any good. I'm not beating myself up. There are facts, and there are reasons that I had a day like I did - so I am learning from it and developing a strategy.

I do hope he gets a job here soon because I really miss him. But. It's much more difficult to keep healthy habits when one person doesn't. (He also has to turn on every light in the house - which I really don't get LOL).

My upcoming challenge for myself is to work out a plan to keep these healthy habits even when he's here. I don't think it will be hard. I just need to make myself a plan.

I thought the webinar was very thought provoking - and I definitely learned some new things. Right now, I think I'm going to change my plan and hold off doing HCG. I'm going to follow the protocols in the webinar and track to see where I get and what happens. I think my first step is to become a fat burner.

I got a blood glucose monitor yesterday. I've tested a couple of times - not nearly as bad as I thought it would be lol. But now I have a baseline and can see where it will improve from here. The one I got also doubles as a blood ketone monitor, so will be checking that as well as soon as I find strips.

Went on a fun walk today with the dogs down by the lake. They loved it, playing in the snow. It's the first decent amount of snow we've had in 2 years! Maybe 3. So much fun!

### diane — 2012-12-31T17:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-68859>

Paulalynn - I searched for it and LCBO and looks like they carry it! So maybe you have it in Nova Scotia! I love how Darleen posted about booze in my journal. It's so fitting!

So is the end of 2012 and I have to say that it was an amazing year. What's the most amazing is that usually each year, I lament on New Year's Eve what a horrible year it was and how the next year will be better. The biggest testament to what I've done this year is that I DON'T feel that this year. Sure, lots of challenging things happened. No, I'm not a millionaire or nor did I lose 50 lbs. I spent a lot of the year miserable, feeling like I couldn't get out of bed and wanting to sleep at my desk every afternoon. My business had a relatively slow year. My partner of 8 years worked away from home for 9 months of the year and I spent most of it alone. So shouldn't I feel like this was a horrible year?

HELL NO!

This was an AMAZING year. It was amazing because out of all the things that I could have made changes in - it was my attitude and how I look at the world. I can't even be upset that I didn't try to make these changes earlier, because everything in its own time. I may not have been ready for it earlier, but for some reason, this year was the year.Â 

Plus, after my consult with Dr. Kruse in August and some of the changes I implemented, I no longer feel like I need to spend the day in bed (I didn't feel this every day, but at least once a week). I have more energy, I'm not as tired. I'm happier. How can you not love this!

Still lots more to come on the journey in 2013. But I'm not getting older, I'm getting better! And thanks to so many of you and what you have given me this past year! Whether you are aware or not, whether I commented or just read, you have all influenced me greatly.

Happy New Year!
