vitex all month....hmm. Would that be safe for a 65 yr old lady? (as in, how much estrogen do I have? ....stored?)

### diane — 2013-03-19T19:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81110>

I don't know janagram. But if 65 year olds are on BHRT and take progesterone - I'm not sure why it wouldn't be ok.

So far today I feel great. Not even hungry for lunch. The real tell of the tape will be end of the day to dinner time - that's when I get cravings the most.

In other news, for some strange reason I was up until midnight last night. WHAT?? That hasn't happened in a while. I'm working on a new blog set up, but wasn't really tired. I ate dinner way too late (8:30 - haven't done that often either, but dinner was really delicious with salmon, kale and sauteed mushrooms). And I had coffee in the afternoon (which I also never do) which probably did it. Felt tired this morning, but took the d-rhibose with my tea and felt better. If I wake up with a tired feeling, the d-rhibose seems to take it away. Amazing that it's different for everyone. Also started to take diatomaceous earth yesterday. 1/4 tsp each for me and the dogs lol. Will see what that does too.

### endless — 2013-03-20T18:04:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81194>

Diane, where did you get the DE? I have only found commercial grade stuff but I don't want to eat that!

### diane — 2013-03-21T03:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81265>

Krista I got it at the place I get the raw food for the dogs. LOL. I seem to pick up a lot there!!

I don't know if it's the Vitex or the Diamataceous earth - but again today I didn't really feel like eating much of the day. I upped the DE to 1/2 tsp. I didn't eat lunch until almost 3. Wasn't that hungry for dinner, but did eat although a bit late at after 7. Is it possible that this is helping kill the cravings?

My progesterone showed up today. Plus my new thyroid and DHEA. Start progesterone on Friday. I got the pills, so will see how that goes. I hope I'm on the road to reversing a lot of this! Ups and downs, right?

### caroline — 2013-03-21T05:58:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81271>

### diane — 2013-03-21T16:52:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81299>

So got the meds yesterday and started the thyroid today. It increased from 30 mg to 60 - still fairly small dose. But holy Batman I'm hot today! Like hot from the inside. Which is what happened when I took the supplement over a year ago but I was never this hot. I think it's a combination of the thyroid and Casteberry - just wait until I start progesterone Friday night!

I'm hoping this means that things are starting to move. Wouldn't that be exciting!

I guess even though I think I don't, I actually do look for "the answer" and what's going to finally work. When I intuitively know that many things have to work together and it takes time. Somehow my pea brain still thinks that something will click. My latest hope is the progesterone and thyroid. Maybe because those were the things that stood out on my latest labs.

Holy cats I'm hot today!!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-21T17:10:19+0200

### diane — 2013-03-23T18:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81548>

Good afternoon! What a beautiful day!!

I took the BHRT last night, 100 mg oral progesterone. First of all, I was so sleepy after I took it. But secondly - I feel amazing today! I know it's just the first day, so maybe it's not all from the progesterone. But I feel great! I didn't take the Vitex today, didn't want to risk overdoing it.

I'm having a really hard time with breakfast lately. Can get down a couple of slices of bacon. Maybe a couple bites of either eggs, this morning I had crab and sauerkraut (quite the combination lol). I don't know if it's because I'm taking the diamotaceous earth right before I eat or not.

Haven't felt as hot the last couple of days taking the thyroid meds. But just before 6am I start getting warm in bed, like my body is starting to wake up and get going. Could this be the adrenals turning on? Thyroid? It's not a hot flash, more like a furnace feeling.

In the winter, they put up a snow fence along the beach of Lake Ontario, and dogs are allowed offleash within the snow fence. So today we went for a walk down there. And I took off my socks and shoes and walked along the water for a bit. It was around freezing but it felt great! The water was a bit cold (lol) and a couple of people asked me how it was. At least they didn't tell me to my face I was crazy.  It was so exhilarating! I loved it! The dogs were splashing around in the water as well. I'm usually such a wimp when it comes to rocks and sand, but now I don't care. My feet are pretty sensitive and usually rocks really bother them - but today, I discovered a new way to tolerate the rocks - just make sure your feet are numb from cold. LOL. I'm so glad we went today. The dogs are super tired, and now my feet are craving getting back in the water! Too bad the fence is only up for another week.

### endless — 2013-03-23T20:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81555>

Your feet will toughen up if you keep walking barefoot....I almost never wore shoes as a kid and my feet were like leather. The rocks give you a good acupressure massage, too!

My breakfast has gotten smaller, I am having a BP chai/matcha every morning and I do fine with that and less food.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-23T20:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81562>

My "BAB" devolved into a HAB and now it's just a normal one and I'm quite content with it. I'm beginning to think too many of us (not pointing out anyone in particular) stuck to the reset for far too long. 6-8 weeks should have been IT and then back to normal. I noticed last night I was in the most amazing good mood! You know. The one I'd been waiting to appear, magically, for a year?!

And all of us waiting for the need for lunch to go away. Well fie on that. I ate 2 small meals and a snack 2 days running and I was NOT hungry. I think too many of us took the original instructions FAR to literally. It's tantamount to doing Atkins Induction for a year. NOT how it was meant to be.

So for now I'll watch my calories to make sure I'm not over- or under-doing it. And now that my knee is feeling better I'm ready to get busy lifting.

### janagram — 2013-03-23T23:47:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81579>

I like this new thinking, Darleen. Gotta get our N-1 on!

### Lava — 2013-03-23T23:59:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81581>

can you tell me what N-1 is...sorry LOL

### diane — 2013-03-24T00:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81582>

lavalocks - no problem. It took me a while to figure out too!

N=1 is your own experience. I guess it stands for a sample size of one - but what I've learned is that you can't just take what everyone is doing and apply it to yourself - you have to experiment to see what works for you. ie what your n=1 is. We are all individual and unique!  If someone can help to explain it better, please by all means jump in!

### caroline — 2013-03-24T00:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-81584>

You said it perfectly Diane! We are all so unique and what works for someone else - is just that ....their N=1
That is why I think it is so important for everyone to keep posting their experiences .... It gives us clues to what works for someone and maybe - down the track - we can apply bits and pieces for our own n=1 ...or it leads us in another direction that we hadn't thought about before

Our own n=1 is our own personal masterpiece....and a work in progress!

### caroline — 2012-12-08T23:40:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66909>

Danny - I have some more thoughts! Â I was just down at the beach swimming in the ocean and I was thinking about you. Â Are you close to a lake/ocean? Â Maybe you could swim with the fish and take them on by osmosis or something LOL.

Anyway - what I was thinking - lately I seem to be a lot more sensitive to stuff. Â I have already mentioned the cans. Â The other day I bought myself a very expensive bottle of imported Balsamic vinegar Â - thinking I would treat myself when I made beef carpaccio. Â I reacted really badly to it. Â It seems I am happiest now if I eat very simply - a little raw meat and some salmon. Â I even am having problems with shrimp now. Â I think they toss them in something or do whatever and I am reacting. Â Honestly raw meat makes me feel better than anything. About allergies for seafood. I read somewhere that some people are allergic against certains stuff they add to almost all seafood today. It was phosphates I think? ### MartiD — 2012-12-10T11:23:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-67005>

My Mother was deadly allergic to scallops. She was fine with all other seafood but had to be careful in restaurants for cross contamination. She would breakout in hives, throat would swell, etc. I have avoided eating scallops until about 6 months ago but didn't have a reaction. They are one of my favorites now.

# Thread: Want to understand the power of temperature for human biology?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-understand-the-power-of-temperature-for-human-biology.5102/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-08T20:27:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-understand-the-power-of-temperature-for-human-biology.5102/post-66885>

Read this and think about what I have laid out in the CT series..........this is quite important to read before you listen to the December Webinar.

Â 

In an ingenious experiment, M . Mendelssohn (cited by Jenning 1906) observed that paramecia randomly dispersed themselves when ambient temperature was kept around 20 C. However, when a temperature gradient was established in their medium from 26 to 38 C or from 10 to 25 C, they congregated close to the area maintained between 24 and 28 degrees C. Moreover, when the temperature of their medium was elevated beyond their preferred temperature, these organisms became more active,Â performing a series of rotatory movements projecting them toward the cooler region . Once they reached a cooler , more comfortable environment , they resumed random locomotion . A similar , abeit slower , type of " avoiding locomotion " occurs when paramecia move from a cool to a hot environment.

Jenning (1906) performed a series of experiments in paramecia that led him to conclusions similar to those of Mendelssohn . Through the microscope , Jenning observed an activation of paramecia movements as the temperature of their medium was uniformly elevated between 40 and 45 degrees C. **When a drop of cold water was placed in their environment , **paramecia gathered in this region** , avoiding the heated area. These experiments clearly show that unicellular organisms without brains have the capacity to select a preferred temperature behaviorally, and therefore to protect themselves from environmental extremes. Â (Implies cold is primordial to all life forms)

Thus , environmental heat was a source of deleterious stimulation " but it was also an essential source of energy. Living organisms required heat for chemical reactions . Although some of the heat for these reactions came from metabolic activity , the main source of heat energy came fromÂ the environment. Therefore, _the control of environmental temperature had to be coupled to the production of internal heat._** It is therefore plausible to infer that unicellular organisms had some rudimentary capacity to control their internal temperature.

"Excerpted from the book called Hot Brain"

COUNTERINTUITIVE MOTIVATION POINT: Cold draws life to it..............this is true in all mammal life forms even when they are not aware of it. Â Mammals exploded after K-T........cold once again was what the environment became overnight after the asteroid hit. Â To maintain internal warmth they had to burn fat efficiently to survive. Â No carbs were around to grow because light cycles were disrupted and temperature fell like a brick. Â All the food was in the deep cold polar seas. Â Connecting any dots people? Â Our deep ancestors and us are still tied together............by one thing. Â EPIGENETICS.

Â 

Then read this gem of a blog from Grace at Dr. B.G blog: Â 

Â 

http://drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/2012/11/mitochondria-fuel-fluxxx-and-heat-nsfw.html

Â 

After reading her blog it is clear people are beginning to realize the implications of cold temperatures and energy efficiency or inefficiency and what a big brain actually means for biochemistry and life in general for wellness or for illness........The tide is turning because people are starting to question many parts of paleo dogma. Â That is a good thing. Â I hope when you read this post before the December webinar is confuses you...........then re read it right after you hear the December webinar for the first time..........and do it before the Q & A.........and I bet your mind will be opened wide to a new reality about all mammals and especially about modern human women and the challenges they face.......and why they might face it by design............moreover because they are built this way, what might the implications be when things go awry in circadian signaling for any reason...........you might understand your plight better if you have a vagina and breasts or love someone who has that type of equipment. Â 

Â 

When we all know better, we tend to do better.........EPIGENETICS/WOMEN/FACTOR X.........Trifecta.

We have a sweet spot for optimal function. The question is do we want to get there or remain comfortable? I think I've discovered that my sweet spot in CT is below 44 deg I recover faster & warm up quicker. That tells me my mitochondria prefer deep cold to moderate cold. Why I think they work better there b/c they don't have to work as hard it's effortless for them at those temps. Where as CT when the water is just a few deg warmer it's harder which tells me they're not as efficient at 50 deg. So if they're not efficient there they're down right sluggish in summer when it's hot So this leads me to question "Are women designed to function better at lower temps?" "Are we designed to leverage cold to influence our progeny?" I ask this b/c up until doing the LR the only other time I've ever been HOT like I am now is in pregnancy and all I wanted to do was lay down in the snow and revel in it but didn't for the fear I couldn't get up out or close to 3 ft of snow. What were my mitochondria doing? What are they doing now?

# Thread: What amazing changes are you noticing in your life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-09T01:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/post-66912>

I know there used to be a thread on what changes we are noticing but I thought maybe it was time for a new one.
Jackkruse.com is almost a year old in a couple of months. I thought it would be great to think about all the things we have accomplished courtesy of our own hard work and Dr. Kruse and all the amazing people on this forum and..... Dr. Kruse did say to put pen to paper so to speak!
When I read the posts by new people it brings to mind all the questions I had - so many things I wasn't sure of - even tho it is all here. I seemed to always need to circle back and pick up another piece of the puzzle - always because of something that some one else or JK said. Sometimes he nudges gently and some times he is like a freight train!
It has been an amazing journey that I wish I had taken years ago but none the less -I am here now. When I think about that in particular I realize that this journey only started for Dr. K himself 7 or 8 years ago.
I wanted to talk about changes that I notice - but - you know what - there are way to many. An example of this - I had been seeing a grief counselor for a couple of years. I hadn't seen her for over three months because of travel and life etc. until the other day. Well -she couldn't believe her eyes \[and ears\] There is a 360 degree change in me and we spent my whole hour talking about Jack Kruse!!! As usual for me lately - I couldn't sit still, couldn't stop with the silly grin and just this amazing sense of joy.
I think the most important thing that I have learned is to think ...think for myself and think about myself and others and listen to my body's cues. Epipaleo makes the signalling clearer. I am sure my wiring was pretty screwy/non existent before - that is a technical term by the way!
I would love to hear about the changes you are noticing and how it is affecting friends and family - the people you love most in your life..... and I think it would be great for Dr. Kruse to know that we are listening - even tho sometimes it must seem like we are not. He keeps doing the heavy lifting - so we need to as well - it seems only fair- that is if we want an exciting, dynamic life full of love and endless possibilities - in whatever form that takes. I have so much respect for everyone on this forum. We are all so different and contribute in many different ways - you never really know when something you say is going to have a positive affect on someone else and give them a missing piece to their puzzle. That happens for me over and over and over again. sometimes it takes days or weeks to sink in. LOL
I seems that this has turned out to be kind of an open love letter to all of us. I hope that doesn't sound gross!

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-09T10:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/post-66926>

I love this!

I've noticed:

\--I don't get calluses on my feet anymore.Â  They stay smooth for months now without pedicures!Â  Big one for me, as I actually own one of those skin-shaver things (not just the PedEgg, but the actual razor blade one) because I'd have to shave my callusesÂ weekly.Â  Always blamed it on my shoes, duh, now I know it was my diet.

\--I had a skin issue "down there" for decades.Â  I was given every test by every OB/GYN, including biopsies; no one could figure out the cause.Â  I was even sent to Baylor College of Medicine so that a Professor Emeritus could examine me.Â  No cause.Â  It was terrible and caused horrendous itching, rash, and also large white thick scaly patches.Â  The diagnosis bounced between pre-cancerous lesions to lichen sclerosis to just plain old eczema.Â  Maddening.Â  Yes, you guessed it....my skin has been completely clear "down there" for about a year.

\--I found me a new doc!Â  Yay!Â  Found her from paleophysicians.com, and she asked me if I'm eating "enough" fat!Â  LOL.Â  Got the complete Genova nutritional panel, and she has me on Vit. D and B's and curcumin, as well as other things.Â  It's like, OMG, a doctor who didn't just hand me a bunch of Rx's for nonsense drugs...what a concept.

\--Biggie:Â  Sleep.Â  I actually sleep straight through for 7-8 hours a night.Â  Vivid dreams.Â 

\--Not afraid of a 50 degree tub anymore!Â  I was a holdout.Â  When Dr. K. first started writing about CT, I thought ok, here's where I step off.Â  But one boring Saturday, I just....did it.Â  And look forward to my tub time!

I have more days of joy, where I'm amazed at the world and grateful for the people/blessings in my life.Â 

I have IBS symptoms only rarely now and they're mild -- compared to debilitating and fairly often. I don't crave sweets, I have less muscle pain,Â I have more energy and crave salt less.

It's taken a long time to get here, but I'm very happy with where I'm going.

# Thread: What's the best way to read the blog?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ruthie — 2012-12-09T12:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66938>

Hi there,

Â 

Any pointers for how to go about reading Dr. Kruse's information on the blog? Â I am just starting in December 2012, and feeling overwhelmed and unsure of how to methodically get through all the information. Â By date? Â Index? (I looked at the index, but each category didn't seem to be in any particular order.) Â 

Â 

Thanks,

Ruthie

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-09T13:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66940>

Hi Ruthie, and welcome.Â  Hey, the blogs are still overwhelming for those of us who have been here since last year at this time, so don't get too worried about it.Â  Thing is, you will read a blog about 3 times and still not fully understand it; that's ok.Â  It will start to make sense as you read different blogs.Â  You'll be like....oh....and light bulbs will start going off.

I'd say the best way to start is chronologically.Â  Skim through a blog post and read through the comments.Â  Skim through the next one, comments, and so on.Â  Then, when something occurs to you that you've maybe already heard before, go back to those first ones.Â  It's a lot like taking a test for a subject you just don't understand.Â  Also, read the Levees in the Quilt.Â  Don't worry if you don't understand them now, just sort of read through them, because you'll see references to some Levees in different places.

I've read every blog post multiple times, and I still go back.Â  I will go by index; for example, a few weeks ago, I wanted to re-read on Sleep, so I re-read all the sleep blogs.

And if you have questions on a particular blog after you've read through it and still don't understand it, and it hasn't already been asked in the comments section, feel free to post a question either here on the forums or on the blog itself.

### diane — 2012-12-09T16:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66945>

I agree with Pam - it does help to re-read the blogs a few times. And they do get easier to read.Â 

Â 

You may want to start with the Leptin Guide (I think there's a page that organizes them), then to CT and the BG series. The Hormone 101 blog is very important too.Â 

Â 

Don't try to rush through them- but if I got stuck and didn't understand something, I did keep moving then came back later. Sometimes it takes reading something else to help the pieces fall into place!

### caroline — 2012-12-09T17:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66950>

Great suggestions! Â I still struggle Â - I think because I will be reading and something I don't understand will come up so I will have to look that up and it becomes an endless circle. Â It does help to focus on one subject. Â I find tho - when you go back and re read -some things all of a sudden stand out or you missed them completely the first or second time around. Â We just keep trying! Â  Â Hopefully this is helping us to make new brain cells! Â A friend said to me the other day - you really have a handle on all this stuff and I thought OMG - if you only knew. Â fake it till you make it I guess. Â  Â  Â  Â A year from now..... you won't recognise Â YOU .....and you will be so proud.

### Ruthie — 2012-12-10T21:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-67066>

Thanks for the suggestions! Â Looks like we all have our work cut our for us!

Â 

Cheers,

Ruthie

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T10:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-67208>

It does not matter where you start........but you must begin.

# Thread: Started gaining weight back at 4 months
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-12-09T15:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-66942>

HI, everyone, I've been off the forum for awhile as I had a lot of personal life problems to attend to and had to take a computer break. My last hcg round ended beginning of May and I lost the 8 lbs I wanted to goal. I had a good summer maintaining on p3 Paleo, stayed at ldw and even a little below. Then, in September I started gaining around the time when it becomes fall but I can't say for sure. I know I started feeling thyroid symptoms coming back. I have very low t3, almost out of range at 2.6 (the range starts at 2.5) and my tsh is 1.5, t4 is in the middle, and rt3 is very high at 29 (range ends at 35). I have a conversion issue but I haven't arrived at the reason for it. So, with lots of hypothyroid symptoms for years, I was given Cytomel (t3) and started that in the summer at a very low dose of 5mcg. In the fall, I called the doc and said I didn't feel well (major symptom is fatigue) and he increased my Cytomel to 10 mcg. It seemed a little better but not great...not anywhere near how I felt in the summer, which was increased energy and more myself. ANd the 10mcg didn't stop the creeping weight gain. Then, another doc prescribed progesterone cream (200mg) compounded and I started that and gained another 5 lbs in a week and had awful symptoms from it (couldn't function, awful brain fog, nauseated) and think it was way too high of a dose. I can't fit in my clothes, bloated, lots of fatigue despite the 10mcg of cytomel. I don't know what to do about the progesterone cream as I certainly don't need to keep gaining weight. I think I'm going to stop taking it, it's just frightening to be gaining weight that I can't control.

I'm thinking of starting a round right after Christmas. I just feel so frustrated because I was done. I think something is wrong with me with this hormonal gain. This is not the first time this happened. I did 2 round in 2011, lost to goal, and gained it back, not because of eating but because of thyroid or some other Â hormonal imbalance. The docs I see are ok but not excellent, they haven't prevented my weight gain and they don't get a the root of what's wrong. So, I'm just wondering if anyone else out there has experienced this over and over, lose with hcg, gain it back. It's the only way I can lose any weight but it's also a lot slower than others lose.

### MsYo — 2012-12-11T14:45:10+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T10:56:17+0200

My free t3 is 2.6 (range 2.5-4.3 pg/mL)

tsh 1.350 (0.270-4.200 uIU/mL)

free t4 Â 1.15 ng/dL (0.8301.62 ng/dL0

Â 

I feel lousy. I don't know if it's a good idea for me to try another hcg round or not. Don't know if it's actually making me worse off in the long run.

I'm going to look this up Jack:Â a mitochondrial inefficiency that manifests via a bad hormone panel.

Thanks. I also recently found out I have the gene mutation MTHFR c677T, and I am Â homozygous with 2 copies. I am taking supps for that now, methylfolate, 1600 mcg a day, active b6, and methylcobalamin, via injection once weekly (.5 mL). I thought the b12 would give me some energy but I don't feel any different.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-04T16:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-85447>

> [AlaskaLadyie said:](/goto/post?id=85406)
>
> **Jack...... could we get somesort of explaination to what this means.... please**

When your mitochondria aren't functioning properly, it directly impacts your hormone panel. think of if this way: your car isn't running well, and you need to take it in for a tune up but don't/can't for what ever reason. your car continues to run badly, you spend more $ on gas b/c its not efficient using the fuel you give it.

we were designed to run on fat, but we've been told that's wrong for the past 30-40years and we've avoided it. so now we're trying to teach our body how to use fat for fuel and stop using glucose. when you get into using FAT as your fuel you can enter the PPP if you've got your hormones right fixed. when you enter the PPP you are using the ATP you make from the fat to allow your body to use water as energy instead of ATP... as you move toward this you begin to fix your mitochondria

### erixsparhawk — 2013-07-24T23:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-90752>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=88823)
>
> That is the difficult part ... to teach your body to use fat for fuel. I think that is my problem. My triglycerides have been consistently increasing on ketogenic and high fat diet. When I was low carb, moderate carbs, my trigs were 55, now they are 261.

I've been continuously in ketosis for over a year now and have done extensive blood testing. Here is what fixed it for me: Your trigs will reduce by about 25% if you cut out dairy and will drop back down to original levels if you also make a concerted effort to eliminated all sources of omega 6 fats i.e. pork and nuts become a rare indulgence and no more cooking or using fruit and vegetable oil (use tallow, butter, coconut or macadamia oils not olive, avocado, almond, lard, etc.) Lastly if you can't get grass fed meats switch to lean meats for protein and get your fat from other sources.

Essentially you are eating huge quantities of fat on a keto diet and when you calculate the total amount of grams omega 6 consumed you will see it becomes very high. Especially from nut flours.

I'm a firm believer that omega 6 reduction is far more important than supplementing omega 3 to reach balance. There is only so much PUFA that your metabolic pathways can handle and all PUFA is delicate and can easily be oxidized and do damage to your body.

My .02

# Thread: What is that distinctive icy sensation in my solar plexus region post CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-that-distinctive-icy-sensation-in-my-solar-plexus-region-post-ct.5117/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2012-12-10T20:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-that-distinctive-icy-sensation-in-my-solar-plexus-region-post-ct.5117/post-67062>

I have been meaning to ask for ages! Does anyone else get this? Â I getÂ an icyÂ sensation in the solar plexus region (I think I've got the location pinned down right) within a minute or two of exiting my cold bath. I only get it if the water is about 50 F or under. It lasts for just a few minutes and it's quite distinctive....like nothing else I've experienced.

Anyone else get this, or care to speculate/shed some light?

### endless — 2012-12-10T23:16:38+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T09:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-primal-spirit.5129/post-67196>

http://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-the-primal-spirit/

### nonchalant — 2012-12-12T10:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-primal-spirit.5129/post-67201>

This part really resonates with me:

> **Day 9 Wish:** _Dedicate your free time to things you have become passionate about, and watch your life expand_. Passion does not stem exclusively from one area; it can be different for each of us. For some it might be our job, our hobby, or found in our friends or family. One thing I wish for you today is to learn how to know when you have hit upon your passion. The first things you learn is that you will feel your days are too short. Doing will become your primary task in life, and result will be a secondary goal. You will feel a greater detachment and develop a sense of freedom. You will find happiness in doing precisely what you are doing at this moment all the time.

# Thread: Mountain Cedar....thy art my enemy I shall smite thee!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-12-12T01:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67181>

Anyway...I was told today pretty much the only thing in the air is Mountain Cedar...and apparently I have allergies.Â  I kept telling my mom it was a cold...until I went to Waxahachie from Lewisville where I live...which is around 1:30 away...or so and my symptoms vanished.Â  So...I was wrong it was allergies.

Â 

I was hoping to get some good tips on getting rid of this allergy and dealing with it until I do.Â  If I understand correctly many types of allergies are "curable", but I'm guessing I'll need to do something specific and it may take time.Â  So I need a good way to deal with symptoms until I get rid of it.Â 

Â 

Thanks for any and all help.Â  This allergy thing sucks...one good thing about Midland, TX...dry not many allergens.Â  Much more in this area and I hope this is one of a very small amount of things I have an allergy to...as its very annoying.

### endless — 2012-12-12T03:13:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67183>

Pycnogenol, nettle tea or extract, beta glucans, and a good omega 3/6 ratio are all things I've heard suggested for seasonal allergies. I hope you can get relief soon!

### fitness@home — 2012-12-12T06:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67185>

I live in Houston. Every springtime there is a period where tree pollen is very heavy (it even coats my car with a green layer!). What works for me is taking Quercetin with Bromelain. I use NOW brand.

### Destiny — 2012-12-12T07:04:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67187>

My husband uses cold showers, twice a day. Used to take Claritin-d and Afrin for years. Now, he doesn't have to.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T10:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67204>

there ya go.........

### nonchalant — 2012-12-12T12:17:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67214>

I used to suffer horribly with allergies, mountain cedar and ragweed were the worst, then other tree pollens in spring, and grass pollens.Â  Once I reduced my overall inflammation level, all I get now is a very slightly runny nose on occasion, and sometimes I sneeze.Â  No big deal.Â  No asthma any more, no ear infections, no sinus headaches, no eye infections.

I think if your inflammation level is high, seasonal allergies add quite a bit to that level of inflammation, and your body goes nuts.Â  If your inflammation level is low, you have a good buffer for these type allergies, and you get minimal symptoms.

Â 

That's my story and I'm stickin' to it.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-12T12:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67220>

Interesting, given my last hs-CRP was **0.05** and my allergies went completely out of control last summer and are still bothering me. Â I've been doing cold showers for nearly a year and am finally racking up the CT hours on a regular basis. Â So something else is evidently at work here.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-12T13:23:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67226>

If its green and grows I'm allergic. Â if its small fluffy, and meows - I'm deathly allergic (full blown asthma attack that requires hospitalization) Â If it can mold - I'm allergic. Â If it feeds of dust (dust mites/cockroaches) Â I'm allergic.Â 

The only place I don't have allergies (w/the exception to cats) is in CO, UT, ID, MT, WY, Mountains of AZ, NM, and CA. Â  I need high desert step and HIGH elevation to be allergy free.Â 

Â 

other than moving - which we can't do right this instant (But DH is now on board w/in the next couple of years) I've started doing allergy shots. Â in the 3 weeks since I've started I've already noticed that I use my inhaler less. Â the allergist I went to is an immunologist - he pulled labs and did a spirometry - pre/post allergy test - and noted that I don't respond to inhaled steroids. Â he also told me that the oral sigulair wouldn't work for me for preventing an attack, and that eventually after enough allergy shots I can come off that medication. Â he thinks eventually that I'll no longer need the abuterol as well.Â 

Â 

I'm also taking the now brands quercertin - 3x/day. Â definitely helps.

7.Â  _**Critical point: The best time to work out biologically occurs when it is **least likely****_ **to be convenient for you because of our neolithic lives wont allow it.Â  I re-tooled my entire schedule as a surgeon to make this work optimally for me to lose weight and change my body.Â  It is that important biologically to get to optimal.Â  1-5 PM is the ideal workout window**.Â  For best results, try to do the exercise in bright sunlight.

nice find! Â When you do all the things I mentioned in that blog do you know what happens naturally? Â The Leptin Rx winds up colliding with the Dopamine Rx and the Epi Paleo Rx. Â The Dopamine Rx allows forÂ your hormones adjustment phase back to allostasis to occur slowly and naturally. Â It begins to affecting your neurotransmitters in your reward tracts of the frontal lobes. With time as you rewire you will notice a clam come over you......it took you decades to become broken......so allow yourself time to heal. You begin to know _**intuitively**_ you are proceeding on a path to wellness. When you perceive this, then realize when the rage comes into your consciousness from any desire or craving that it is the result of your brain rewiring and the job is not yet complete. The Epi paleo Rx is the train track that keeps you on the correct path as you move forward. Â The only think that changes around you then is the scenery from your locomotive's windows. Â 

Take that thought in very slowly and allow it to alter your present biologic response. The behaviors of others will still make your emotions boil but you will begin to see you are really in total control of your responses. Staying on course, or the train tracks, is a function of eating the via your biologic dictum. Â It silences your bad choices without effort. Â You just have failed to realize that power has always resided within you. When you fully rewire your brain and your dopamine and serotonin tracts are once again at allostasis you will see the other benefits of how all the Rx's act to reset you in unison. I promise...........that is how the conductor makes great music from the orchestral parts.

# Thread: Do I HAVE TO eat 3 meals per day when starting out on the Leptin Rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-12T16:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67249>

Just wondering if it's mandatory to eat 3 meals per day when starting out on the Leptin Rx?

Some days I feel like having 3 meals, others, I only feel like having 2. Or maybe 2 with a light "snack" in lieu of lunch in between. (As long as that "snack" consists of Epi-Paleo approved food. i.e. a piece of dark chocolate, a few pieces of shrimp etc, but not a "full" meal. Or is it vital to get as much fat ingested at the start of Leptin Rx to start the whole adaptation/weaning off of carbs process, and to initiate fat burning, or the Leptin sensitivity activation?

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-12T18:17:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67264>

You want to eat BAB upon rising like it's your religion. After that the guidelines I believe are no snacking between meals which should be 4 hours apart with your last meal 4 hrs before bed. When you start eating a proper BAB(lots of fat and protein) you should not be hungry for lunch. After BAB you should do what feels right. If you're not hungry, don't eat.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T18:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67265>

Yup, everything Dali said.....

Â 

I started out eating three meals for several months.Â  Then I went to two, back to three, and now I am steady at two meals a day.Â  I've been at this for almost a year.Â  But most importantly is the BAB.Â  Everyone is different and will need/want differently.Â  Find your sweet spot and your food life will change for the better...you will just "know".

### caroline — 2012-12-12T19:15:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67270>

after all this time - I am wondering if I am doing this right.
I read - when you become LS you can cut back on the BAB. For some reason - I don't remember why - I changed my eating patterns. I started eating only when I was hungry. It just seemed intuitive.
I would eat pretty lightly around 10am. A couple of BPC and some protein - depending on my hunger signals.
Then - dinner - around 4:00pm or even earlier if I was hungry and I ate until I was full - sometimes a lot and sometimes not much. I feel terrific.
Now - I am very hungry when I wake up - so I eat then. Dinner is still around/between 2:30 and 4:00pm.
I am assuming that since I am following my bodies cues that this is okay? previously by body wouldn't have had a clue about signalling if it had risen up and hit it in the face!

### furface — 2012-12-12T19:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67272>

Oh I have no problem with the BAB, I'm following those "orders" faithfully, and religiously.

I was just wondering if when starting out on the LR, and esp being overweight if 3 meals per day, esp so that we can ingest as much fat as we can per day to get our bodies adpated to eating no grains, no legumes, no dairy per se, limited fruits etc, and to get our bodies Leptin sensitive again is basically mandatory.

I've had no cravings for snacks between meals...surprisingly, or even at night.

It's just that at times after my BAB I'm not really all that hungry until dinner time around 6 P.M., but I don't want to throw my Leptin Reset out of whack if I don't ingest enough proteins or fats throughout the day.

I honestly never thought I'd be posting a topic on wanting to eat less.

I just want to do this right, and as best as I can.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T23:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67296>

After doing and watching the Dec 2012 Webinar I think you really would benefit from listening to it. Â It is a convergence of 4 Rx I have written to explain a very confounding topic for people. Â Men and women will get a ton from it in my view. Â I showed it to three people and they wanted a burnt copy......

### furface — 2012-12-13T00:43:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67307>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> After doing and watching the Dec 2012 Webinar I think you really would benefit from listening to it. Â It is a convergence of 4 Rx I have written to explain a very confounding topic for people. Â Men and women will get a ton from it in my view. Â I showed it to three people and they wanted a burnt copy......

Awesome, thanks Dr. Kruse. I can't wait to listen to it. When, and where will it be available? I really am trying to give my all into this, and I'm doing my best to follow all the guidelines, and information. I love face dunking, love the limited CT I can do by having cold showers. Love the BAB, love seafood. I just want to do the best that I can. I apologize if I've been asking too many questions.

### caroline — 2012-12-13T01:09:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67311>

Oh I have no problem with the BAB, I'm following those "orders" faithfully, and religiously.

I was just wondering if when starting out on the LR, and esp being overweight if 3 meals per day, esp so that we can ingest as much fat as we can per day to get our bodies adpated to eating no grains, no legumes, no dairy per se, limited fruits etc, and to get our bodies Leptin sensitive again is basically mandatory.

I've had no cravings for snacks between meals...surprisingly, or even at night.

It's just that at times after my BAB I'm not really all that hungry until dinner time around 6 P.M., but I don't want to throw my Leptin Reset out of whack if I don't ingest enough proteins or fats throughout the day.

I honestly never thought I'd be posting a topic on wanting to eat less.

I just want to do this right, and as best as I can.

I think if you re read the leptin RX - you will find that it is okay to go to 2 meals a day. You will know when. After a BAB you will be able to go to around 4:00pm. You just can't fit another meal in - you are too full! amazing isn't it? The most important thing is the BAB and the timing

### furface — 2012-12-13T01:47:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67318>

Thanks Caroline. I'm actually surprised that adapting to a lot of this isn't really as difficult as I expected. Especially as I mentioned before, not snacking. And as far as only having 2 meals per day instead of 3, as I also said, some days I do feel like having 3, other days, only two. I have read the Leptin Rx, but just wanted to make sure I was doing things right.

I do faithfully have my BAB within 30 minutes of rising, and it really does make a huge difference. I just want to do all I can to lose as much fat as I can, as fast as I can. I know that may not be the proper mindset, but when you've been as fat as I have for so long, even though I've always had plenty of energy...until I found out I had extremely low testosterone levels, and severe sleep apnea that is, both of which totally wiped out my energy levels, so hopefully people will understand why I can't wait to see results. Naturally that's not all I'm interested in, I want to be the best I can be, and always feel great, and be full of energy, and look great etc.

I realize that this is a lifelong commitment, and I am committed to going as far as I can, as best I can. Each small step we take each day, is another part we add to the final, giant, puzzle that is the best individual "I" we can each become. It's up to us to decide how we complete that puzzle.

I'm not hesitant to trying some new things...notice I said some, not all.(aka raw meat) LOL But I'm trying my best to keep a positive attitude about things. I mean sure, we all have our daily issues, and problems with our daily lives...who doesn't? But we can't always dwell on them, or on the negative aspects, as it will really bring a person down fast. Besides, dwelling on them isn't going to change anything, or make them go away. So like I said, I'm trying to remain as positive as I can.

I think of every "new" thing that I've tried in all of this, the one that I actually enjoy the most, is face dunking in a bucket of ice water. LOL. I find it so invigorating, and refreshing. I don't even mind the slight "burning" sensation I feel on my face. (Just the forehead, as I had a bit of bruising there recently.) But the icy, cold water just feels soo nice!

Oh and of course being able to eat all that bacon isn't a bad thing either. haha

# Thread: Paleo diet supplements/email

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-12T18:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-diet-supplements-email.5140/post-67263>

Darn it! Â Jack closed the [thread](http:///forum-2/ask-jack/any-diet-that-tells-you-it-should-have-a-designed-supplement-line/#p67239) over in the "Ask Jack" forum about the email he received today. Â I wanted to comment after he posted the email because it just made me LOL. Â SERIOUSLY!? Â I need specialized supplements to make paleo work for me?? Â Really???

(insert a lot of giggling on my part)

Granted, I've been trying to keep up with everything Jack has been teaching us and I'm not optimal yet but you know what? Â I'm starting to feel so much better it just amazes me. Â The more fish I eat the better my brain is working ("they" used to say when I was a wee lass that fish was brain food. Â Guess "they" knew what they were talking about). Â 

The more I do CT the stronger I feel. Â It may take a while yet but I"m getting there and I wouldn't come off this path for anything. Â And I KNOW, not just because Jack says so but because I feel it -- there's no other way to say it -- I just FEEL it that THIS path is the right one, the best one to heal all the decades of damage I have done to myself. Â It's working. Â Nothing else I did ever worked or if it did it was short term at best.

It's sad that so many out there would/will fall for this specialized supplement crap. Â But try to remember they are searching too. Â I found Jack because I had tried (I thought) everything and was about to just give up. Â I had been half-heartedly reading through the forums at MDA but nothing, and I mean NOTHING, was working for me. Â I kept gaining. Â I'd complain and people would rush in and tell me I needed to eat more! Â Exercise! Â Just DO IT and all would be well. Â Or worse, I must not be doing it right. Â That one always made me angry.

Fighting this is not a good approach either. Â We all (as Jack is so wont to say) must be starfish. Â As we heal and reach optimal it's then our task, our DUTY, to share this with those we love and even those we don't know. Â In the meantime, those hawking these supplements will fill their bank account. Â People will think they're doing the right thing. Â We cannot stop it, all we can do is stand on the sidelines and offer assistance when the time is right.

### Lyndra — 2012-12-12T20:06:33+0200

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-12T20:13:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-diet-supplements-email.5140/post-67279>

I take vitamins and a double handful of supplements daily. Â What I don't need or want is a "special" supplement to help me transition to a "paleo" diet. Â That's what was being hawked in the email.

### Lyndra — 2012-12-12T20:21:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-diet-supplements-email.5140/post-67281>

I take my share of supplements but I know why I take them and it's not because I'm eating sub-standard food. The idea that you can create a "healthy" breakfast food (hey, let's call it Krave) that is 1/3 sugar by fortifying it with vitamins is what perturbs me. I don't object to the idea of taking supplements for specific conditions or even a transition phase into paleo while your body still isn't digesting fats well or for other reasons. I think back then I was not as diplomatic with my friend as I could have been.Â

### kathylu — 2012-12-13T00:30:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-diet-supplements-email.5140/post-67305>

Darleen, I think you covered it. I am taking quite a few supplements too. I understand why though...and I am looking forward to recovering to a point where I will no longer need them. That's the kind of transition to paleo that I am looking for.

I am very bothered by Chris Kresser. He was dispensing detailed medical advice via his website before he had even completed his L.Ac. (acupuncture license). I am not aware that he is trained or credentialed in any other way. Now to give him some credit, a lot of what he says is ok. But not enough for me to jump on a line of custom supplements he and Robb Wolf have designed.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-13T07:42:05+0200

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-13T08:48:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-diet-supplements-email.5140/post-67341>

I've found that I need less supplements the further down this road I'm traveling. Yes I'm taking specific things right now to support healing and once my labs say I don't need it any more I'm not going to be taking them.

It's crazy that pple try to push supplement to "heal" your ills. My chiro does this. But now that I'm eating properly & using BHRT he's boggled why I don't need the supplements I used to take. He keeps push DH to get fish oils & DH tells him I eat enough seafood for all my o3 And then the chiro is floored when DH tells him he's eating seafood btwn 14-18x/week & then he goes on a rant its too much! # Thread: Confused a bit about something.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-12T18:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67269>

I've been watching a few youtube videos by Robb Wolf on the Paleo diet, and he says basically it's eating lots of lean protein, vegetables, and avoiding grains, legumes, dairy (in a nutshell).

That part I understand.

What's confusing me is the lean protein statement.

I thought when we are on the LeptinRx we should be ingesting as much "healthy" fats as we can, some of which includes bacon, bacon fat, regular grass-fed ground beef since it contains more omega 3 fats, and CLA than lean, or extra lean ground beef, grass-fed lamb for instance etc, now aren't all of these not very lean sources of protein? So should we eat these or not?

I know that shellfish, and crustaceans, and other types of seafood are the base, and foundation of the Leptin Rx diet, but these meats are also allowed, but I'm confused about whether or not they're considered "lean" proteins.

Or is there a difference between what we should eat during Leptin Rx (an Epi-Paleo diet) esp if we're overweight to begin with, and then what we should be eating on an Epi-Paleo, or Paleo diet once we're LS, even if we still want to lose weight.

Should we be eating, bacon, grass-fed REGULAR ground beef over GF Lean, or Extra Lean ground beef, GF Lamb, grain free, hormone free pork etc? Both on Leptin Rx, and while LS if we're trying to lose weight?

Â 

Sorry if I'm being naive, or for being confused.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T19:29:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67271>

My understanding is that the Leptin Rx is approx. 60% fat. You get that in many ways.Â  I use FitDay on my phone to track it although not every meal or even every day.Â  I use it as a guide for fat consumption since everything else I know what to eat.

Â 

Maybe the next thing I should (or shouldn't) say is to stop listening to Robb Wolf. Maybe that was a bit harsh but in my experience he is only good for a bit of basic Paleo info. Beyond that he is too mainstream and I feel in a system to make money. Good on ya for questioning him though. Just my 2 cents! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### furface — 2012-12-12T19:52:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67274>

Thanks Patty, I appreciate yourÂ  honesty. At times I think we can become over inundated with information, and it gets confusing just who or what to believe, even if they're basically on the same page, or have the same ideology about a subject.Â 

I have nothing against someone wanting to make money, as long as the information they're providing is actually beneficial to their target audience. I'm just trying to get as much information as possible.

I love bacon, lamb, pork (as long as it's from good, healthy ungrained, unhormoned sources), heck I may even have the odd free range chicken or turkey meal once in a blue moon. It may not be completely Epi-Paleo approved, but is one meal from those sources every 3 months or so going to completely ruin my progress?

I love fish, wish I could afford lobster twice a week, can't wait to try some dungeness crab (and not from a can), am even excited to try clams...not so much oysters, as the can of smoked oysters I had last week were gritty, and tasted like dirt. But I'm willing to give them another shot. I love prawns. So the seafood aspect is no problem.

I just don't want to sabotage myself by eating something that I shouldn't eat.

As far as the GF regular ground beef vs GF lean, or extra lean ground beef goes, or even the GF lamb, and pork is concerned, maybe while on the LR and trying to consume as much "good" fats as we can, I should stick with the "fattier" versions, even if trying to lose weight, but once I become LS, and trim down, then perhaps switch to leaner versions of those meats, and limit my bacon intake etc. Unless I have that wrong as well.

Someone should just make a list of what we should or should not eat if trying to lose weight while on Leptin Reset, and once we're Leptin Sensitive. Plain and Simple. Or maybe I'm just too stupid to figure that out for myself, even though there is so much conflicting information.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T21:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67288>

Yes it is a lot in the beginning to figure out.Â  You will figure out the goods and bads sooner rather than later as it seems you are questioning and researching on your own.Â  Get in touch with your intuition and try to use that as well.Â  We are all different and it helps.

Keep in mind that some lose weight some don't right away.Â  I lost 9# in the first month and gained it right back when I started CTing and the detox started.Â  It was six more months of no more loss or gain after that. I think a lot of things had to change inside of me and then I dropped almost all of that in a matter of a couple weeks.Â  Once your body senses that what you are doing it right and you get to fixing some of the broken systems the weight will do what it is supposed to. It just may not be right away so don't get discouraged.Â  Then again you may be one of the lucky ones!Â  Good luck!Â

### furface — 2012-12-12T21:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67289>

Thanks again Patty. You're right, we are all individuals, and as the saying goes, Individual results will vary. Heck, I've lost 12 pounds since I started a week ago, Monday. I hope I don't stop losing, and have to wait another 6 months before I start losing again though. I realize we can't expect instant gratification, but it would be nice to see some results so we don't get discouraged. It's only human nature to do so. Plus I want to look good when I go on holidays next year. haha

Â 

Best of luck to you as well, Patty.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-12T23:54:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67303>

Furface,Â  this is truly confusing at first.Â  If you just eat the right fats and proteins -Â meaning red meat and as much seafood as you can, you'll be great.Â Your mealÂ does not have to beÂ dripping in fat at all times, at least as far as I know. Â For some of us who started this 1.5 yrs ago, epi-paleo was not part of the initial program and people were still doing great.Â  Jack introduced that later to us.Â Â I think I read where you have lost 12 lbs. in a week; if so, that is truly phenomenal and leads me to believe you are completely on track.Â  Mix it up.Â  Eat some read meat for the sat. fat meals and some seafood meals for the dha.Â  Give your body what it needs and it won't argue with you.Â 

I guess you have do to your own research as to who you choose to believe.Â  None of us can tell you who and what to believe.Â  I trust Jack completely but that is after I have read my brains out for the last couple of years.Â  He is leading me down the right path.Â  Paleo is far better than any sad diet and thus has merit for those that don't know any better.Â  But, like Jack says: when you know better, you do better!Â  Keep reading and trying and eventually you will figure this out for you.Â  Some people say they actually lose more weightÂ while eating seafood vs. red meat.Â  It is different for all of us.

As for eating according to what your needs are, have you read the blog Jack wrote for after you become ls?Â  This will answer a lot of questions.Â  ThereÂ are really great answers in the comments of the blogs too.Â  If you haven't read those for the before ls and after ls I would start there for sure.Â

### furface — 2012-12-13T00:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-a-bit-about-something.5141/post-67304>

Thanks again, Vkiernan. You make great points.

I have read many of the blogs, I believe I've read the before LS, and after LS ones, although to be totally honest, I find the length of them at times a bit distracting, and wish he would get to the point a bit quicker. haha. No offense meant of course, but as I've mentioned before, I like taking the direct route, not the scenic route most of the time, and some of the techno babble can get confusing, and makes me lose interest in what I'm reading. But I will reread them.

Â 

Another great point you make is about not HAVING to drown each meal in fat. I guess I'm just thinking that since it's been mentioned that fat should be the largest component of the three macronutrients in each meal, then protein, and miniscule amount of carbs, then if my BAB for instance contains say 60 grams of carbs, then if fat should make up 70-75 % of the macronutrient total, the meal should contain over 80 grams of healthy fats. (Sorry if my math is off, but I don't feel like figuring out the exact numbers right now. lol) As I said, I'm just trying to "follow orders".

Â 

As I've mentioned, I began this a week and a half ago, which was about a week and a half since I last got paid (I get paid once per month), so my freezer was pretty much stocked up with what I had bought previously. Some grass-fed lean ground beef, which I formed into burgers before freezing them individually, quite a bit of lean ground turkey, which I also preformed into burgers, some salmon, tilapia, and a few individually wrapped pieces of pork shoulder butt steaks. (Boneless).Â  I don't like to waste food, and don't know anyone to give some of it away too...plus if I did, I wouldn't have anything to eat, so I've been "cleaning" out my freezer, while trying to add some of the better choices when I've come into a few dollars before payday.

I've bought a couple of bags of frozen prawns, a can of crabmeat (meh it was okay) a can of smoked oysters (tasted like dirt), 3 nice big fillets of Basa (white roughy), and lots of bacon for my BAB, along with some free-run eggs. I've also managed to get a grass-fed lamb chop, and some grass-fed beef short ribs...OMG sooo good.

When I get paid next week, I'm planning on buying plenty more basa, some salmon, bacon, GF ground beef, and short ribs, prawns, clams, I might even try some fresh oysters. Hopefully I'll even be able to afford some fresh dungeness crab, and esp some fresh Lobster. As well as free run eggs, coconut oil...which I also bought a small jar of last week, some kerrygold butter, heavy whipping cream to make a nice clam chowder. (Don't worry, no potatoes...I hate potatoes in soups or stews etc) Some 85, 90, 99 % dark chocolate. Baby bok choy, asparagus, long english cucumbers, and even some sea veggies if I can find some. Might even pick up some pistachios and macadamia nuts to have a FEW as part of a meal...just for a change of pace.

So I have a good idea of what to get, I'm just waiting to be able to get it. haha.

I'm actually surprised that it really hasn't been difficult to eliminate snacks. Also, my blood sugar levels have been fantastic, and that also inspires me.

I'm going to give this my best effort, and so far I'm liking it very much.

I also appreciate all the help and support the members of this board have given me, and wish you, and everyone else continued best of luck, and success.

### caroline — 2012-12-13T16:50:25+0200
