### caroline — 2013-01-02T18:19:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69060>

Does anyone have a full proof way to maximise a consult with JK???? Honestly - I write everything down - I read as much as I can and still something happens! My mind turns to Salami ... and I seem to always overlook important questions. I am a pretty efficient person - but all that seems to fly out the window.....WTH....
Than God for recordings - but still ... there has to be a better and more efficient way to do this.... any ideas? what has worked for you? It would be so much more efficient if he could just read our mind .....would save a ton of time....surely he would be up to the task......

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-02T21:40:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69071>

One of the things I tried was to keep notes in my phone when I think of something I need to ask him. It worked for some things on the last consult but I didn't start it early enough. I look forward to seeing what other ideas people have come up with!

### diane — 2013-01-02T22:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69075>

I took furious notes during mine, and have gone over the notes quite a few times. But I'm thinking that it's kind of like reading the blogs - it takes more than one to really get everything out! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-02T23:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69084>

my advice.........turn off that little voice in your head that is always thinking as we talk to one another.......and just focus on listening. Record it. The educational consults are not meant for you to ask a ton of questions.......they are really about teaching you how to understand how your biology takes electrons in food and uses them to make energy......and how your particular labs help decipher what is going on in your Ferrari engines. I think too many people try to do too much during the consult instead of the intended purpose. You will see after you employ this technique your follow up consults will lead to deeper meaning and understanding. For members......anything that comes up later can be asked in Ask Jack........and I usually will answer it unless it is way off base for the forum.

### caroline — 2013-01-03T04:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69101>

Actually - I am pretty proud of myself. I was thinking about this all afternoon and I realized something .....when I go to see my Doc - I have a limited amount of time - 15 minutes or 30 minutes if I book a double. I would never dream of trying to ask a million questions ... I would just focus on a couple of things and then listen and learn. So Dr. K. - I figured that out on my own. It is much more important to just listen and learn.
Thanks for putting that so clearly. We definitely do better when we know better.

### caroline — 2013-01-03T05:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69106>

Dr. Kruse ..... in your response above ....you said something pretty special ... "we are talking to one another". I guess we aren't used to that attitude from our Doctors. It never seems like a two way street. You are educating us in so many ways. I used to watch a remarkable Kidney Specialist interacting with his patients in the hospital. He wasn't my husband's Doc but I saw him in action so many times. He always listened intently to his patients and always wanted their opinions. He spent at least as much time listening as he did talking. He would think nothing of cruising into the ward at 11:00pm because he had thought of something and he always joked that he only saw half the amount of patients on his day off as he did during his regular clinic. A very special guy.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T06:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69111>

I got a good advice from Adriana before I got my consult. She told me that I should only listen and not talk too much myself. I tried, but since my papers were lost in the 'either' somewhere between Germany and Tennessee, Dr. Kruse had none of my medical history or lab results. So I tried to make up by giving him as much information as possible. Since he is very intuitive he knew most of it anyway and had it figured out often before I mentioned the result. But I guess I wasted a lot of time by affirming something he already knew. He gave me the prescription of which supplements to take in that consult which is the same he gave in his December webinar. Since the webinar I understand why. Since I trust Dr. Kruses advice, I do not question it, only try to understand the implications. Guess I am like his son, just saying, tell me how you did it and I do the same. It all makes so much sense. The understanding for me comes mostly later, when I can observe the results on me.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-03T10:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69126>

Question everything including me..............just sayin'

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T16:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-a-foolproof-way-to.5321/post-69196>

Don't feel bad ... my consult was a disaster from the get go. Poor Jack! Add in to that the fact I was so MORTIFIED I couldn't even LOOK at him ... I can laugh about it now but it was just awful. However, the end result has been I have opened up emotionally to parts of myself I have tried to hide from and have done a complete 180Â° turnaround from where I was prior to the consult.

and yes, once I find a new doc here and can figure get new labs I'll be booking another one. Hopefully next time I won't have to make Jack chase me all over the internets and I will be able to at least be coherent and not trying to hide under my desk.

sorry about all that Jack. I still feel like I owe you a big one.

Don't worry, Darleen, I got exactly what I wanted, some real good advice. And I put it into motion immediately. Until I heard the webinar about weight loss and women though, I did not exactly know why I was taking all that stuff and honestly, I was really choking it down. Now I take an extra of each, which makes everybody at my office look at me incrediously. By the time I am done I am not hungry anymore. So now I have a new technique. I chew a bite, put the capsules with it and swallow it. It gives me at least the illusion of just eating food.
But now I am so happy to get back to eating epi-paleo. Today was my last day of hCG and I was dreaming about bacon and eggs. And I already have a HUGE pig leg with loads of fat on it in my fridge which is waiting for me to be cooked and roasted. Yummy!

# Thread: Just wondering.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T20:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69068>

These last three days I've started having massive adrenaline runs at the drop of a hat - well the sound of a voice more like. To start with I was feeling a bit despondent that after all the work I've been doing I had a new set of symptoms. Then it came to me that given that I've had massive fatigue, CFS/fibromyalgia + multiple symptoms all my life I was wondering if the adrenal rush is a step forward - an improvement. Perhaps I'm actually producing adrenaline but my body doesn't know how to manage it properly yet.

I've been paleo eating 90-95% of the time for five years. Weight loss 20kg during this time, mostly in three periods of time and maintenance during the times of autoimmune flares. I knew with the AI and exercise intolerance that things weren't right so I finally decided to take Jack's stuff seriously. I dropped out eggs, solanums (potatoes, tomatoes etc) and added mussels and offal as breakfast foods (instead of bacon and eggs). Then I couldn't buy offal as the shops didn't have them so I just ate the mussels (New Zealand green lipped mussels with a little bit of home made bacon (salt and smoke cured, no nitrites). Other meats are largely grass fed. I went back to having cold water baths, though they are currently about 80 degrees which is all I can manage at the moment or on a couple of days trekking out to the beach for a swim. For the last four years I've been rolling out of bed at dawn and taking the dog for a walk and not wearing sun glasses as I walk into the rising sun. I go to bed about an hour after dusk in summer and within three hours in winter. Then over Christmas I had prawns in my diet as well instead of other meats at a number of meals. I have meditated regularly for years and am good at managing all the psycho-social-spiritual issues that come up.

Other things I've done is to stop taking 5HTP in November for depression as I didn't feel like I needed it any more - no depression after 60 years - that's massive for me. Then stopped taking the thyroid meds in December and I haven't got cold or had the other myriad of symptoms I used to have. Then stopped taking dozens of supplement pills a day as Jack says supplements should be for the short term not forever. PreChristmas I was waking feeling a deep and huge total body experience joy peaking through my normal morning feeligns.

Then the adrenaline starts running and I have anxiety about things I have no need to be anxious about. So... is this things getting worse due to a four day eating blip over Christmas (2 servings of Christmas pudding and too many chocolates and sweets, but no real pigging out)? Or is it an indicator that post Christmas I was eating more shell fish and crustaceans and gone back to the cold baths and things are improving and I only have high cortisol now, not the total adrenal fatigue with depressed cortisol?

Any thoughts anyone?

### nonchalant — 2013-01-02T22:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69074>

Hi Nana, welcome!
My first thought is that face dunking into ice water might ease your anxiety fairly quickly.
Perhaps some of the supplements are still needed? Magnesium or something else? I know I have been dropping supplements, but now my sleep is messed up. The holidays have also disrupted my normal CT and sun watching/light avoiding patterns. I need to get things back to normal and see if things magically even out, or if I need to add back in some supplements. Or add some better nutrition, as Jack would say.

How long were you taking the 5-HTP? Serotonin needs to be balanced with dopamine.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-02T23:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69087>

Thanks for the welcome Nonchalant. I was taking the 5 HTP for about three years. It was the only thing that got rid of my residual depression. After I had worked through my life issues from every angle I was still depressed - though not deeply so - till I started the 5HTP. That worked a treat. And I suspected I was somewhat dopamine deficient but wasn't sure what to do about that.

I started the face dunking yesterday and might have overdone it as I had a spot on the tip of my nose this morning. I dunked very briefly - 1-5 seconds was all I could manage, about 8 times I think, till I could do it no more.

I took some notes from Jack's adrenal fatigue posts and will add a few supplements back. So will go look see what I have in my cupboard. I know I have some green tea extract there. Also go put some more water to make ice in the freezer.
"Adrenal fatigue Rx for cortisol elevation: Control cytokine response by controlling fast acting eicosanoid hormone system through Leptin Rx, CT protocol, supplements or foods with curcumin, quercetin, resveratrol, pycnogenol, green tea extract."

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-03T02:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69097>

Any idea of how long "pretty quickly" is when it comes to easing anxiety? Two face dunks done and a third one coming up. Plus I had a teaspoon of tumeric (curcumin) and a green tea capsule. If no-one knows about how long, I'll let you know how long it takes me.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-04T01:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69258>

Day 3 of face dunking. Adrenaline and anxiety now only around one issue but it still woke me up around 3.30am and I've been awake since. I wasn't dreaming of anything relating to what makes me anxious and there is no reason to be anxious about the topic which sets me off so I think the 3.30am wake up was physiological, not psychological.

### Inger — 2013-01-04T01:59:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69259>

Nana,
I have suffered quite a lot from anxiety too.. since childhood. I guess I somehow have it from my mother as she still have anxiety issues. I am thinking my bulemia was a way to try to deal with that anxiety too. It "kind of" went away after binging and purging but sure it did not lasted for long. It was very stupid, indeed.

What helps anxiety disappear.. to me?

To eat raw. Yes, it is not something I understand why, but it works. It really works. I have tested it on and off many many times. When I eat cooked I get anxious much easier. Raw makes my nerves strong as steel..huh. Or almost. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

It also makes it easier for me to face the stressors and deal with them in a good way.
To eat crap like puddings and such is really bad.. at least for me. So I never do that. IDK why but my body does not like it at all.. or my psyche. Cigarettes does it to me too. If I smoke just a half cigarette I can feel it for days after. I get a low. Nope. Not worth it at all. I always tell myself I need to feel happy and to feel love inside all the time, and enjoy my life every minute! Because you never know when you die and I want to die happy! It makes the fear for dying go away quite a lot too.. because I figure, when I die it will be the state of mind I am in that continues..

No idea if it is true but I like to think like that.

Then.. coffee adds anxiety if I already have it. So to quit coffee might help! Coffee might be absolutely no issue for me if I feel well and happy and stressless.. but the more stressed out I am the worse I feel from coffee..

Then.. if I do too much CT I might get a weird feeling of anxiety around my heart. It can last for many days. I think it is detox of some sort.. I am not sure though. But I keep my sessions short and do not overdo it. But it is only from submerging the whole body in close to freezing water, icepacks or facedunking never get me this feeling. Might be only a physical thing as it feels like my abdomen and heart area get frozen somehow.. feels very much like anxiety though and I do not like it.

To let go. That is the mental work that have helped me. To let go of anything I was clinging to. It really helps anxiety too.
To always do my best, to do the right things even if they are painful, to face my fears. Just go through them. If you feel anxious, just ask yourself, why? Dig deep. It is actually fun stuff to do that work, very interesting. I am by far not done yet..
I have figured.. fear and anxiety are somehow very close. And what have I to fear, if I do the right things? It might be hard to know what the right things are at times, but then I read, and search...and search. And then I find the answers. It is lots of work but it is very interesting! So it does not feel like work at all.

When I use both the mental and the physical way I don't suffer from anxiety any more. There is just a beautiful peace inside.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T11:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69294>

getting in the cold ocean is the best panacea for anxiety

### Inger — 2013-01-05T16:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wondering.5322/post-69484>

It always helps me to go for a dip in the river too.

Totally. Maybe it is the grounding? Together with the cold..

I thought about you Aussie nana today at work and I wanted to add that lightly cooked low fat fish/mussels with seaweeds never give me anxiety. Cooked salmon is not that good though. Don't ask me why.

So I always eat salmon raw.

# Thread: Clearly, the scales are broken...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 237
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-03T02:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69098>

I've been using the Internet since 1994 and have never kept a blog.. I lurk on fora but rarely post.. I'm outing myself as a lurker here, and have been inspired to actually participate in an attempt to change things (if you always do what you've always done you'll always get what you've always gotten). So here's the back story..

I have always thought I was fat, turns out i was a healthy looking kid, full of muscle, energy and coordination but paralysed with self-consciousness issues because my mum was constantly dieting and telling me what was "flattering" (small child brain: you normally look fat but that dress hides it well) ...and etc. Pretty sure this is a common story so no need to expand further.

After I had a child and stopped breast feeding I began to actually get fat, (and now that I think about it, the experience coincides with staying up all night to talk on irc with people who were interesting...ooh circadian snap!)

I trained as a naturopath and knew all the nutrition things and the chemistry things and the herbs that would help, but they didn't - much. After I realised I didn't want to be responsible for anyone else's health and stopped practicing, I gradually forgot all the good things I knew and got fatter till eventually I went to fat classes to learn how to not be fat. I lost some..

Then I discovered substances that not only made me not hungry, but also made me want to dance a whole lot and feel confident enough to do so.. I lost almost all the weight I needed to.. (Rockstar on the outside, blubbering mess inside was me. Moreover, looking good is kinda irrelevant when your lifestyle is so damn selfish that you destroy all of your good relationships... So I saved my relationships and started to put on weight.)

When I was almost back to my starting weight, I decided it was time to get serious again and started doing all the cw things that, of course, made no difference. So I started to do some proper research..
\-I found out about leptin and heavy lifting, about cheat days and low carb.. It worked for a bit then stopped.
\-I tried subbing meals with shakes to lower my total calories down combined with medication (pretty much slow release speed) to give me energy to exercise.. Worked a bit then stopped.
\-Cut out sugar.. Worked really well, then stopped.
\-Tried adding more exercise.. No effect... Figured I must be destined to just be fat.

I'm glad I tried all those things because it made it much easier to accept the crazy ideas I found here.

I had already realised my body was no good at getting at the stored fat and using it for energy, so regardless of how low I reduced calories, I just couldnâ€™t shift it. Reading the science of why was actually quite liberating, and gave me enough optimism to try just one thing and see how it went.. I found JK's site in mid November, read everything for 2 weeks and began to make some changes in December.

Things I learned in the last 18 months;
Â· about cardio â€“ it is a complete waste of time, and actually makes you weaker and less fit. Exercise should be done no more than 3 times a week for 20 minute sessions and should involve lifting heavy things and not cardio
Â· about sugar â€“ its an addictive poison and breaks your hormones among other things which stops you from being able to utilise stored energy (also causes low immunity and hellish period pains)
Â· about calorie counting â€“ complete bullshit and doesnâ€™t work.

Things I recently learned about from the paleo people:
Â· about saturated fat â€“ your body needs it to function, and if you donâ€™t eat it, you will store every carb you eat because your body thinks thereâ€™s a famine
Â· about vitamin D â€“ thereâ€™s a deficiency epidemic because of sunscreens and sunglasses and deficiency causes depression and low energy sore muscles (and some suggest, skin cancer)
Â· about carbs - you donâ€™t actually need them for energy and your body and brain function better with protein and sat-fat as its main source of energy
Â· about sleep â€“ how you utilise energy and heal is all tied into circadian rhythms. You release melatonin 4 hours after eating when it is dark. Any insulin spike stops the production of melatonin (=insomnia) Melatonin makes you release a bunch of other hormones that make you burn excess fat, stay asleep all night, and heal damaged cells. If you break the melatonin cycle, the hormones donâ€™t happen and metabolism gets progressively brokener.
\- About cold - Cold heals metabolic disorders like insulin resistance and diabetes because it forces you to use other mechanisms than the sugar burning ones. Freezing fat cells causes them to suicide.
(wow, this post is getting epic)
\- leptin reset â€“ retrain your body to burn fat preferentially over carbs.

I began the thing a bit half arsed, with just the giant breakfast and no snacking and mostly paleo eating, but after losing a kilo per day in the first week, I added more elements until by 21st December I was following these 10 rules strictly:

1\. Eat 50grams protein for breakfast every day within 30 minutes of waking
2\. Eat dinner early so that 3-4 hours elapses between eating and sleeping
3\. Never snack, never count calories, and donâ€™t do any cardio.
4\. Eat less than 25 g carbs per day (it goes faster if you can do less than 10g/day because you go Keto, which is awesome for making you good at using stored fat)
5\. Look at no artificial light after the sun goes down each day (I have sexy blue blockers and f.lux) get to bed before midnight and 8 hrs sleep
6\. Lunch and dinner should be strict paleo (seasonal with no grains or legumes at all (and ever again))
7\. Supplement with lots of magnesium, krill oil and vitamin d
8\. Make sure you look at natural sunlight during the day, and get some on your skin
9\. Eat liberal amounts of coconut oil because it cannot be stored as fat and the body can use it for energy immediately
10\. Expose yourself to cold whenever possible

Results after 1 month: (sample size of 1)
Â· In 4 weeks, I dropped a couple of dress sizes, and can see my ribs and 6-pack muscles (yer, not buff yet, but I can see them)
Â· I often sleep all the way through the night
Â· I have energy all day with no dumb slump at 2pm
Â· Skin is tighter, less wrinkles and less flabby skin.
Â· I eat breakfast at 5am and am not hungry for 12 hours without lightheadedness, cravings, tiredness or any of the other cranky stuff that comes with being hungry
Â· I donâ€™t feel deprived by not eating deliciousness when it is around, I am not particularly interested in it any more
Â· I no longer have sore muscles all the time
Â· Less period pain no PMS (although I want a few months to test this to make sure it wasnâ€™t a freak month) and shorter duration
Â· Increased motivation / less depression
Â· Frozen shoulder has mostly sorted itself out
Â· When I get pedicures now, they donâ€™t have to razor off the hard skin on my feet or cut out ingrowing toenails (Used to do this weekly)
Â· No pimples

Things I discovered:
Â· Icepacks on your belly arenâ€™t that uncomfortable, and they make you fall asleep fast afterwards, and they actually do make fat go away without leaving loose skin. (Iâ€™m considering rigging up something to ice my canteen lady arms!)
Â· There is only so much bacon a person can eat in one sitting,
Â· 50 grams of protein is an awful lot of meat.
Â· Bulletproof coffee is focking awesome (see below for recipe)
Â· Once you get past sugar addiction, it isnâ€™t actually that delicious.
Â· You can lose dress sizes and not have your scales change much at all
Â· You can lose weight doing no exercise and sleeping more and eating giant amounts of fat per day (seriously giant amounts â€“ 30% protein, 70%fat most days)
Â· Coconut oil is awesome for removing makeup and moisturising and sunscreen
Â· You donâ€™t need fibre in your diet
Â· I can actually cook.
Â· I donâ€™t burn as easily as I thought I did.
Â· You can make awesome pizza bases with coconut flour
Â· Homemade chocolate is really good
Â· Cold Showers are quite niceâ€¦ once youâ€™re in!

Not so fun things I had to deal with:
Â· Detox the first week (itchy skin, trouble sleeping, skin breakout, scaly legs, nausea) I think this might have been easier for me because of giving up sugar 6 months ago.
Â· Some cooking disasters and wasted food
Â· One day of incredible sleepiness
Â· Everyone predicting that I would get sick, constipated, destroy my liver/kidneys, have no energy.
Â· Bad hangover after the first Wednesday night dinner with my parents (because I ate ice-cream I think)
These things have all gone away now, but I havenâ€™t tested the ice-cream thing to see if I get a hangover. People have stopped telling me Iâ€™m going to â€˜die from my eating disorderâ€™ because I look healthy and they can see itâ€™s working.

Caveman things I plan/hope to do in the nearish future:
Â· Go camping more
Â· Learn to like seafood
Â· Swim in the ocean daily
Â· Graduate from cold showers to ice baths (10 degC water)
Â· Lift heavy things sometimes

What a giant essay... I hope to track things I'm learning and things that work and don't work here just to stop myself getting it arse about in my head and make sure I stay on track. After all, it was reading other people's experiences that helped me put together the pieces, so here's my first step at paying it forward.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T03:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69099>

PS I realise that much of what I wrote about the protocol is incomplete and simplistic, and I am slowly fitting more of the science into my understanding as I read more, but seriously, the first post was long enough, if anyone is reading it for a complete understanding, allow me to point you towards the blog index...

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T05:59:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69108>

Hi Ashryn, WOW, I am thoroughly impressed. You are a meticulous observer. And WELCOME, happy to have a new contributor and such a knowlegable as that.
I did experience a lot of the same. What also stuck was, that I used to have a huge build-up of skin on and around my feet, which needed extensive pedicure to keep in check, with itchy soles and open wounds (scratching) which made me miserable at times as it made walking a chore. Now, My feet just don't have that skin anymore and I haven't even had pedicure in a very long time. I also had some rheumatoid like pain in my left shoulder which is also completely gone. And lots of other little corrections that sorted themselves out.
So I am looking forward to hearing a lot more from you.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T07:25:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69112>

Hi Charlotte, thanks for the warm welcome { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

it's a bit sad that I don't \*need\* pedicures each week now.. Bt I figure ill just swap them for something more enjoyable.. Like a massage.

And talk about opening the floodgates.. From silence to my whole life in a couple of hours... I feel an urgent need to write more things.
Here goes;
When I was studying naturopathy, I learned an adage in homoeopathy that has been on my mind a lot whilst reading people's journals... It is about the direction of healing, they say it proceeds from above downward, from within outward, from more important organs to less important organs and in reverse order of their appearance. It seems to me that this is the case with many many people in here, with old symptoms and illnesses cropping up as they walk their paths. (Luckily I've been reasonably healthy thus far, and did some hardcore healing with homoeopathy way back then so I hope I'll get away with only seeing a brief resurgence of eczema and hay fever... I hope. I have always had tight muscles and joint pain and nothing has really sorted that for any length of time but I am hoping the no grains and ct will get that gone too. )

So, this week I have had some weird things coming back that I haven't had for years, hip pain that my parents used to call 'growing pains' (sorted that with spot icing) and cracks under my little toes (no idea what to do about that, a small voice in my head is telling me that's a candida thing, but I might just give it a few days before I go all mad rush to add more treatments!) as a child they got painted with mercurochrome, but I'm pretty sure that stuff doesn't exist or work on humans any more.

What I really wanted to post about now is the amazingness of cold! How did I never work this out for myself? I've been sitting around reading this website for several hours a day with a giant bag of ice strapped to me... (DH and DD thought I was mad, until they saw the results.. They are both a bit miffed that the freezer is so full of ice that they cant fit any bread in... and then they cant have bread (snigger) thees plenty of meat in the house though.) anyway... I got a cool fat burner vest today and DH asked me about how it works, then said he'd like to give it a try.. Eh heh heh... I'll bring him with me on the journey eventually! Oh.. It might have something to do with the oxytocin therapy that ensues after a cold bath...

It is going to be a challenge to ever manage to get water here below 10C... Even at its coldest the ocean doesn't go below 20C. Also there's a drought here.. Not a chance that I can waste that much water filling the bath every day or spending the money on ice... I have convinced the family that we NEED a pool stat. Until then ice bags and cold baths it will be.

Other odd things I have noticed this week:
Food tastes too salty
I can walk much faster than recently
I don't get tongue tied so much when I try to discuss the science of this stuff with people

Things I need to find solutions to:
I am struggling big time with the BAB- it was always hard but doable, and I pushed through because it is clearly the most important part of the reset.. But this week I physically can't put that much food in me. I am going to have to go looking for recipes that include super high protein foods in small quantities.
I bought fish, but after two abortive efforts at cooking the stuff, (probably shouldn't have forced myself to eat it, but I was working on the premise that I'd get used to it if I ate it) I am having trouble trying it again... Maybe next week.

Argh I have neighbours that sound like they are beating each other to death with their children.. I can't concentrate and have to go eavesdrop. Probably I'll be back later with more burning things to write about..

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T08:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69118>

I love when the flood gates open as that is a fountain of information also important to me. So keep pouring it out.
I have never been a big breakfast eater myself, but I ordered me some good protein from this website here. A little tedious, as I have to have it shipped to my son in NJ and he then needs to forward to me. So once it is here I just make me a shake with that, raw eggs and oil. I can drink a lot and it is a good way to get fluids into my body. Also I eat lots of sashimi in the morning. It is so good when it is fresh and with all the fat it goes down very easy. I find it easier to eat BAB when most of it is raw. The cooked stuff often is too dry for my taste.

Okay, CT. I have a suggestion. I bought me a bunch of hot water bottles at the supermarket for real cheap. Filled them with water and put them in the ice chest. I just take them out and use them. Since we have really cold weather now in Germany, I do not need to cool the water down, but to spot ice, they are perfect. If you fill you tub with water and throw the bottles in it gets pretty cold also. I will have to do this when the weather gets warmer here. This way you do not need to buy ice and after you are done just stick them back into the freezer.

Now I must run, as my cake is waiting to be made. Your neighbours sound like a real challenge. When children are involved it gives me the creeps. Thanks that there is a law now in Germany that you cannot beat up children. I become a fury when I see that happen and I go to any length to stop that. Old memories of my own childhood.

### ashryn — 2013-01-03T10:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69122>

Argh.. The raw thing. I am sure I will get the eventually, but right now, not a chance.. I will keep it in mind that you need less quantity when it's raw... But.. Ugh. Perhaps when I'm closer to optimal eh?

So.. The food thing. The weight thing..
I started tracking with paleo track because it has all the cool stuff like how many micronutrients in the foods you track, and also as a way to calculate 50g protein, so I really like it, except that I suspect the quality of food between the states and Australia is quite different, also there's foods I don't know the American term for, which is a bit of a pain, not to mention trying to constantly convert measurements between imperial and metric.. All that was ok, but the biggest thing that was doing my head in is knowing the number of calories I am consuming per day.. I am not counting intentionally, but it counts them for me, and I see that I'm up to 2500 or more and freak out. So I have stopped that for a while.. Though I did also learn that I am regularly not getting enough vitamin a or k - not even half of the RDI... So I have been adding in some herbs and baby spinach when it grows in my garden... I also have some spring onions ready to add, so that should sort out those two. Next multivitamin I buy will hopefully include them too.

I'm going to see my doctor next week to convince her to give me all the tests that exist.. My plan is to tell her I'm doing a radical change to my diet and I want a baseline to start from so that I can compare in 6 months and make sure everything is ok.. Pretty sure she will play ball, it isn't her money after all..

The first couple of weeks I was weighing myself daily and that was happy times for the first week at least, then the scales stopped moving. Each day I'd get up, look at the changes going on in my BC and be really excited to see how many more kilos I had lost.. But my scales are clearly broken because they haven't moved even though I've had to dig out old smaller clothes because my others were hanging on me. So my first journalling goal is to open up the "don't be ridiculous" suitcase of clothes in my garage and wear things from it

Ok, so I should record the food here too I suppose... (Though it seems all kinds of obsessive and I want to avoid being so focused on food all the time, I guess it could be valuable to track potential issues, so I'll try)
B: 3 chicken thighs crumbed with coconut flour, turmeric, sesame seed, almond and hazelnut meal fried in co, Bulletproof coffee, enough supplements to make me rattle washed down with water and lemon juice.
L: nothing
D: avocado and tomato salad with lemon juice avocado oil and black pepper and two GF lamb chops
Supplements: milk thistle, women's multivitamin, vitamin d, magnesium, wild salmon oil, glucosamine

Gawd.. That's really enough from me for one day..

I hope you don't mind but I want to email your first post to my sister (and now that I think about, my husband) who is still caught up in the "fat will kill you" syndrome and I can hear her saying to herself when I describe what I'm doing "OMG she's going to die!" ### ashryn — 2013-01-03T21:49:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69246>

Ha ha ha Thor!
Diane, I get the science.. But i dont have the words to explain it (what? no words... surely this cant be true i hear you say) and i think translating the science into something practical makes it a whole lot easier to follow and explain to people in a non-evangelical way... the science reminds me of things I learned back in hippie school eg the vitamin d and sunglasses thing- I knew it years ago, and lived by it then gradually forgot about it as I moved away from being a hippie... Also I recently read a brilliant book called the brain that changes itself ( normandoidge.com/normandoidge.com/MAIN.html ) that turns out to be quite old but when I read it I thought it was new.. It started me thinking about the science again, and neuroscience... Actually coursera has a neuroscience 101 course starting this month that I am enrolled in... So like most other things in my life, my science is a bit dusty, but I'm going to give it a bit of a rub and see if I can't start talking the science talk

Darleen, thanks for the hate

I must confess it was your journal I read first all the way through that made me out my lurking self, so thanks.. It might be that I just wanted to talk to someone else that has a rock star connection... For the last 5 years I have been the state editor of a music website called fasterlouder.com.au I just recently gave it up and I think I'm having withdrawals finally... Though I can play several instruments badly, I love to write music reviews and take photos of bands rocking out, and I love finding new local bands before the radio stations do.. Can I ask what was the name of your band? And did you do originals that are recorded somewhere that I can listen to?

DH is in a band and fills my house with original music that is awesome, and I'd love to dust off my vocal chords or an instrument and join him, but... Too shy. Maybe another goal for optimal eh?

Of course you can send my first post to your sister if you think it will help... I suspect it won't since people can only come to this stuff on their own and when they are ready... My approach after spending many years trying to convince people the truth of things is just to be the example.. Answer questions when they ask, but not too fully, and I always caveat with 'this is my experiment... Lets see how it works for me before you try it..' Of course all my people are stubborn and more likely to try something if I tell them not to \*evil grin\*

How I lost weight so fast? Dunno.. I wasn't all that strict the first week, so I suspect it was water weight that I didn't know I had. Add to that the low to no fat diet I have eaten all my life which means I am unable to use carbs as energy, and it makes a good case for getting into keto and staying there because then my body just has to find another source of energy. I must admit the lack of vegetables is making my brain shriek at me that this isn't healthy but I figure I'll just go with it for now and make sure my vitamins and minerals come from supplements until I get reset.

The only dietary omission I struggle with is chocolate.. I used to eat it daily, but it isn't so hard now.. After Xmas our pantry is pretty much made of chocolate, but I haven't gone there. A couple of times after my bab I've thought a piece might be nice, then I think some more and just don't go there.. I do make bulletproof mocha about once a week, (Espresso, two tbsp butter, 1/4cup CO with a teaspoon of raw cacao powder) but after reading the travails of many people on this site who can't lose weight, it seems to me that the common thread in their stories is eating cacao, so I don't go there often.

One other thing about the protocol that I love more than anything else is the stricture on no exercise... w00t! I hate exercise for exercise's sake. Don't get me wrong, I love being active when there is a reason, but it has to be a fun/life reason, not a get thin reason.

Today's food so far totals 2900 calories.. (Damn you paleotrack) that's just the BAB! The only thing that makes this slightly ok is that I won't eat much else today but still..
2 lamb chops (scooped the marrow out of the bones too.. Eww but apparently it's good for me so whatevs)
1 chicken thigh same as yesterday
2 eggs fried in CO
Assorted herbs and things
\=51g protein, 302g fat, 4g carbs... That's 7% protein, 93%fat and 1% carb.. How this isn't going to kill me or make me put on weight I have no idea..

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T21:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69381>

Yesterday's lunch was 2 lamb chops, and dinner was some Greek yoghurt with 5grapes a passionfruit and a sliced peach
Went to play board games with friends last nit and drank wine and didn't get to sleep till midnight, but still woke at 5.30am tried to go back to sleep but got hungry!

Today's BAB included 250g steak, 2 strips of bacon, 2 eggs and a handful of mushrooms all cooked in coconut oil with turmeric. Pus a bulletproof coffee. No idea what the numbers are I'll stick it into paleotrack later, it is definitely 50g protein though.

I recently increased my dose of magnesium and have been doing 4 or more hours of CT in multiple sessions and I appear to be getting a bit of detox from my lungs this morning.

I am heading to the beach for a long hard soak in not so cold water shortly.
Sunburn from my idiotic nude sunbathing effort yesterday seems worse this morning despite slathering on CO all day yesterday. I'm going to try tea bags if the beach doesn't soothe it. (Imagine the look on DH's face when I carry not only ice upstairs to my cold bath but a box of tea bags as well!)

### endless — 2013-01-04T21:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69384>

4 HOURS of CT??? Wow. I am impressed...I feel accomplished if I manage to fit in 30 minutes!

### ashryn — 2013-01-05T07:29:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69401>

Endless: Ahh yeah but it isn't hardcore CT like others around the place.. Just spot icing and baths with tap temp water, which feels warm to me (summer here)
When we got to the beach today, everyone was standing on the shore and the shark warning siren was going off so we watched for a while hoping they would chase it away.. Eventually they turned off the siren and we went in (but not too far out, DD would never forgive me if I got eaten by a shark) but then they turned the siren on again and we were advised to go no more than ankle deep. Being as how ankle CT is sub-optimal, we decided to drive down the road to the kids and nana beach (no surf, protected by a reef, no rips or blue holes) and after parking in the wrong car park and picking our way like mountain goats through the rocks for 15 mins we spent a pleasant couple of hours soaking, but not getting cold unfortunately.

My sunburn damaged skin is quite painful, especially on the bit of my belly where the stretch marks from pregnancy are worst, which I guess makes sense.. The skin is very thin and papery there so it would damage more easily. I couldn't work out how to keep enough cold tea bags balanced there, so I just chucked 20 bags into the bath and had a bath in cold tea.. CTea \*nyuk nyuk\* No idea if it helped.. Slathered myself in CO afterwards and a couple of times since.. Not sure if its my skin or my clothes absorbing it so fast..

Speaking of skin, my skin seems very dry in general the last couple of days, especially on my hands.. I used to say I had old lady hands, but have been quite happy with how they have been firming up lately, but now the skin seems super thin - I probably wouldn't have noticed except that I got a small scratch on one hand somehow and even though it is shallow, the skin is all inflamed and very red.. I've been keeping it soft with CO but it seems to soak in really fast.

What with coating myself and my food and 'oil pulling' with coconut oil every day, I am really getting through it fast. I did some research to find someone closer to Australia that makes it because of the high carbon cost of transporting it to here from Fiji and I found a company called niugini organics that supports coconut growers in Papua new guinea, i bought some in bulk a couple weeks ago and am onto the second last jar already!

Weighed myself today, no scale movement still, disheartening even though I know they aren't a reliable guide. I took no measurements when I started so all I have to go by are my clothes.. I know I KNOW it doesn't matter.. But it does to me. Today I ate 2 sausages for lunch and some more lamb chops for dinner with stir fried veg and butter.. I shouldn't have eaten the sausages because I really wasn't hungry and wasn't real hungry at 5 when I aim to eat dinner.. So I ate dinner anyway since its a circadian thing and the vagus nerve must be stretched, but I will go back to not bothering with lunch from now on.

I have taken to freezing my wild salmon oil to hopefully prevent it from going rancid.. And I took an extra multivitamin at dinner cos I'm concerned that chronic vit K and A deficiency might be contributing to my skin's current condition.. Prolly need more vit c as well - guess I'm off to the shops tomorrow to get those things sorted..

I am wondering about light cycles in summer because the sky starts to get light here at 4.30 am and we're lucky if we have full dark by 8.30pm at night.. I like the idea of watching the sun rise and set to get the natural light in my brain, but how does that square with needing 4 hours of dark for melatonin secretion, and getting a long enough sleep? (When I'm working I have to get up at 5am, so I wake up with the sun anyway)

I am also wondering how to increase the intensity of my CT (economically) I have the cool fat burner on over a damp t-shirt and 6 kilos of ice packs strapped to my belly right now, and though I feel numb, and it takes about an hour for the iced areas to go back to normal, I don't really feel that shivery cold or have goosebumps... Am I getting cold enough? How can I ger MOAR COLD!?

Ahh that's enough..I have to find some motivation to do the computer work that's waiting for me upstairs.. But it is just so nice to read everyone's stories in the lounge room on my iPad.. Feeling super lazy and inexplicably sad this evening.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-05T09:19:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69404>

Hi Ashryn, so sorry to hear you are sad today. When I read your last post, I thought, wow it would be nice to be on your part of the planet now. I just cannot get warm today. Even CT was a meager event this morning. I just could not tolerate the cold, so after 5 minutes I got out. Today is my first day after over 6 weeks of 500 kacal on hCG and all last week I have been looking forward to my first BAB. Well, was not hungry at all and all I ate was a bowl of bone broth which has been cooking since thursday with some bone marrow and meat. Well, come to think of it. I was so busy yesterday that I forgot to eat. When I remembered, it was already past 5 in the evening, and I did not want to eat so late, so I did not eat anything all day. As a result I could not go to sleep until after 3 in the morning and I woke up early nevertheless, so maybe that is why I am so cold today.
Then when cooking (I am expecting visit today and tomorrow) I took three bites out of a raw milk cheese and 15 minutes later I had a big pressure on my liver. Guess, must get used to the fat slowly. How you can eat so much is beyond me. 2900 kcal just for breakfast, wow. I realize it is easy when you are eating a lot of fat but still. I have to work up to that.
I got up early this morning and went to the quail farm to get eggs and looking at the little quails. How nic,

I think the forum sprite ate some of your post... What were you thinking about the quails?

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-05T11:51:22+0200

I just want to say hi, and thank you SO much for all that you wrote! Outstanding! I'm bookmarking your thread to fully digest later. I actually have two (2 !!! ) family members who are moving from SAD to Paleo as we speak, and I'm going to point them to your thread.

### diane — 2013-01-05T13:11:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69444>

Hi Ashryn. Here (in Canada) I usually get really dry skin in the winter - and this is pretty much the first time that I haven't. The naturopath had me adding a lot of Omega 3 and fish oil for my dry skin, and it didn't do a thing for me. I figured that something wasn't processing right which was accounting for the dry skin.

Try adding seafood - since I've increased my seafood I haven't had dry skin. I usually eat beef for breakfast then try to make lunch and dinner seafood meals. I may eat other meat for dinner a couple of times a week, but mainly seafood.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T14:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69450>

OOOH goody! A fellow rock enthusiast. You are lucky to have so much music still in your life. My DH cannot carry a tune in a bucket nor can he manage to move with the beat, LOL. when he used to dance I swore he looked like Ichabod Crane. And he calls ME "grace," LOL.

Our band was named The Legal Limit. Guitar, keyboards, bass, drums, flute/sax and vocals. This is one of those things that really chopped me off at the knees ... the guitarist (a\*\*wipe misogynistic pr\*\*\* that he was and probably still is) broke the band up on purpose just to get rid of me. He figured he could get everyone back together and off they'd go w/o me. Too bad for him the others saw right through what he was doing. It was a real shame because we were just getting some regional notice and could have done well.

No we hadn't gotten to doing our own material yet. But I was starting to dip my toes into songwriting which is probably why he did what he did. He had evidently written some songs and entered a contest or two and had gotten nowhere. Did I already call him an a\*\*wipe? Let me do it again. I let that rejection ruin my love of music for a very long time. It just hurt to even go there. It was like getting a divorce times 5. Have I mentioned I do not handle rejection very well?

Eventually I moved past it. Music may never rule my life like it once did ... I think I have upwards of 400 lp's and at least that many CD's and then there's the stuff I've bought on iTunes and now Spotify. Did I mention I was also a DJ for a local radio station for several years? Yeah, that too. I had "Music is life is music" engraved on my first iPod.

Anyway, returning you now to YOUR journey ...

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T05:40:37+0200

> **diane said**
>
> Hi Ashryn. Here (in Canada) I usually get really dry skin in the winter - and this is pretty much the first time that I haven't. The naturopath had me adding a lot of Omega 3 and fish oil for my dry skin, and it didn't do a thing for me. I figured that something wasn't processing right which was accounting for the dry skin.
> Try adding seafood - since I've increased my seafood I haven't had dry skin. I usually eat beef for breakfast then try to make lunch and dinner seafood meals. I may eat other meat for dinner a couple of times a week, but mainly seafood.

Hi Diane,
Based on what I now understand about omega 3s (see giant treatise in other thread where I asked if fish oil sups were always bad and Dr K said go and read the blog and work it out yourself) I have been adding too much to my diet, so it's interesting your naturopath had you omit for dry skin.. I suspect it was more to balance ratios than to add more o3. The other thing I learned about that is that your skin needs more o6 for the inflammatory-->healing response in the case of tissue trauma.. I was whining about a scratch on my hand yesterday, and I'm going to taks a guess that I didn't haven the right ratio of o6 for my body to deal with it effectively, hence the extra pain and swelling and slow time to form a scab..
Huh. I love it when something I learn can be directly related to me.. Reminds me that I AM the centre of the universe after all \*grin\*

I hate seafood! But I am trying to acquire a taste for it.. Any and all recipes that trick me into eating seafood without realising it would be gratefully accepted

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T06:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69564>

Episode four: In which ashryn whines a whole lot about fish, not being able to cook, or get cold...

I am sitting in the lounge with the air on as low as it will go, belly ice on and ice vest on too. Unfortunately it's stupidly hot so all that ice does is keep me comfortable.. Sigh. Stepped up the process of having a pool installed (ie. hassled DH to pester the dude who is supposed to give us a quote) at this rate I'll have one just in time for the cold weather.

Today I got up late because the sky was overcast which was awesome, then we went to the nana beach again because DH was freaked about the sharks at our normal surfing beach (he said there were 5 of them about 3 metres long, but I figure.. The ocean is where sharks live, right?)

Anyway.. The clouds made it feel quite cold which was a nice change. We stayed for a couple of hours. I am really looking forward to the chance to immerse myself in actual cold water someday.

BAB was leftover meats and BPC pretty uninspiring but it had to be eaten and now it is. Not hungry for lunch, and dinner happened around 5pm... I ATE FISH! Ok, so it was a couple of little tins of tuna in chilli and oil with a handful of spinach and cheese.. Not too bad. I got a bit sick of it after about the first tin (95g) but it didn't make me feel ill, so maybe we can call that progress.. I have a tin of smoked oysters in the pantry but so far haven't been able to bring myself to try them.

I do realise that fresh is better, but fresh requires prep and knowledge, neither of which I have yet, and the smell of cooking fish makes me lose my appetite completely so I'll wait till I am a bit used to it first. I have a tin of salmon in the pantry too - how does one eat that? What's good to mix it with?

I went on a shopping expedition to find DHEA and k2 and a better multivitamin preferably with A and iodine, but not much luck there.. I'm not sure we can buy DHEA in Australia, and the health food lady said you can't get vitamin K here either. When she asked me why I wanted it I forgot what it was for.. So I said I was deficient in it. She was really asking because she didn't know it was used for anything other than to prevent phenylketonuria in newborns (I think) when I didn't give her a good answer she went and dug up her nutrition book and looked up what it was for hahah.

So anyway, I bought dried herbs, kale, spinach, spring onions and broccoli hopefully they will do the vit k trick. Will have to check with paleotrack later to make sure I remembered the right foods to buy. I also found some wakeme, but it tastes very fishy and salty.. Anyone got any tips for hiding that stuff in food?

Maybe I'll just chow down on some fresh seaweed at the beach (seriously.. I could probably do that, except are there any poisonous seaweeds?)

I had my first go at deciphering a blog post today and putting it into a context I could understand. I put it in the nutrition forum http://www.jackkruse.com/forum-2/optimal-nutrition/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad/ hopefully I understood it right. It made me realise how much of the blogs I really haven't taken in, and I might have to start at the beginning and summarise them all (dust off my anatomy and physiology and my organic chemistry and see if I can make it make enough sense that I can explain it back to someone.)

Still haven't found the motivation to go work on the computer upstairs yet. Will need to get onto that tonight. I also need to come up with a plan for my BAB tomorrow since I have finished all the leftovers. Perhaps I'll have another go at fish. It's whiting, how do I cook it? Last time I just fried it in butter but it was too salty and fishy.. And I think I cooked it too long. Any fish cooking experts reading this? (Not you Inger, I'm not brave enough for raw yet!

) I will be one day, I promise.

Also.. How the hell do I prepare kale? Man, I'm such a retard in the kitchen.

Hrm. Not a totally successful day in all.. Seems like the beach was the only thing that went well. Maybe I'll suggest we go again when DH gets home from band practice.

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-06T11:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69587>

From one seafood-hater to another, all I can say is, I hear you. I did find a recipe for coconut shrimp (coconut flour, shredded coconut and egg) that sounds good, as a way to hide the taste of the shrimp! I also made a lemon-butter sauce that I froze & haven't used yet that sounds great. Although Dr. K. says not to eat sat. fat (i.e. butter) with seafood....sigh. Next time, I'll make it with Ghee.

I have to force-feed myself my seafood. I buy frozen shrimp, and then I let them thaw in the fridge during the day. When I come home, either I eat them or throw them out. Since the frugal side of me can't stand wasting food, I. Eat. It. I still don't love it, but down the hatch it goes. If I don't thaw it out, it will just stay in the freezer forever.

As to preparing kale, the only way I like it is sauteed in olive oil & garlic. Actually kind of spinach-y this way. Just wash the entire bunch, put it in a pan, sautee till it wilts. It will reduce to about 1/3 the physical size, and it's pretty good this way. Or you can sneak it in salads.

Re: the dry skin. Being epiPaleo, I've had significant improvement. Something else I've done is, instead of lotions and chapsticks, I make my own, which is so much better. Beeswax pellets, coconut oil, and cocoa butter, melted together and formed into little bars (I actually poured into muffin cups). I found the recipe on a coconut oil site; if you want, I can dig it up. Way more moisturizing and no harmful ingredients.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T11:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69590>

How can you NOT like fish???? We're having mussels and shrimp tonight or as Vickie says, muscles and shrimp.

Canned (hopefully wild caught) salmon ... try this is it so good. Take however much thick sliced bacon you like (I use 3 slices), slice it up into tiny bits and sautee until crisp. You can add in a bit of minced onion if you like. When ready, drain the can of salmon and toss it in (keep heat low so moisture/fat spatters don't hit you) and stir until warm. Pile on your breakfast plate and chow down. EVERYthing is better with bacon.

As to the band, actually I did drag bass player off to Seattle with me (which at the time was a hotbed of rock music) to try and put another band together. Didn't work out. He chose to sit on his ass all day long, do nothing to help locate potential band mates while I, busy little bee that I was, found a temp job during the day and spent my evenings trying to figure out where all the musicians were hiding. I finally gave up because of that and the fact I missed DH and came back home. Oh well.

Oh yeah, and I'm with your hubs on the sharks and the beach thing. Of course, the ocean kinda scares me anyway it is just so overwhelming. I prefer the mountains. Nice and stable and big and strong ... always at your back (or front or side depending on how they and you are oriented).

don't know how you turn off auto-correct on the iPad. I don't use for surfing or email. That's what my computer is for. And I only have the wi-fi version anyway. I don't run around much so saw no need for the 4G version. Mine only gets used at home.

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T21:29:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69674>

Episode 5: in which fish plays a starring role...

Interesting... Fish has lotsa protein but few calories... Can someone remind me what fat to protein ratio we're aiming for? I never thought i'd say this, but i'm not sure how to get more fat... (There's no way i can drink it out of the pan, so don't suggest it!) current ratio is 70% protein to 30%fat...
I ate fish for bab this morning.. I didn't love it, but i ate it. I actually might have overdone the coating, but i was trying to hide the taste.
Defrosted two whiting fillets in the fridge last night, then dipped them in egg and a coating of almond, hazelnut meal, garlic and sesame seeds with some italian mixed herbs and black pepper. Cooked them in some unsalted butter because it had to be used up. I'll get some lard (bacon fat) next time i brave the grocery store and use that in future. Served it with a cup of baby spinach, some cheese and lemon juice with a couple of chunks of polish sausage to make up the protein quotient. Apparently all that was just over 1000 calories but 75g protein so it will be interesting to see if it takes me through to dinner.

Interestingly, eating fish and the green things gets the problem vitamins up, but leaves me low in calcium iron and zinc.. Getting some bacon in me at dinner should sort those out i guess.

Without salmon oil supps my omega 6-3 ratio is 3:1 not too bad i think. Will keep a close eye on my skin and that stupid cut on my hand to see how it is healing.. Added coenzymeQ10, vitamin A and bioC to the daily pharmacy i scarf down, but it looks like i wont need vit A every day if i keep eating the green stuff. I planted the spring onion ends in the garden so i have a steady supply of them in future.

Had some detox stuff this morning, nothing too serious, but a good sign i think, and my rings are falling off my fingers again which makes me happy and sad at the same time. (Actually it makes me want to jump on the scales.. Just to see.. But i shall endeavour to resist!)

CT is cold today, i can feel the fat cells suiciding as i type! It is probably due to the overcast sky today, i am able to get the room cold for a change.

This is my last week of holidays which is quite sad, but it means i need to make that appointment with my doc for the blood tests.. I don't know why i'm procrastinating.. Surely it will be interesting to know... But what if they find something scary? I know.. If they do, then it's better to know early so i can fix it.. But still i procrastinate. JUST DO IT WOMAN!

In other news, my nude sunbathing disaster of a few days ago appears to have resulted in sunburned butt too because today it's peeling! It didn't hurt, so it didn't get the coconut oil love that my belly got, and i don't make a habit of checking out my naked arse in the mirror these days, so i didn't notice, but hahaha! So not telling DH. Belly isn't peeling yet, though it looked like it was going to for a while, the coconut love might have saved it.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T00:30:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69704>

\_\_Summary of the post leptin reset eating template:\_\_

\*\*I doubt i am reset yet, but in order to be ready when i am, i thought i would summarise the plan for myself so i have it straight in my head. Explaining something back to a person seems to be the best way for me to understand things, so here goes.\*\*

The reset takes 6-8 weeks for most people, and is designed to bypass the leptin system that is currently broken and rewire the hypothalamus to deal with food in another way. It takes 6 weeks of training the hypothalamus to work with the vagus nerve signals and to figure out what to do with the food and cravings. Essentially, the LRx teaches the brain to associate the time of the day with the timing of meals, and to work out the best way to apportion the nutrients to the body. Because it hasn't used this method since we were tiny, it doesn't make the mistakes our leptin receptor pathway does by storing everything. We are initially trained to link light and activity cycles and meal times as foetuses so the ability is innate in our biology, which means that even if you or your mother broke your leptin system, it can be reset as an adult.

\*\*\*Look for the following signs that you are reset:\*\*\*

\*Weight loss, or change in body comp- clothes fit differently
\*Sweat differently- more or less depending on your issues.
\*Increased energy and improved recovery from exercise
\*No hunger or cravings
\*Waking up refreshed

When you see these signs you may add some high intensity interval training if you wish..

NOTES ABOUT WHY:
The way the mother wakes, eats and sleeps during pregnancy trains the hypocretin neurones prior to the leptin receptor developing, and so ultradian rhythms control things like "hormonal release, heart rate, thermoregulation, urination, bowel activity, nostril dilation and appetite."
The body rhythmically releases peptides that stimulate or suppress appetite in time with the cycle of the mother's body. If the mother doesn't sleep well during pregnancy, the foetus has to work out what the time is based on the hormones being released by the mother. (Many of the hormone issues we struggle with as adults are directly tied to what kind of signals were sent from mum during gestation. In particular, these hormones dictate how our leptin receptor will function.) After birth, we have no need of these circuits since we can work out day and night and feeding times for ourselves, so the pathways go dormant, but they don't disappear.

Since the leptin receptor in the hypothalamus is more efficient at interpreting circadian cycles and electrons that arrive at meal times, babies quickly start using this pathway instead. It all starts when the baby gets leptin from colostrum in mothers milk. The receptor is trained by feeding and sleep cycles by repetition (fire/wire)

This all works very well unless the receptor has to deal with inflammation, and then it tries to downregulate by paying less attention to the leptin coming from our food.. This makes us not efficient fat burners but good fat storers (which leads to further inflammation and a whole vicious cycle - leptin resistance)

POST RESET RULES:
Once the leptin receptor is retrained, we keep it functioning properly by eating a low inflammatory diet (paleo template) high in fat moderate protein and low carbs. Some people can go as high as 30% carbs, but YMMV.

\*Breakfast still has to be eaten within 30 minutes of rising and should include 50% of the carbs you will eat that day, plus 25g protein and 25-30g of fat.
\*THOU SHALT NEVER MISS BREAKFAST (bf starts the circadian rhythm for the secretion of gastric acid which we will need later in the day to deal with body comp.)
\*Don't work out before bf because it raises cortisol and breaks the circadian cycle.
\*Be active between 9am and 4pm, look at the sun, walk around and do stuff. Sitting down is bad.
\*if you are hungry at lunch, you can eat 25% of your carbs for that day, skip it if you want.
\*if you want to work out you should do it between 1pm and 5pm... In the sun.
\*Dinner should be 45mins after your afternoon workout (if you did one) and 4hours before you will sleep. Done by 8pm at the latest. (Eating within 45 minutes of a workout triples the amount of protein you make so it gets you 'ripped' faster. If you do it in cold, not only do you build muscle, but you burn fat at an astounding rate.)
\*Dinner should have 25-75g protein, any carbs left over and the rest fats. The best fats for dinner are coconut oil, ghee, pastured butter, and bacon fat because they kill the appetite.
\*Sleep by 11pm at the latest, earlier in autumn and winter. Get 7-8 hours a night and try to wake without the alarm.

WHAT ELSE?
\*If active drop all supplements.. You shouldnt need them with proper eating. If not active, keep CQQ and vitamin d.
\*You can do intermittent fasting now if you want so long as you never miss breakfast.

\*High protein is now eaten at dinner because thats when we're ready to use it. The BAB was just for retraining our brains.
\*Carb intake and choice should follow the seasons.. More carb in summer, less in winter.
\*Sleep is imperative, because thats when we get rid of excess fat and calories. We do this using T3, leptin and uncoupling proteins.
\*Not enough sleep leads to reduced DHEA levels and reduced Growth Hormone release which causes high cortisol (and carb cravings)

Of course some of this has now been updated by the epi paleo template, augmented by cold thermogenisis, but its good to start from somewhere....

Taken from blog post here http://www.jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next/

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T06:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69723>

Insulin inhibits lipolysis and stimulates lipogenesis. If you have a lot of insulin floating around, you can't burn fat efficiently because insulin blocks hormone sensitive lipase (no cleavage of fatty acid esters can occur). Insulin increases lipogenesis by increasing the concentration of malonyl-CoA, a substrate required for fatty acid synthesis. Too much insulin signals a "fed" state to the brain and fat storage occurs easily. Doesn't matter if the insulin is "spiked" by protein or carbs, the result is the sameâ€¦you store calories as fat.

Stolen from kathylu...

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T09:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69727>

Episode 5-1 redux in which ashryn crows about having a good day with fish and CT...

Grr.. Eating more fish tips my omga 6-3 ratio the other way.. Now i am not getting enough o6... Where do we get them from?
Not much on the list that i actually eat... Sunflower seeds maybe i could add to something pine nuts, chicken, sesame seeds, hrm..

I was right about not enough fat in the fish breakfast, I was hungry for lunch today, but I ate a little tin of tuna with lime and chilli and ginger with the oil it came in, and that sorted me till dinner. Also, I LIKED IT! Tomorrow's breakfast is going to be leftover burgers I made with spinach and an avocado but i'll take out the fish and try to have another go at cooking it well.

Heading to the shops to get more tuna tins, scallops and bacon fat plus bacon to wrap the scallops in. I'll get \*fish adapted\* by hook or by crook! (See what i did there?)

I wrote this somewhere else, but I shall record it here too because i'm very pleased with how things worked out today.. Not only did morning CT get me cold, but i wrote a summary of the post reset plan and feel like I understood it... Then, I managed to sync up exercise in the sun with CT and the right timing for exercise (swimming was exercise today because the current was strong and the water over my head 90% of the time) as well as eating in the 45 minute window after exercise that maximises the muscle build and the fat burn!
It may even be possible for me to do this all week too!

In other news, after looking closely at the reset signs, I think I might be nearly there. I'd just like to be able to sleep the whole night through without getting up to pee at 2am. I have been sleeping through a couple of times in a week, but i really really wake feeling refreshed when I don't wake up compared to waking 'refreshed enough' also, when I sleep all the way through I remember my dreams which is exciting for me since I rarely do that. It is hard to break myself of the habit of drinking water just before bed.

I'm not sure what the change in sweating pattern is supposed to be... Sweating is for cooling us down, so we should sweat when it is hot and when we exercise enough to make us hot... Now, I walked to my parents house last week and usually by half way there i'm tired and my feets hurt. Normally DD walks much faster than me but this time I walked faster than either of them.. But it didnt seem like I was working harder than usual, and I wasnt tired half way. When i got there mum said my face was pink, and I felt hot, but I cooled down quickly and didn't notice that I was sweating at all. Its a change.. But I don't know if its the change i'm looking for.

Arg, thats enough.. God i ramble on.

I haven't had cravings for anything except chocolate for well over six months, since i quit sugar, but i have noticed that once ive eaten, i'm done and am not interested in more food even if it is sweets of some kind. I think that counts as appetite has gone? I am still hungry within 5 mins or waking up. And was hungry at lunch today, as i said before.. So not sure about that one...

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T20:16:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69798>

From tracking my foods... Theres a site called paleo track where you enter all the things you eat and it tells you what micronutrients are in the foods. Problem is that it also tells you the calories and that freaks me out a bit, but its good for deciding what to supplement.

Actually after i wrote the last post it occured to me that i have to consider the rationAND the amount.. If i aim for 500g of omega 3, it means i need 1000g of omega 6... I dont know where to get that much o6 with keto paleo diet. It is possible that paleo track isnt all that accurate though, because tuna has some o6 in it, but paleotrack doesnt show it.

### ashryn — 2013-01-08T09:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69842>

Well, I wasnt going to bother to update today, since not much has changed... Nothing on the scales, yes.. I caved again. Stress about going back to work next week and wanting to have a number to give them when they ask what i have been doing and react in a horrified way to the no carbs revelation.. Ahh well. I am getting impatient for something else to change... I could use some dramatic improvement to keep me motivated to do even better.

Got a haircut today because it has been growing really fast due to the vitamin D I've been getting. The hairdresser was a competitive bodybuilder, so I asked how she eats.. She said high protein low carb, and mentioned a 'protein muffin' that she makes.. I asked what is in it (always looking for ways to get the BAB in) and it was full of carbs! Banana, apple juice, oats... And when I said, I didn't need her to write down the recipe because that is more carbs than I eat in a week and more grains than I've eaten in 6weeks.. She was horrified. Lecturing ensued.

\*sigh\* when I am looking healthy and svelt, i'll get into these conversations with random strangers for sure.. But right now, when I am not finished yet, I don't think i'm a great advertisement for the protocols.

I can't remember who said it on another page, but people do get REALLY emotionally attached to their carbs; it is EXACTLY like an addiction response.

Got most of the way towards making a new CT vest with belly strap, but the icepack shop wasnt open, so thats on hold. ( hopefully it wont be another never to be completed craft project)

In good CT news, dude is coming round tomorrow to valuate the house so we can remortgage for the pool.. Yay!

In good circadian rhythm news, I slept all the way through the night last night and BOUNCED out of bed this morning. Not sure if it was all the swimmingIi did yesterday, the not drinking water before bed, inverting the colours on the ipad so it is white text on black (btw, your avatars look very funny as negative images) or the tap temp cold bath just before sleeping, or if it is a reset sign.. Whatever, I'll take it, and any more where it came from

Went again to the beach this arvo, it wasnt as nice as it has been, but still, I got some cold on.. I even munched on a bit of seaweed, but was too chicken to swallow it.. Maybe when i've done some more research on it.. (Anyone know if you can just harvest seaweed straight out of the ocean as it floats by? Or do you have to cut it off the plant?

I ate tuna and salmon today, bought some lard to cook my next whiting in, couldn't find any scallops though. I liked the tuna, didnt like the salmon.. Then I poured lemon juice on it and managed to finish it.. This fish eating thing makes me get hungry at lunch though, which confuses me. Can someone point me at the blog that will explain how to eat fish and reduce hunger?

Well.. For someone that had not much to say, i said a lot..

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T11:48:43+0200
