### nonchalant — 2013-01-08T12:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69862>

Ashryn, about your 03/06 ratio, you don't have to have your daily ratios balance. If you are like me, your body stored lots and lots of oxidized 06's from the various vegetable oils you ate for years trying to follow CW. I think it takes a year or two to really change your body's stored ratios. Your sunburn is probably because of these excess low-quality 06's. Since it is summer for you, I think you should eat all the 03's you can. Then go for more quality 06's in the autumn and winter.

Also, about the seafood being less filling, Dr. K has mentioned that the body knows the value of the DHA O3's in seafood, and will shuttle these important nutrients to the brain and elsewhere as needed. Unless you have been eating seafood for a while, the body will not waste it on mere fuel. I find seafood quite satiating now, but it wasn't earlier.

### diane — 2013-01-08T13:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69873>

I think that a few people eat their saturated fat for BAB (along with other fat), which then makes the seafood seem more filling later on in the day. That's what I've been doing - although I'm good with seafood for breakfast as well. If you're still hungry, I would say add fat!

### Linz — 2013-01-08T14:14:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69876>

Nonchalant, I think CT speeds up improvements in 06/3 ratio. I had lots of bad O6s from vegetable oil too.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-69900>

I'm with Diane ... loads of sat fat for breakfast. for weeks I had bacon and salmon then ran out of the canned salmon so now it's bacon and eggs with ALL the bacon fat. I'd lick the pan but it's too hot, LOL.

I'm glad someone else piped up about trying to figure your ratios from your daily food intake. Just off the top of my head that didn't seem right but I hesitate to just jump in and offer opinions on things I'm not real clear on.

What I'm trying to do is to just let my body guide me on what I want and need. I've been doing the leptin rx for a year now (you know the drill, BAB within 30 minutes of rising, no snacking, yadda yadda) and it's becoming almost second nature. Which is why, for the last couple of months, I have just gobbled up the fat and didn't worry about it. I don't WANT veggies I want fat and a little protein. OKeydokey. That's what I eat. I don't measure, don't think, just eat what I want until I don't want anymore. See, I have a little issue with overthinking things. (stop laughing) It's not easy just letting it be but it's a great way to live!

### ashryn — 2013-01-08T22:11:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70004>

\*\*\*Diane says - If you're still hungry, I would say add fat!\*\*\*

Hahaha, thats what everyone says! I tried, but it seems that eating a low fat diet all my life has left me with no real understanding of what fat is good to eat -weird huh? I drained the first tin of tuna I ate, then when it didn't fill me up I stopped draining them so I get some oils from that, and I started adding a spinach and avocado salad with EVavocado oil. Mainly because short of chugging the oil straight from the bottle I didn't know how to get it in me!

So I was eating sat fat for BAB which was fine- didnt want lunch, but it isnt convenient for my fish-phobic family for me to eat fish for dinner so I thought I'd do my experimental cooking in the morning when they are both out of the house.. Hence swapping to fish for BAB this week. I guess the upside to this is that I ate extra fish for lunch on the days I had it for brekky. It will all be moot shortly since I go back to work on Monday so BAB will be whatever leftovers I can shovel into myself before leaving the house at 6.15am.

Charlotte, I ate the salmon straight out of the tin. I will work up to cooking it one day, but I think that's one I would like to have professionally prepared first so I know how it SHOULD taste

Thanks everyone on the o3/o6 ratios. Of course! I'm such a knob sometimes. Thanks for setting me straight about stored o6s.
Though it might not affect me immediately, I still think balancing daily ratios over time will result in balanced ratios overall. So I won't stress too much about it being perfect, but it is probably a good habit of eating to get into, no? Incidentally, why do we store o6 and not o3? Or is it just that i've never really eaten enough fish to ever store it, because my brain slurps up every skerrick immediately?

Caroline, thanks for the encouragement

I don't remember writing much about hormones. I just wanted to find some DHEA because of one of the blogs that made it loook like the wonder cure for aging! I saw your post on that other thread, and I shall order some online later today

I don't know too much about melatonin either, except what I learnt from hippies years ago, and what I have picked up along the way here. Also I cant have the webinars because i'm not a paid member. However, I will go see what I can find in the blogs for you... I dont know of any reason you wouldnt produce your own, if all other conditions are right. But I am only just starting to get my head around all the hormone stuff too...
You'd think I would know more since my main health complaint (apart from fatness) has been super debillitatingly painful periods.. Obviously they weren't too messed up though \*touch wood\* because the leptin rx and CT seems to have sorted that out really quickly.

House valuer will be here any minute now, so i'd best go get pretty for that..

### ashryn — 2013-01-09T01:44:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70021>

> DHEA pills 50mg $100.00 for 100 \[I asked 3 times if this price was right and they said yes!\]

> I just saw my Doc yesterday and He really doesn't know alot about melatonin either and then ..... I mentioned mitochondria .....He said - can we just do a couple of things at once ....... and not a million...

I plan to make sure the pool is shaded, that should keep it cool. But I think i'm just going to have to accept the fact that I live in a hot place, and take cold (or cool) wherever I can get it.

I have to admit that the ocean is still a cold shock when I get in.. I know that as soon as i have dunked my head it will be fine, not cold at all, but I persist in walking in trying to acclimatise.. Such a baby, I know :/
My dad was telling me that our ocean has a strong cold current running in close to the shore (closer than usual) which is why we've had so many shark attacks lately. Normally they stay west and south of Rottnest because thats where the current usually runs. Of course, my dad often believes anything someone tells him if they are persuasive enough, so.. Maybe grain of salt that one

Your poor doc

maybe he wants a chance to read up on one thing first before you hit him with another! The thing we have to remember is that GPs are like triage.. They are for sick people, and their job is to stop sick people dying by directing the to the appropriate specialist. They don't study health, they study disease.. Specialists on the other hand \*should\* know all about their field and how people came to be in the state they're in so they can recommend appropriate changes... But if they did that too well, they would have no patients!

### ashryn — 2013-01-09T03:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70034>

Today I ate more tuna, breakfast and lunch.. Maybe its beginning to grow on me.. (Not the scales i hope!) maybe. I shall persevere and give proper big-girl fish another go after a week.

CT last night after the beach was great.. I shivered and all! Not so much this morning though, but i still did a couple of hours after the house valuer came through. Then later I went for a spot of nudity on the balcony, and the hell with the aeroplanes

I did have an alarming thought though.. What if today was the day google was taking satellite pics of my house for google earth? And now that i have written that, i remember a site... Streetviewfun.com and this http://googlesightseeing.com/2006/11/top-10-naked-people-on-google-earth/
There really is no privacy at all, is there?

Slept right through the night again with no wakings.. Funny, I didn't think sleep was much of an issue for me, but sleeping the whole 8 hours really makes a difference to how I wake up. I love it! Now if i can just get me some awesome dreams like the one Darleen had the other day (seriously, a baby she immediately loved as her own being raised by the whole epiP village? Freaking AWESOME DREAM)

The last dream I remember DH was driving us to the beach along a route we always take, and some dude in a white ute was being a tool behind us.. Instead of getting stressed and angry as he usually does, DH changed lanes to take a different route. Ute guy sped past as soon as we got out of the way, through a red light, realised, jammed on his breaks, lost control and smashed at full speed into a wall. (He was seriously and graphically dead BTW) DH and I were nonplussed..
Me: "argh shit, do we have to hang around and be a witness or something?"
DH: "naah, he's already dead"

Go you dream talkers go!

So I'll be walking to my folks house tonight (it will be interesting to test the new fast-walking-without-getting-tired theory now after another week of healthful eating and CT) Dinner will be late unfortunately, but I have decided to wear my sexy blue blockers tonight and who cares what they think (actually, DH & DD both say they're cute on me, so I'll take that, even if the rest of my people call me a hipster)
Dinner doesn't usually begin till 7, so its going to be a reduced repair night.. But perhaps if i get some oxytocin in before sleep i can mitigate the damage.. We'll see.

Right... Going to look for the melatonin stuff to summarise, if I have time between now and the troops arriving home from work..

### Linz — 2013-01-10T05:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70130>

Ashryn, with your question on storing O6s, we don't store O6 and not O3. Most modern diets have a ratio that is far too high in 06 mainly due to using vegetable oils and grain-fed meat, and a lot of it is oxidised before consumption.
From one (or more) of the blogs on CT I think we actually store very different ratios in different tissues as they are required so long as we have a ratio close to 4:1 in our diet. My understanding is that there is a natural seasonal change in these ratios to help cold tolerance and the ratio can be improved rapidly with CT. When you start ct with poor ratios there are some unpleasant detox symptoms if you look at the early CT posts. Face pain with dunking and skin problems were some of the common ones and I think Reynaud-like symptoms. The O6 is not bad, we just have too much and when the right ratios are restored one of the obvious signs is greatly reduced sun burn.

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-13T13:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70510>

Tuna is so full of mercury, and if you are not able to clear it properly, you could have a metal problem. I was Paleo for 2 years, then epi-Paleo for many months, and had a Genova full nutritional profile done. Almost everything looked awesome, except mercury was so off the charts it was scary and B vitamins were extremely low. My doctor (paleo, gets it) put me on a concoction of B vitamins, as B's can help clear mercury. So I'm wondering what your B vitamin situation is? Are you on supplemental B?

I'd ditch the tuna, as it's such a large fish it eats so many smaller fish and gets so much higher mercury concentration. I know, it was one of the only fish I liked, and I've had to give it up too. It's funny, but I ditched it the first time I read Dr. K. calling canned tuna "the spam of the sea". I'm afraid even the tuna steaks or sushi just has too much mercury, which in my case contributed to my super-excessive levels.

### ashryn — 2013-01-15T08:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70837>

Hi Pam, i have lotsa b vitamins, both supplemental and from all the meat i eat, i doubt its that. If i am ever going to develop a tastefor fish, i have to start somewhere, and tuna is about the only thing i can manage, so i was erring on the side of something is better than nothing.

Argh, i don't know.. Maybe this is going to be just like every other thing i've tried.. They work like magic for the first couple of weeks, then bam. Plateau and no change. Maybe its cortisol from work stress compared to being on holidays. I need to learn how to not let myself get stressed at work.. It is really a nice job with supportive team and decent managers doing meaningful work.. I create the stress myself, i just don't know how to not stress..

Ahem.. Anyway, heres an update..
BAB has been going fine, 50 or more o protein within 30 of waking, plus 1/4cup coconut oil for good measure.
Still getting hungry at lunch now.. Going to try adding extra-more coconut oil to brekky tomorrow and see what happens. It is really convenient to not be hungry at lunch time, so i'd like to get that back. Dinners have pretty much been the same as BAB only with some spinach and avocado or some similar veg. Usually dinner happens about 5pm and usually that has me sorted till breakfast, though last night i had to work late on the computer editing photos, and so couldn't use the blue blockers or all my photos would have had their colours skewed. I noticed that my stomach started to growl around 11pm... Which is about when i always used to go get a snack to get me through another hour of work, so thats interesting. luckily that photo editing job was a once off thang..

The fish saga has stalled for the moment. I WILL get back on the fish horse sometime soon.

Cold showers only, at least, no hot water.. I'm not sure the water could fit anyone's definition of cold.. I've drunk colder cups of coffee!
Not so good with the ice belly the last few days, after whatever day it was when i had my little spaz attack here and got too discouraged to bother writing. I have still been wearing the ice vest daily for as long as it stays ice anyway. I'm not sure if it is ToM impending, or not belly icing, or a combination, but today is a fat day and i'm trying not to dwell on it.

Got some new beta blockers, but they give me headaches, and dont seem to block as much blue light as my cheap ones, so i've switched back.
Sleep this week has been ok, but not as optimL as i'd like.. Too much light, wine and visiting. Back to doing all the right things tonight.

On the supplement front, i have added DHEA to my daily regimen, just 33 mg this morning to ease into it, haven't noticed any difference in energy levels, but i'll give it a week.. Uless anyone reckons i should jump straight to the 100mg dose immediately? I'm willing to experiment, hell, maybe it will shake things up and get them moving again.

Shock of my life, my daughter has joined me on the leptin reset journey this week.. She has gone from being the never-eats-breakfast-always tired-late-tv-watching-carb fiend to being up every morning scarfing down meat.. She has even had a go on eggs and mushrooms. Amazing-
She only has a couple of kilos to lose, so hopefully she will feel much better in herself and keep off the carb/sugars when the reset is done.
It is nice to have someone ask me what to do and just trust that i'll tell her the right things, but i'd really like it if she was interested in the whole package, and the reasons why this lifestyle is important.. She keeps calling it a diet, so i don't think she sees it as a way of life. But i have to be cacreful to not bombard her with too much, just have good succinct answers ready when she needs them. My sister is doing the no carb keto thing too, now if i can just convince my mum and dad that grains are what is causing their bellies, i'll be happy.. But i'm not going to evangelise.. Just wait till they ask, and let them see the results on me.. Assuming i see any more results. DH is losing weight just by being near people on the paleo diet.. The bastard!

The pool is going ahead for sure, 6.5% deposit paid, expected to go into the ground in 3 weeks.. Then watch how easy my CT is!
Unfortunately my mad powers of persuasion didn't extend to convincing DH that we should drop several thousand on rollershutters for our bedroom windows, so there is still light leakage through the curtains. I bought a pile of material planning to make something i could block the light with but then i lost my craft mojo and couldnt work out how to make it effective. DH has been doing the manly thing and finding tradesmen to come and finish off all the bits of our house we didnt do before we moved in.. On the weekend we moved all of the timber we ecycled from the house we demolished from storage to the garage, and i noticed i could carry as much as the boys with no trouble at all. I also noticed that i can really see my biceps these days.. (Ahah! Thats why the scales haven't changed in weeks.. All the fat weight is being replaced with biceps!) ----listen woman, Hold on to that change.. Its a change in the right direction, its proof that you're not wasting time and energy here..
Yes, also, now that i think of it, the bathers i wore to the beach today are too big, ( i know this because once they were wet they were hanging off me, and the bit of boobie support they have was loose and thus unsupportive heheh) when they weren't too big last week. GOOD THING NUMBER 2!

I remember now, the way to keep myself positive is to LOOK for and notice the good stuff. Duh. Someone slap me next time i start whining ok?

Right, journal is up to date.. I'm off to summarise the DHEA blog now since i read, understood and acted on it, but can't explain why.. Except that its a cool hormone i want more of..

### ashryn — 2013-01-15T09:36:04+0200

Oh! jansz just reminded me of something i had forgotten on the LRx.. BAB should be 50% of daily calories!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-15T13:35:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70874>

Clearly you are still on step one

because the day you stop worrying about how much you weigh and become more concerned with the markers that show you are on the way to optimal is the day you have finally gotten "it." don't feel bad about this because it's taken me about a year to get there. this program is NOT about losing weight (my little low-estrogen driven temper tantrum from this past weekend aside) it is about repairing the damage done to your health over however-many years you have been getting it "wrong."

The best advice I can toss your way is to stop worrying about your weight. Focus on how you are feeling. Get the labs done to check your baseline and then rinse and repeat to track your progress. (god I'm starting to sound like Mini-Jack here)

I hear you starting to voice doubts that this will work. Trust me. Seriously ... trust me on this. IT DOES. Ask Vickie, she'll say pretty much the same thing. I feel SO much better than I did a year ago. When doubts start to creep in I go read Jack's story again. I trust what he says because he has BEEN there.

And kudos on your daughter joining you. Leading by example is the best way to convince others.

### endless — 2013-01-15T22:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-70974>

I would not worry about the mercury issue...eat some tuna when you can and don't stress about it. If you get to the point that you are more comfortable with fish you will probably naturally eat more fresh fish anyway.

I have to agree with Darleen as well....if you read the first pages of my journal you'll see that I was reluctant to start the reset because I knew from reading that some people GAINED weight before they lost it....it's hard to do something that might result in weight gain when you've spent your entire adult life trying to lose weight

I had to decide that it was ok with me if that happened because the end result I was after was not just about weight. So far, that has not happened to me, but if it does I will not freak out about it because I believe that in the long run this is what I need to do.

Sometimes we just have a shitty day and our outlook is crappy too....hope you feel more positive soon!

### ashryn — 2013-01-16T05:48:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71008>

Hrm.. Yes, i know all those things you helpful people are saying.. Thanks, Don't mind me, sometimes I need to 'say it out loud' (or in this case write things publicly) to counter the negative imp on repeat in my brain. Hence finding the good signs at the end of my post. I am not technically counting calories but I do need a tool to make sure I get the required amount of protein for BAB, I eat till I am full and don't opt for the 'low cal' alternative. I don't ever know how many I have consumed till the end of the day when I record it for the purposes of making sure I have enough vitamins and working out how much protein is in the meals I prepare so I know what is the minimum amount I have to eat etc.
...but i forgot that most of your food should be done in the morning. Thats why I was getting hungry when I changed to tuna for breakfast, it doesn't have enough fat to make sure I get half at breakfast.. Hence needing to eat lunch again.

I ate a stupid amount of fat off the lamb roast for breakfast this morning plus 1/4 cup of CO and have only just started to feel hungry 12 hours later..

Like furface, i am keen to continue to see fast results like the first couple of weeks, but being impatient doesn't mean i am going to give up. It just means I don't have exciting news to write in my journal

And it was a fat day because of ToM - i didn't even bother with the scales, they are broken! I just felt like a lump.. I really should be happy about this, since I used to feel like a lump but in so much pain that I was doped up to not feel anything. Even if its a fat day, it has been one with no need for painkillers, an amazing development that I give credit to CT for.

Woah, check out that the semi-defensive self justification... Think i shall put this one to bed and go have a little think about what's been going on with me the last couple of days.. Tomorrow i'll have interesting things to say..

### diane — 2013-01-16T12:51:29+0200

### endless — 2013-01-17T01:43:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71153>

I use avocados liberally to make sure I am getting enough fat......but I also don't plug it into an online counting tool. I have a rough estimate of my most common breakfast foods, so I can get as close to 50 g protein as possible....but the other meal(s) have no pressure, so I don't record them at all.; I am sorta shooting for 50% of my daily intake in those second 1 or 2 meals, and that is all I have to know!

Hasta Manana!

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T07:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71184>

blockquote>**diane said**

Ashryn, you have a unique way of writing that makes everything you say interesting!

Cheers Diane, glad I'm entertaining someone!

Endless, I will probably use paleotrack less as I get better at judging protein amounts, but until then it helps me to know i'm on track.. Knowing me, if I wasn't counting and had a slowdown in changes I'd be stressing about it.. And you know, cortisol poisoning

A little bit, I want a record of what I was eating when I lost all my excess weight forever.. For posterity or something, or to prove something to someone one day if I ever need to..

Today's update:
Felt shitty this morning because of ToM cramping, woke at 3.50am in much agony. Decided to give CT a go and got an ice pack elastic strapped to my belly and went back to sleep. It worked... ish. But by the time I had to get up the ice had gone warm and melty and my ovaries were screaming again which left me shaking and unable to think straight. So I took ibuprofen and a tablespoon of disappointment and went to work.

The good news is that I didn't need any more painkillers today, and despite being insanely busy at work, i had focus, patience and insight with an ability to inspire people to join me on my dream conspiracy to upgrade the computer systems.. (Long story, boring)
I am doing some preventative CT as I write this in the hopes that this morning was the worst of it over, and if that's all I have to deal with, I can cope with that for a few months whilst the hormones sort themselves out.

I stopped eating salmon oil a couple of weeks ago, whereas I ate it for the full month previously.. So I took some this morning and will keep going for the month to see how we go.. Then stop for a month to test what happens..

I have been asked to head a huge project at work despite having little experience in it, and it makes me feel like people have recognised my competence and trust me to do a good job, this makes me feel very positive. I want to attribute it to my brain working better that I am not terrified ( ok, i am a bit, but it isn't paralysing me for a change) I don't feel the need to point out my uncertainties or shortcomings as much, and this, more than anything makes me feel GOOD!

PS feeling positive or happy about anything on day 2 of ToM is unheard of for me, so... Shh if you think i haven't been doing this long enough for my brain to be healing. Just shh.

And now a curious thing about which I would welcome speculation...
I have long suspected PCOS but never had it checked, tbh, I kind of liked an excuse to zonk myself out with codeine 6 nights of every month and sleep well - I sleep well most nights now, so it's a nonissue. Several times in the past I have wondered if I could feel lumps in my abdomen where my ovaries live, but haven't really taken myself seriously since I do have a tendency to be a bit of a hypochondriac, but now that my belly is shrinking, I can actually see a slight lump on one side, compared to the other side which is flatter. The lump is about fist sized, and stands out about a centimetre different to the other side. I'm not overly worried since it has probably been there hidden by fat for years, but I am curious to know if anyone has any ideas about this.. When you grow cysts on your ovaries, can you see them from outside? It doesn't feel any different to touch.

For the record, I fully expect that if this is PCOS, my CT and epi paleo will sort it, so I don't feel the need to get 'checked out' unless the ToM thing gets worse again..

More odd things...
I have some kind of infection in my belly button.. I noticed this morning the skin inside is very red and angry.. It may well have been like this a long time and Ive never noticed because I couldn't see it hahaha. Salt water? Tea tree oil? Leave it alone and let it sort itself out?

I walked shoeless to mum and dads for dinner last night.. Bless them, DH & DD took 5 whole minutes to ask me why I was carrying my shoes

I told them I was earthing and recharging my negative electrons. They said ok. The words nutjob and crazy only appeared twice during the rest of the walk as well! I think they might be very entertained by my newfound eccentricity. I know I am!

Now off to catch up on all your journalling too!

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T08:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71191>

Notes on melatonin

It is the third most important hormone after DHEA and oxytosin.
We make melatonin from tryptophan, and it is only released after 4 hours of dark, as long as there is no insulin floating around.
We need it to repair and renew our cells, reduce oxidation, take care of the heart, eyes and gut. It helps our body repair damage that would lead to diabetes, heart disease and cancer. Its also involved with immunity, hair growth libido, blood pressure, weight loss, bone maintenance, and lots of other things.

Tryptophan is the least common amino acid in our diet, and difficult to absorb because it needs to compete with other amino acids for absorbtion.

### ashryn — 2013-01-17T09:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71197>

Notes on grounding blog

Grounding is about making contact with the earth for the purposes of recharging your negative electrons. The earth has lots of negative, and bodies have progressively more positively charged electrons owing to insulating shoes/clothes and increasing Electro-magnetic frequencies generated by all our awesome tech.

You do it by sticking both feet sans shoes on the ground. The idea is that negatively charged electrons will move from a place of abundance (earth) to a place of scarcity (body) until they are balanced.
Sticking a conductive rod into the earth and attaching it to something conductive (body, ipad, kettle) works too.

This was proved in a 2005 study by Applewhite.

The charge lasts longer when its cold. You are more conductive when wet.
The ocean is a good option for grounding because there is cold, wet and the earth.

EMF
The power grid produces EMF of 60hz, way higher than the earth. Our buildings have powerlines running through them that produce these frequencies even when nothing is plugged into them. Much like running the blender interferes with the TV reception, these frequencies can cause interference in our body's electrical signals.

Reactions with electrons and EMF in ungrounded bodies creates "unnatural molecular perturbations" grounding shields us from this effect. This was also shown by the Applewhite study.

"An EMF has both an electric and a magnetic field, and the difference between the two and their effects on the body are determined by the bodies current energy supply of free electrons from food and their own ATP production in mitochondria."

A university study found that exposure to electrical energies for long periods increased the risk of stress, illness and degeneration as well as reducing oxygen uptake and energy levels. Insulated and synthetic clothing makes this worse

The SCN in the brain uses the earth's EMF to know what time it is so it can release the appropriate chemicals when we need them. For example, the mitochondria use these signals to create ATP. This creation stops in a positively charged environment, so the more positive charge we build up, the less energy we produce. EMF from our gadgets is so loud it interferes with the reception and our brains become deaf to the earth's signalling. If you are earthed, emf charges coming at you from anywhere else are negated.

The SCN gets signals from light and temp through the retina, which tell the pineal gland when to produce melatonin where there is enough ATP to do so.
The SCN also has the same kinds of cells found in birds, bees and bats that pick up the earth's magnetic frequency, so it stands to reason that we also pick up the earth's magnetic frequency with these cells.

If we can't pick up the earth's frequencies, and we can't tell night from day because of our lights, and our hormone signalling is out of whack because of these things, then we can't produce the right chemicals to make us function in an optimal way. The longer this goes on the harder it is to remain healthy.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T08:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71574>

Ok, update time..
Feeling a bit crap today... I hijacked someones journal to chime in to ask about how much Mg we should be aiming at and got the "up to bowel tolerance" response.. I have found it. 1200 mg morning and night is too much. Wll drop down to 600 morning and 1200 night and see how I go...
(Damn I'm punny)

Couldn't finish BAB , but I almost made it.. I was about 6grams off, which I put in the fridge to eat later when I got hungry... It has been 12 hours, and no sign of hungry yet.. Honestly, this is most confusing. Is this a sign that I am reset, or just a response to the Mg overdose...

Apart from a few hours of bad on day 2, cramps have pretty much disappeared, which spins me out, DD just came to tell me that she has had the same pleasing results, after eating this way for just a week. She did spend 4 hours in a cool bath though. Most interesting.

Was really tired after staying up late with friends last night, despite sleeping 8 hours with no wake-ups, so I went for a nap around 2.30 and slept 3 hours.. In all a pretty wasted day, but I'm going to chalk it up to healing and go with that. DD and I had a good talk about stuff, and how well she is doing, and she said she thinks she will stay on the paleo thing after she has done the reset because she feels better and doesn't really miss the foods she though she would miss all that much. This makes me so happy! We have decided to both go to the doctor and make her agree to send us for all the recommended tests.. I just have to come up with good reasons to give her. I may have to trawl the labs threads to see what I can find to relate to my health..

I am on my second go of CT as I write this, it is quite addictive, and i LOVE the way my belly feels when it is all numb and frozen firm after CT. So what if it doesn't LOOK nice (yet), I just don't look, but I find myself rubbing my belly like a pregnant woman, (which probably looks odd) and imagining that firmness is what its always like... This is as good as positive self talk mumbo jumbo, right?

I have been quite entertained by the grounding craze this week, mostly because my crazy conspiracy theory uncle told me about it years ago (which made me skeptical, of course, contrary thing that I am) but the idea has always stayed with me, and as a consequence, i am amd have always been mostly barefoot whenever i am home... Taking off my shoes is the very first thing I do when I get home from anywhere, some days i don't even make it through the car drive home! As with all of my contradictions, I absolutely LOVE shoes, and cannot walk past a shoe shop without stopping, I have been called Imelda Marcos on occasion.. I just don't like to wear them if I don't have to

Anyway, I have no idea if my new tiled floor is earthed enough to do any good, but I have always chucked a pile of salt into my baths, and use salt to exfoliate the super-deep ingrown hairs I get so I reckon intuition might have got me some way towards good health, now I have to go do some more research to see what I can do to make it more effective..

And just a word of warning... I am about to begin a philosophy course on coursera, so my posts for the next couple of months are likely to be a bit on the thoughtful side..

### ashryn — 2013-01-20T06:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71775>

Oh dear.. My poor belly skin must hate me.. It has just recovered from sunburn, and now I've given it ice-burn. Not sure why it burned today and hasnt any other day... Could it be related to still having salt on my skin from this morning's swim?

I guess I'll have to ease up on the bellyicing for a couple of days.

After feeling vaguely unwell all day yesterday, and not at all hungry, I ate 1slice bacon for dinner (because I figured I should eat more than one time in 24 hours.. Then I wasn't hungry for breakfast either. I still ate it though, and just got hungry at 3pm.. I ate some hardboiled eggs because I didnt feel like eating any meat, I guess I'll call that dinner. Is this what the good Doc means when he says hunger is gone when you're reset? Could it be possible? Eep! What do I do now?

I read back over the post reset eating thing but I'm still not sure whether to start on that now. What will happen if I am not reset and start eating that way? Minus the carbs of course, because I still have weight to lose. Is there any post reset people reading this who have advice?

Maybe I should give it a couple more weeks before I decide? I really like the idea of only having 25 grams of protein for breakfast though.

BAB today was a steak and 200g mushrooms cooked in lard and seasoned with turmeric.
Dinner was 4 hardboiled eggs with pink salt and black pepper.
Earthed and CT at the beach for 2 hours, tried to go to the surf beach but everyone was out of the water again because of sharks. Damn they are getting annoying.

Spot iced and CFB vest for 2 hours whilst I caught up on forum posts, the washing and the EMF film

Last night I had itchy legs again so I did the cold bath with salt and slept like a baby.. A baby who sleeps well. Actually my skin is looking pretty crap this week.. I wonder if it is shedding the nasty layer to make way for lovely childlike skin.. One can only hope..

Perhaps I will keep on the LRx until optimal reset starts, do the 90 day thing and then reassess. Yes, that seems logical.

I have a time consuming job, and I just signed up for my hunting license. Then reading all of Dr. Kruses blogs on which I have not caught up yet, plus the forum and my own research, I am busy from early morning till late evening. But I would love to read what you have learned.
> Neuroscience is very interesting. My friend, who visited me last night is a doctor of psychology and does a lot of Trauma work after Peter Levine and Manfred Spitzer. She is very much into neuroscience as this is a big part of her work and what she tells me is very very interesting. Actually we were discussing this morning, how epi-paleo could support her work with her clients as trauma seems to shut down the endocrinological system at some level. She explained to me some interesting studies along those lines. However, it is not along the lines of my work (but it is along yours, I believe), so I have to set priorities.
> When are you starting?

Hahaha it isn't even close to the lines of my work

Although I trained as a naturopath, I don't practice.. I just like knowing things

I currently work in IT for a government department and spend most of my days helping people to design websites, or fix things they have broken... I study compulsively though, because I never feel like I understand enough, and I want to understand everything. My favourite thing is mixing really dry scientific knowledge with a philosophical way of thinking, I find it hugely entertaining to find patterns in behaviour that link the two...
I actually thought the neuroscience one was starting this month, but it is the philosophy, so N must be in Feb..

Caroline, they are first year units, so you don't need much of a base, just a willingness to do the readings, watch the lectures and participate in the forums... Kind of like here!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T06:31:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-71986>

I understand, Ashryn. I thought you were working in this field.

My job is not limited to 8 or 9 hrs per day but often double that but never less than 12 hrs. per day. So I have to be very selective what I do with the limited spare hrs. I have. So I prefer these days to stick with facts, which good science provides and stay away from what philosphers think about life. To me philosophy (and religion) are opinions and beliefs (I may be wrong, who knows, as I can only base any of my own opinions of what I know now). I have read a lot of philosophy and in the end realized (this is just for me and I do not claim that this necessarily goes for anybody else), that there is nothing there that has not been thought before and the one has this way of thinking and the other the opposite and they both have good arguments with which they back it. Somehow, this all seems to me in the realm of fiction. I make a distinction between the brain and the mind. I like to use the brain for practicalities, not to use my brain to entertain my mind. That is why I want to optimize my brain (there is the true intelligence, it knows what to do without me, actually much better without me, all I have to do is give it the best nutrition and conditions), but my mind I do not trust.

Now, I just want to think for myself and my daily life observations give me ample food for thought. Since I do not even take my own opinions seriously anymore I became a little wary of the others. There are 7 Billion people on this planet who have every day opinions about the world, life, the neighbour, wheather, you name it.

I feel the need to explain that more closely as I do not want you to misunderstand me. One of my best friends is a vegetarian and she has a lot of opinions about that. If she could back it up with hard science I would consider, but she cannot so I just feel she can do as she sees fit and I help to reduce the damage as much as I can. But I am not going into dialogue with her about that, it is futile. She does not care about the facts and I do not care about her opinion. But facts intrigue me.

I really hope that it does not sound harsh, sometimes I come across like that, and please do not take it personal, it is not meant in that way. See it as an attempt to explain where I am at right now and what goes on in my mind (which I do not take seriously

)

### ashryn — 2013-01-21T10:33:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72015>

It does sound harsh.. But 'm not offended.

I do have to reply though because I feel more strongly about what you said than I expected... And because this is my journal!

It feels like you're scolding me for wanting to stretch my mind to consider ideas I have never considered before.. (Because even if someone else has thought about these things, I haven't thought them yet.) If I wasn't the kind of person to be fascinated by ideas, I would never have made it to this site. I would never have even started looking.

I disagree that philosophy and religion can be used interchangeably, philosophy is about embracing ideas, religion is about controlling and restricting ideas to a specific set. Sure, the 'great' philosophers had set ideas about things that they believed wholly, but studying them isn't about learning and following their dogma, it is about exploring their ideas and using them to understand (or try to) something about humans, human nature and our world.

I also don't make a distinction between my mind, brain or body, I think they are all intricately connected in a way we don't understand, and I doubt we ever will undstand if we don't think, ponder, ruminate and explore any and all ideas. If we force a distinction, how do we know we aren't missing something?

Here's a thought... Since Descartes, we have thought there was a distinction between mind and brain, and it led neuroscience to believe (dogmatically, despite evidence to the contrary) that certain parts of the brain were responsible for specific functions. Remove the part of the brain that sees, and the person will never see, even if they have functioning eyes... They now know this to be untrue, and that you can make new brain pathways. This has only been accepted in the industry for a relatively short time, though the thinkers, the explorers in the field had proven it over and over again.. But the BRAINS of the establishment refused to see the patterns, accept the evidence, or even consider the possibility that what they KNEW could be wrong.

This story is common.. Look at CW.. Look at the patterns Dr K makes... Look at the things he links up because he thinks and philosophises about things... Look at the things You know are true that other people KNOW are not.. Who is right? How do you know you have read the right science? How do you know they haven't missed something?

I'm not suggesting you should go do philosophy if you aren't interested, (in fact i was just being encouraging because it sounded like you wished you could study some more) but perhaps you might consider that people thinking (and learning how to think) might have a contribution to make to our society, and not hate on it so vehemently.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-21T11:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72036>

sorry Ashryn, it was never my intention to scold you, I knew you meant well and that is why I said not to take it personally because I was afraid that might happen, when I explain and I do not hate it at all, I am just not interested for my own reasons, but I like the idea of you studying it, if that is what you like doing and I could see that you were excited about it. But I guess I missed my point. There is a distinction between brain and mind as a computer hardware and a computer software are not the same. The software is the interpreter of what the hardware receives. And yes, they are a unit in some way, but my idea is why not let the hardware receive without the software interpreting everything.
Just looked up the german word for "Verstand" and it gives different translations. Maybe I used the wrong one and I should say rather intellect than mind, but mind was the first one that came up on leo.
So what I mean is for example: we see a flower and immediately we give it a name. Ah this is a rose, but could we not, instead, just enjoy the beauty of the color, shape, the smell, its leafs, the softness and leave it there? The message (color, shape, etc.) is received by the brain, but it is interpreted by the mind. I had the best moments in my life when my mind was quiet and I was just taking in what I saw without interpretation. True peacefulness.
So for me now, I rather want to improve this quality of receiving without interpretation.
I guess I made a bad job at explaining, I now hope that I could do it better.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T11:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72039>

\[waves at Ashryn\] ... a fellow traveler!!! How cool! Nice to know there are more out there with a similar mindset to my own. Makes me feel less lonely. THANKS!

### ashryn — 2013-01-27T15:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-72853>

Argh.. It's been a while since I have updated, mainly because I don't have anything interesting to tell. Nothing much is going on with me getting healthy, still sleeping well, still eating BAB getting hungry 10 minutes after waking and around 4pm most days. Plenty of energy, and feeling a bit more capable in general, but still shitty at the lack of weight movement..

yes yes YES! I know what you're going to say, but it doesn't make any difference. And you can tease and bully me all you like for 'still being at the first stage' I am still shitty that I am doing the right things and am not skinny enough yet.

The EMF2 blog was a bit of a letdown I feel, because whilst I believe how important Schumann resonance is, and the benefit/urgency of ensuring electron balance, I felt that the blog would do more to convince people to stop reading into the topic because of the hyperactive tone and constant repetitive insistence that it was true, without a proper explanation. I don't think simply repeating e=mc2 over and over is an effective way to convince or communicate. I get that it was a source of inspiration, but I wish the mechanics had been better explained in more scientific terms.

Gah. I'm probably going to get flamed to death for saying it, but using science words does not make it science. Calling a horse's tail a leg doesn't mean it has five legs...

i understand the inspiration and the metaphor, and the need to pick something no one can argue with to use as a base for an argument, but I am far more convinced by reading the experiences of people exposed to artificial light or wifi, or night shift workers when it is combined with scientific studies that explain the role of melatonin ( for example) i am far more convinced when I read case studies jack has conducted to test his theories than metaphysical insistence that the end of the world is nigh.

And I am convinced.. Just not by the blog.. The first one had me making all kinds of changes, taking extra opportunities for grounding, reexamining my WoL to find more ways to shield myself, congratulating myself on the many things i am already doing right...

I love all the things I have learned since reading this site, and rediscovering the things I once knew and somehow forgot, I truly feel like Dr K has saved me from a life of increasing degeneration and early death, and has freely given me the tools I need to take back control of my life.. I want his ideas to change the world, I want him to be recognised for his genius, and I am afraid that the presentation of the ideas is going to get in the way of that. I would like to help if I could, after all, he has helped me, and I have been able to pass on enough of the ideas to help others whom I love.
I fervently appreciate the opportunities I have had thanks to this website.

I have redoubled my efforts to get 'fish adapted' and cooked some whiting in foil with ghee, garlic and lemon for dinner. It was edible, though I suspect someone else would have cooked it better.

I have been experimenting with my TENS machine and have found that I can set it to 8hz, which is close to the magic number.. It makes me feel energised, but is it replenishing electrons? No idea. The fact that it is brilliant for chronic pain management suggests to me that it is, since the painkilling effect lasts several hours after using it, and longer after each session. (I don't have any pain at the moment to test it on, but this is true based on past experience from a chronic back injury) DH has a 'sound and light machine' that pulses light in time with the frequency of beats it hears, and he has come up with a way to make it pulse in response to binaural beats in Schumann resonance. (You have to mix a binaural beat track into both channels to make the light pulse properly) we haven't tried it out yet, but maybe using it on the grass outside will increase the earthing effect... Who knows?

I am interested in TCS now too, especially since the existing tens unit i have can be used for that.. But I can't find any info on whether the frequency has to be precise, or if anything that pulses electricity into your brain will do to increase electrons. It sounds like that's what jack is saying... How do you tell if its a negative or positive charge?

### diane — 2013-01-27T15:59:28+0200

Hi Ashryn! I don't think that anyone is trying to tease and bully you - they're just trying to help. But it's fine for you feel shitty about your weight - those are your feelings and you have to experience them. I'm not saying it's not frustrating, cause it is - and I'm not trying to convince you to not have them. Just saying I understand.

### ashryn — 2013-01-29T11:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73129>

Yesterday I ate a 'paleo pizza' made with a batter base made from coconut flour and eggs.. Probably too many carbs in it anyway, since I later ate a couple of biscuits from a Xmas present. Sadly, it wasn't even worth it. If they had been delicious, I wouldn't regret it.. Lesson learned I suppose.

Still, considering its the first sugary carb thing I have eaten since December 20 (ish- tbh I can't remember the date is started doing this properly) I don't think it will put me off the rails. I expect to be back in keto tomorrow if I'm not already.

I have been quite sleepy the last three days, needing a nap in the afternoon on Sunday and Monday, but I also got my front garden sorted yesterday with 28 bags of mulch and a bunch of plants and some additional reticulation, so even the naps didnt stop me for long. I noticed that I had no trouble at all picking up 50kg bags to load them into the trolley, into and out of the car and then out to the garden, I'm sure in the past that sort of thing would have left me tired and sore, but I had no ill effects from it. (The nap I had before the mulch work.)

Shortly I am going out to sit in the light of the setting sun to weed the lawn for a bit and get some grounding at the same time.

I ate lunch yesterday then wasn't hungry for dinner, and was still not hungry for breakfast this morning. I made bacon wrapped scallops as recommended by many of the fish lovers, but they were yucky, and I could only choke down 3 of the 4 I made. As a consequence I got hungry for lunch.

So... I have a bunch of frozen wild caught scallops still in the freezer.. What else can I do with them? Preferably something that hides their taste. - I retasted breakfast for hours today

For lunch I mixed up tuna, salmon and cream cheese, stuck it in a rammikin with broccoli and cauliflower mash on top and a sprinkling of cheese and black pepper and am calling it sea-shepherd's pie.. It's actually ok. I have another one heating in the oven for dinner as I write this. I'm not hungry at all, but I assume I should eat anyway.. Shouldn't I? It eats a bit messed up when I have days off because I want to use the time that DH & DD are out to experiment with fishy cooking.. So there is lunch when I probably don't need it and dinner so I don't get hungry later.

Next time I will use just cauliflower in the mash, and make sure i get a real (not frozen) cauli, but for some reason I was ultra-vague today and forgot to buy it twice, then my local grocery store didn't have any real ones.. I have a suspicion that they might be out of season because they source all their fresh produce locally. I am looking forward to trying a slow cooked shepherd's pie with the cauli mash.. Should be awesome.

I am actually struggling to come up with interesting things to cook because I'm just not really hungry.

Been a bit slack on the CT after the ice burning incident, partly because I am letting the burn heal, and partly because now the CFB seems to be hurting where it sits on my collarbones, and sort of burns on my back. Whilst I am happy that I have collarbones to hurt, I am wondering why CT is painful on my skin all of a sudden... Any ideas?

### ashryn — 2013-01-29T13:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73135>

Lawn is weeded dude.. Sitting on the grass in the sun gives me too much time to think, so now I have more musings i wish to record here..
About being pissed that I am not getting skinnier..
Everyone says its about getting health sorted first, but I feel like mine is sorted mostly. I mean, I had some hormonal issues clearly, since the painful periods, but they have gone away, and I sleep well and all night these days and don't get cravings for food or energy crashes, and I don't have any long term illnesses.. Except fatness. I am seeing changes in my strength and energy, stamina for walking or doing gardening, I even feel more confident in myself, so why am I not losing weight now?

Reading everyone's journals just makes me more depressed because everyone keeps finding more things wrong with them, and adding more and more supplements and gadgets to their routine, and I begin to wonder whether we are all trooping up the garden path.

Did anyone start off not so unwell and get the right results in the 6-8 weeks initially estimated? Apparently calories don't count when you produce the right amount of melatonin at the right time because it kicks off the chain that will ultimately result in burning all the excess off.. This makes sense to me, except that I'm not burning off any of the stored fat any more. How do I make it start again?

One of the blogs says that as you get reset you naturally eat less, so it seems to me that perhaps I should just not eat when I'm not hungry, and stop trying to eat till I am full at all meals. Is the no IF rule just to prevent craving/bingeing? That's generally not an issue for me anyway.

\*sigh\*

### NittDan — 2013-01-29T13:47:18+0200

Ashryn, this is a long journey...rejoice in all the great changes that have occured...keep on keepin' on.

You are on the right path.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-29T15:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73139>

Hi Ashryn, I was logged out since the change of the forum, so I could not post. I wish there was something I could say to ease your discomfort which I can understand clearly. For what my personal opinion is worth:
Like you, I am strong, do not have a lot of health issues (thats what I think, maybe Dr. Kruse is of a different opinion, my doctor, however, thinks I am doing great). I am just fat, not so much anymore though. I do not have the patience like Darleen to hang in there for year and maybe another and then maybe lose the weight I need and want to lose. So I have my own approach which is to lose the weight now and then worry about the other issues later. At least I do not have to suffer that way, as my weight is going in the right direction. Coming off my 2nd hCG round I noticed that I still have leptin resistance as well as insulin resistance, but it is still better than it used to be. So this is moving in the right direction also. And even though I know that this is not the approach that Dr. Kruse prefers, I do prefer it that way and it suits me. And oh, it feels so good to lose that weight. I got really confused for a while reading on this website and was trying to mix what I have been doing sucessfully with all the new insights I gained here just to realize that this did not work well except throw me off. So back to square one for me. hCG until I have lost all the weight, then leptin reset and then all else. One thing at a time. I guess, what I want to say is, when you have made up your mind and realized what works for you, stick with it and do not get discouraged. Watch what is happening and adjust. Maybe a good step is like you said, not eat when you are not hungry and stop when you are not hungry anymore.

### Inger — 2013-01-29T19:42:13+0200

Ohh.. Ashryn. When I read your posts I just get such a strong urge to take you by my hand and just jump out into life... right into the sun and show you all the beauty..
Look Ashryn.. the beauty, the healing is right in front of you I promise! You'll do it! Just.. give it some time, and do all the good things. You are so worth it!

### diane — 2013-01-29T19:55:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73180>

Ashryn, I think it's all about context and what works for you. Darleen's journey is her journey - Charlotte's is hers, neither is the same, nor is it better or worse in my view. They have each gotten to where they are through different paths and the one that's right for them. It's for you to find yours.

I'm starting to think that the body is like an instrument panel. Sometimes one light has to come on before the next one does, sometimes multiple come on at the same time. And sometimes turning one on turns others off, so you have to figure out which one gets them back. I could be wrong - that's just my view. It's all very complex - that I know for sure! But from what I've seen from people here, when start with the foundational things, certain things will change. They will be different from person to person. From there, you can see what changes and what doesn't, and then figure out where you need to go next. Which is my naturopath's approach, and one that really resonated with me. Again, just my observation and I could be wrong.

Where I've gotten in my journey is that when I just concentrate on losing weight - I know I screw things up for myself down the line, and it comes back plus more, and then makes it harder the next time. And that's not just through the SAD diets - thats' from paleo too. But that's my journey, and so I'm trying to turn on a bunch of panel lights, then work on the weight.

Charlotte, my journey isn't about patience. I have no choice in the matter. RT3 keeps me from losing and keeping the weight off. Cutting calories (the only way I knew how to lose prior to finding paleo and Jack) only makes the situation worse.

So when I get impatient, which is like ALWAYS, I have to step back and remind myself of just how I'm broken and what I have to do to fix it. i.e., lots of good calories, CT and more CT.It wasn't until I finally pinned down the WHY that I have been able to relax about it just a bit (low estrogen meltdowns aside). ### Charlotte — 2013-01-29T21:19:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73197>

I do not deny that Darleen. As Diane has put it, we all have our own ways.
We all need to do what we feel works for us. I never lose my focus, and I know exactly where my focus is, professionally and personally. That is how I built my company and that is how I reach my personal goals.
I never ask WHY, maybe that is where we are different. For me the why has never helped me fix anything. Why, to me is irrelevant. Most may not agree, but it works for me. I am not looking for answers, I am looking for results. I see, something is broken, I fix it, I see something needs my attention, I give it. In Germany we say: there are many roads leading to Rome. You can take Via Appia or Via Romana or any other Via. Important is that we get were we want to go.

### ashryn — 2013-01-30T15:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73338>

Update time... Thank you all for being encouraging.. I will keep on powering on regardless of how impatient I get, because i DID lose some weight, and I have plenty of energy (mostly) and despite the suspicion that I have turned into a crazy bitch with my sunglasses at night, ice cuddling and barefoot walks on the street, I do feel better, sleep better, think better etc.
Diane: I agree it is about my personal context, but I would like to deal with it from the vantage of another dress size down!
Charlotte: I don't think HGC is for me.. Prior to finding this stuff, I had been 6 months on VLC of 700 calories per day, and whilst I know there is more to that protocol than shakes and soup meal replacements, I never want to go back to trying to deal with that again... And I do have some concern about what it will do to my long term health what with me having already broken my metabolism with other things in the past. It is good to know its there though and that many people have had great success with it.. I shall give that a go before i sign up for gastric banding surgery or something!

Gadgets:
I just read the MDA blog on earthing and all of the comments.. Interesting. Someone's idea that you could make an earthing sheet out of an electric blanket has me interested.. I won one at a quiz night once, but have never used it. I wonder if I still have it. Actually on second thoughts it wouldn't work because the wires in the blanket would be insulated.. I shall consider it some more before I decide whether to add a little bit more crazy to the mix

some of those MDA forum posters are total wankers, aren't they? Mark was all balanced and curious, and then you have all these other people (who are already following what everyone else considers to be a whack job lifestyle, lets remember) jumping in and insisting that the science isn't right... "I have a PDH in physics, and a masters in electrical engineering" oh, really.

i did have a play with my TENS unit at 8hz for a while, but didn't notice a difference... Probably needs tombe for longer, or more regularly, or to have controlled all my contexts  for it to me more precise as a test though.. By the way, these things (TENS-transcutaneous electrical neural stimulator) are magic for pain relief-no idea if it has anything to do with electrons or whatever, but musculoskeletal pain is sorted out really fast. (And yeah, apparently people DO use them as a BoB!)

I haven't gotten around to finding DH's sound and light machine that pulses Schumann frequency of light and sound into your head, but I will give it a shot when I find it.

Today:
the fish odyssey continues...
for breakfast, I ate 250g tuna with ginger, chilli and lime. 'twas ok even if it was in sunflower oil. Certainly it was a fast BAB. made sure to add a good dollop of CO to my coffee and lasted through lunch with just a little twinge around 2pm which vanished in a few minutes. Usually I'm starving by the time I get home at 4pm, but today i was good till 5.

Cooked lamb chops with fresh rosemary for DD and leftover breakfasts, and ate a couple of pieces of fish (it was frozen, in a packet with some kind of sauce of evil on it (soy, some kind of thickener guar gum... All artificial etc) it was edible though, even tasty, but next time I'll look for something less full of plastic coating. I had it with a big fat spinach salad covered in avocado oil and lemon with cheese and avocado and tomato. That was also tasty, and there is some left for tomorrow! I figure I have to start somewhere with fish, and the frozen prepackaged shit just might get me acclimatised to the fish concept, so I am going with that and assuming some is better than none, even if its nothing resembling 'optimal'

...speaking of optimal, I have had occasion several times this last week to use the word optimal in some very professional sounding reports. I have this little imagining that someone will read the word and know what we REALLY mean when we say optimal, and it will be like I have added an in-joke just for them...

For the first time since starting this thing (afaicr) I am feeling peckish now. Probably the fat free processed evilness of the fish sauce. Damn. I went out of my way to add extra fats to dinner too. \*edit\* actually, I think I felt this one night when I had to stay up late and do some colour sensitive photo editing\*

I speaking of crazy.. I had to open a new jar of CO whilst I was cooking, (does anyone else love the fact that if you spill it on you when you're pouring it out of the jar you just rub it into your skin?) and the oil had spilt and filled up the plastic that seals the jar for shipping. DH &DD stood around watching me try to work out a way to not coat the kitchen in CO. I ended up slathering it all over myself which normally would have been fine, except I was already well moisturised thankyou, and I was standing in a hot kitchen with the cooking.. I got so incredibly, uncomfortably whiningly hot and sticky I had to air condition myself under the vent as cold as it would go! Hilarity (for my family anyway) ensued. God help me when there is menopause..

next I did some CFB time without the belly icing because the ice burn is still scabby and tender (yah, more crazy-DH was asking if it has turned black yet.. Yes, gangrene belly... Maybe it will fall off!) and now my regime of madness is complete, except for going to bed soon.

Yesterday I spent several hours sitting on the grass, and went to bed at 9pm.. Slept all the way through till 5 but was really groggy on waking.. I could have slept another hour I think. I haven't felt that stumbly in the morning for ages. I wonder what that was about.

No parental dinner tonight because my dad has ruptured an Achilles' tendon and was waiting on the orthopaedic surgeon's pleasure. I convinced him that ice might prevent him from needing surgery, but since it is in a cast its a bit hard to ice the injury, all he can do is ice above the knee. It will be interesting to see what the surgeon has to say.

Oh my Science! some days I just rabbit on like a three year old on red cordial.. If you made it to the end, congratulations.. Or commiserations!

### ashryn — 2013-01-31T14:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73448>

Not so many words today, ate lamb chops and spinach and avocado for BAB no lunch as usual, and the last of the plastic coated fish, with the sauce scraped off (duh.. Why didn't I think of that yesterday?) also finished off the sea-shepherds pie, and more spinach salad.
Got my CT on, ice packs have almost melted, i'm ready for some proper immersion! pool goes in in 13 days.. then watch me go! (i hope)

and the days are getting shorter, but no cooler just yet, not that it has been a hot summer thus far, but still, too hot when I'm looking for cold.

I'm missing the beach, must make sure i get down there this weekend.
I'm also thinking if giving some HIIT a go.. Just body weight stuff for now, and maybe for just one day a week to see if it helps. Is it time? Or you reckon I keep on being lazy for a couple more weeks just to be sure? I'm not dying for exercise or anything, but it'd be cool to see some more muscles appear.

Yes, yes, I know.. Gt get labs done then decide where I'm at. I shall try to remember to make an appointment tomorrow. Honestly, I just don't think about it during the day.. I am busy thinking about work things.

So as a minimum we want D, DHEA and HS CRP. How long do I stop supplementing with D AND DHEA before I test? Should I stop all supps?
I will also get hormone and lipid panels done. Not sure about thyroid.. I guess if its available and she's willing to refer me, I will. Anything I've missed?

### ashryn — 2013-02-02T15:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73781>

Finally remembered to book an appointment with my Doc. Yay.
feeling a bit bloated in the tummy the last couple of days.. Need to go back to basics I think.. I ate some 85% chocolate over the same time I was feeling bloated for, and tomatoes in my salad. I have been eating chillies as well. All of those things I avoided completely at the beginning, so I shall do so again for the next week or so and see how I go.

Went to the beach this morning - no sharks! It was so breathtakingly lovely. I am going again tomorrow. Every time I forget how excellent the ocean makes me feel.

Cooked up some more pot pies... A batch with beef mince and bacon, and one with diced pOrk and bacon. Both have a couple of eggs beaten with milk to create a bit of a gravy with the fat. Cauliflower mash on top with cheese 15 mins in the oven and yum!

Now if I could come up with some kind of pastry substitute with no carbs, I'll be a happy girl.

Did some kitchen chemistry last night to try to make magnesium oil with Epsom salts.. Mostly I just made a mess. And then is had some trouble staying asleep... Possibly too much stimulation from the mag? I just went old Skool with it today and stuck 1kg in a shallow cold bath and got as much of it on me as possible. Now I am quite relaxed and can't wait for it to be time for bed.

Ate cauliflower and beef pie for breakfast, some hard boiled eggs for lunch and finished off the extra dark chocolate (not buying any more) ate a pork cauliflower pie for dinner. Possibly these things aren't quite making it to the required amount of protein.. There's probably only 40g in each one... I should put the recipe into paleptrack and find out i suppose. But I didn't get hungry. Perhaps I'll add a tin o tuna to BAB tomorrow to make sure.

Going on the hunt for mag chloride tomorrow -the non health food ripoff version.

Thinking I should have a go at the autoimmune protocol for a while to see if that helps with weightless.. And maybe I should look up what I was eating when I first started compared to now to see what is different.

I enjoyed today's blog, and found the global coherence project information really interesting. That's going to be my next new interest..

oh, I went shopping today and bought not one but two bikinis.. (Because.. We will have a pool, and if I can't get my guts out in my own backyard, then where can I?

also, I bought some shirts and underwear all another size down... That was pretty liberating.

### kathylu — 2013-02-03T17:20:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearly-the-scales-are-broken.5324/post-73954>

Coconut Almond piecrust

3/4 cup sifted coconut flour
3/4 cup almond flour (or FINELY chopped almond meal)
3 eggs
1 tbsp honey (optional)
5 tbsp of melted butter or coconut oil
1/4 tsp salt

Mix well and roll out on parchment paper. The crust is fragile and the paper makes it easier to transfer the crust to a pie plate. However, if it breaks, just repair by patting with your fingers. Works well for sweet or savory fillings.

If the baking time is longer than 20 minutes, cover the edges with foil to keep them from browning too much.
