)

Charlotte.. just WOW. I would love you to live to 120!!! That is what I am aiming for too, btw..lol love how one can feel just younger and younger even if one gets older! Most days I do feel like a child somehow.. much better than in my teenager years for sure. Weird.. even if I am much older now!

Caroline.. too old...? Mom is turning 61 and she goes every morning in the icehole, dad is 65 and he goes too (most days..lol) but he always goes when he is in the sauna, 3 times for dipping in the icehole in about one hour!). Caroline I guess we never get too old.. maybe we need to do it a bit carefully in the beginning for sure, but huh.. then, life all of a sudden is so fun!

I am not always feeling like going in my hole either. But I just go out and tell myself "just for a second". No need to stay any longer, but you MUST get clean (as I have forbidden myself to use the shower..lol) and then I just go and say loud.. oh come on, just DO it! I am just DOING it (and I say the doing really loud) and then I step in. I never regret it! I am my own health couch all the time. I sometimes get lazy.. eat too much cooked or not the optimal stuff and then I need to talk to myself. Do you want to feel better? Yes? Then start eating everything raw! And I do and feel better so fast!It is all very easy when one knows what to do.

The bad hair day that was mentioned here.. that is so cool. I used to have bad hair days all the time.. Now, I still have thin hair that always look terrible before I use my hair straightener. But now it is shinier and it keeps the form much better! It really is a difference! IDK if it is all the seaweeds I am drinking and eating or what.. but I like it! I am not doing anything else different. I still wash almost daily with ghassul mud, and use my hair dryer too.. as I have done for years.
I cannot wait how my hair will look in one year.. I guess great!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-12T08:14:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-70380>

> **caroline said**
>
> Inger - you say things so beautifully .... you always give me so much inspiration! Thankyou. Have your parents always done the ice hole thing? or is that your superb influence?? I really can't wait to see you when you grow up
>
> I do intend to swim in your icehole one day

This quote really touched me this AM.......it speaks to another human being being touched by another who had announced her presence........by painting their own picture and running their own race. It is very Starfish like. You can see it in the post before you even feel it............

Do you have to see IT, before you feel IT? Presence is the result of confidence, and it can be felt, even before it is ever expressed to the world. The confidence comes from knowledge of self, and other information absorbed due to a willingness to learn, explore, and discover, then share. When the knowledge, sharing, and energy come together in one person, it is presence. It says here I am. Your work begins to take on a certain gravity or magnetism. New discoveries and new opportunities begin to be pulled to you. Things become easier for you because of the energy you share with others. Presence is more than just being there. It is not just about being physically present in a certain place. It is about who you are, your character, values, knowledge, your essence, your beliefs, your emotional state of being, your ability to connect with those around you and your confidence. With it, you begin to create your inner masterpiece by continually painting your canvas and others canvas, with big bold colors and with large brushstrokes. Yes, it is time for all of us now to paint.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-12T11:08:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-70389>

This is a really a beautiful picture of a fully authentic way of being....fully engaged and peacefully confident in who you are. Presence without ego. You know the phrase that is often used ironically "he or she 'gifted' us with their presence"....well I think that holds a very significant truth and when we are around people creating themselves as is described above...we really are receiving a gift.

I mean, if I listed all the positive health changes I've had from paleo, it would take six pages, but it's the tweaking based on the Optimal Living protocols that have really made some major headway in some long-term issues. So I'll start!
>
> Leptin Reset - Ended my decade-long battle with out-of-control binge eating disorder. The war is 99.5% won now. Removed ALL cravings for carbohydrates 99.9% of the time, and made eating a totally non-emotional decision, based solely on what's nourishing for me, 99.9% of the time. The BAB has saved my life.
>
>
> Labwork - I've learned about my hypothyroidism, autoimmune disease, likely Celiac's, premature entry into perimenopause, and much more
>
> Educational Consults - I've learned how to interpret all those labs, and what specifically I can do to change the problems I have, and how to evaluate progress going forward. I've gained the courage to overcome my med-hatred and get on some desiccated thyroid, finally!
>
> ...
>
> I especially am interested in seeing what changes y'all can tie \*specifically\* to
> \- switching to unprocessed foods
> \- switching to paleo/primal diet (Wolf/Sisson) as recommended by Dr. K)

> \- recommended labwork
> \- educational consults

> \- seafood
> \- dark
> \- mito protocol
> \- EMF protocol

I'm really looking for \*specific\* inspiration to help me focus on the bits where I'm weak. I'm donig great on diet, labwork, seafood, etc., but need to get pumped up about getting serious about lights after dark, so (other than getting sleepy at a reasonable hour), what changes have you seen that you know started when you got serious about lights after dark? (And you know it was the light b/c you didn't also start 5 other protocols at the same time) That kind of thing...

### caroline — 2013-01-31T01:16:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-our-success-stories.5335/post-73401>

for me - the really big thing is - no electronics after dark. I sleep so much better. If i have the tv on for awhile I just close my eyes and listen and no laptop in the evening. If I read on my ipad in the evening I will have a terrible sleep. I also was using some liquid stevia in my green tea and I will be up 3 or 4 times during the night - going to the bathroom. It is like my kidneys are irritated or something. If I don't use it I will be up only once and sometimes not at all.

### Thor — 2013-01-03T23:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-69255>

.
not offend anyone but just wondering any thoughs about Dr Doug McGuff, MD ? Sure seems very knowledge about Paleo & how it ties into exercise. I could be wrong but he seems on point.

To me he seems on point to exercise correctly - train to gain. His book is called "body science" - how your phenotype is expressed in exercise. IL-15 gene is one type and how it shows in your body type. Bio-chemistry and how global metabolic conditioning matters. Dr Mcguff, MD - really gives a deep geek lecture. Here's a video to explain this better than I can.

\[video=youtube\_share;2PdJFbjWHEU\]http://youtu.be/2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]

Tom

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-01-23T10:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-72494>

> **Zorica Vuletic said**
>
> I only want strength gains without size. It's best to be un assuming...then when needed you can whip out your powers. lol

Don't worry about that ZV, you simply are not equipped to get size. Just like if you took steroids your testes wouldn't shrink. So far, he has the most accurate descriptor of what exercise actually does to the male body, and most of the principles can be transferred to how it will work with Women as well. It stops short of exploring/explaining the additional and subsequent reactions with female chemistry.

There is nothing out there now, short of unreadable scientific publications, that does a great job of exploring exercise and female physiology.

The net result is most of what he says does work the same in women, it's just that there is OTHER stuff that happens with women and it's not clear what the downhill/unintended consequence is.

ZV if you are eating epi paleo you will not build too much muscle. Only when you start doing drastic things to re-engineer all your sex hormones and brain signals can women even put on bulky muscle. Compact and useful muscle fibres in conjunction with female hormones is tight and compact. It's a whole different texture than bodybuilder bulk.

### hellojtm — 2013-01-31T00:42:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-73397>

I followed the BBS workout for several months a couple of times. I never gained any size but I always progressed in strength. I'll make a couple of edumacated guesses about why that is, order of importance.

1\. I wasn't eating enough to gain size (independent of macronutrients).
2\. The workout is fairly low volume, unless you do even lighter weights. Higher volume would be more anabolic, IMO.
3\. The workout is infrequent, giving you many days of "extra" rest before the next stimulus. This may favor your fast twitch muscle generation, but
4\. If you want to get bigger you probably shouldn't worry about fast/slow twitch muscle, with a couple weeks off you will start to express the fast twitch.

All that said, I did gain 2.5lbs of muscle on the program (per DEXA), while losing 17.5lbs of fat (from paleo lifestyle). By going to this program I probably reduced my gym time from 3 hours to 25 minutes a week. I was going to do it again starting in January and simply add a ton of calories to the equation. In fact, I started it, but a friend convinced me to transition to a more traditional strongman method including squats and deadlifts. I like the BBS and have taught it to a couple of co-workers with varying results. I'll always have it in my back pocket, but for now I really like the feeling of picking up heavy weights.

Would be interested if anyone HAD gained size on this program, with no other alterations than overeating.

best,
jesse

### shah78 — 2014-07-17T19:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-134756>

The idea that only strength gains , not mass gains can be derived by a combo of BBS + low carb paleo+CT is inaccurate.. I've spent approx.46 hours in the gym over the last 40 months and gained over twentyfive lbs of mucle while losing around ten lbs of fat. I'm am 58 yrs old now and recovering from CFS/FM/Hypothyroid/adrenal fatigue. . ...My new biohack it going to lower the time in the gym for the next three months to forty minutes a month and add lots more saunas keeping my Cold Therapy at ten hours a week. ....Essentially, no one in the" real world" believes me.  Even people who watch me work out and then spend 90% of my gym time, siting in the sauna, think I come back some other day and workout again. But no, just more saunas. Hilarious. I feel like Jed Clampett, walking around Beverly Hills .or driving around in the "truck"., except I have Doug Mcguff riding upfront where Jethro usually sits, and Jack Kruse is sitting in Granny"s rocker. Pretty remarkable and pretty funny.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-07-20T02:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-doug-mcguff-md-anyone-follow-him.5336/post-134955>

Centration is the ability for the body to be maximally congruent and balanced
Ie joint centration

When we observe the infant in its first year of development we see the installation of all motor patterning with full centration
Ie saggital, ipsilateral, contralateral in that order.
This is why i always laugh that people in gyms constantly load the saggital plane, the most basic of all patterns.

# Thread: The love of your life ....... part deux...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-01-04T02:39:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69262>

Do you think it is possible to have more than one ........ "The Love of your Life"?

On one hand ...It seems like it should be possible I guess... but ... on the other hand.....I don't really think it is....
Thoughts???

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T02:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69263>

I think it is possible, because we grow and change over time, I am not the same person I was 10 years ago at all.. Then again, if the love of your life grows with you and you both encourage the changes in one another, I think it is also possible to have one LoYL for your whole life... If you have one, why would you look for another? I suspect if for whatever reason one love has gone, that another may appear... Sometimes they may appear before the LoYL has physically gone but maybe he/she is spiritually gone from you.. And I also think LoYL2 may appear to teach you what you have forgotten to love in yourself, and may stick around just until you have re learned the lesson. I think lots of couples who go through an affair and come out the other side still being each other's LoYL are experiencing this.

At least that's how it looks to me..

### caroline — 2013-01-04T03:15:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69266>

I think when someone is "The Love of Your Life" then you have been growing and changing and learning and loving and laughing and crying together and supporting each other and putting them first.

### caroline — 2013-01-04T03:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69267>

If you have a chance ..... Dr. K. says some lovely things about his wife Sandy and the art/joy of being married .... on Twitter.

### ashryn — 2013-01-04T03:19:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69268>

True, but sometimes when our brain gets broken and we don't care for ourselves properly, then we also don't care for them properly..

### caroline — 2013-01-04T04:01:28+0200

Yes ...so true. Dr. K. talks about that in the original thread .... you first have to learn to love yourself ......

### caroline — 2013-01-04T04:05:05+0200

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### caroline — 2013-01-06T20:06:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69659>

very cute

Are you trying to tell us something??

### Charlotte — 2013-01-07T11:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69735>

To be honest, I am not sure, how similar or different we are from other animals, but when I look at the many kinds of different animals, there are surely differences also about their "love life". The grey geese for example chose their partner and then they stay together for the rest of their life like the albatross also, then there is the females spider who tries to kill the male after the act. Then there is the bowerbird who only builds a nest to impress the female, but never intends to live with her there, in the world of the hyenas, it is the females who reign, sometime they even develop male genitals. Most whales are downright promiscuous like penguins, shimpanses and some other species. And lions kill the offspring of their former rival, so the female can get pregnant soon again.
So I think like in nature, also humans are different. There are people who meet and stay together for all their life, many just can never be faithful (male and female alike), some can raise their offspring together, but that is it, then there are others who only like the same gender, and so on.
In nature, all is possible and actually happens.
Before the age of Romantisism in Europe marriage was not intended to be a romantic venture but a way to keep wealth together, to make sure, girls get married off into a good family and the man marries a woman who can represent with him their social status, in short, they were marriages of convenience. Some of these marriages were quite successful and many "arranged" themselves around the inevitable.
How it actually happened in the paleolithic age I do not know, I even do not know if such a thing as marriage even existed. I tend to believe it did not. The act was performed and the child was a consequence of it which was then reared as part of the clan.
I realize that my view is a little detached as I am not a romantic person and I do not intend to destroy anybodies romantic feelings about the topic, but I think a little less romanticism is quite healthy. I tend to view this topic unemotional or prosaic.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T11:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69741>

i like that view......different than most american women.......but refreshing.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-07T17:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69777>

> **Most whales are downright promiscuous like penguins.**

This does nothing to address Caroline's original question, but furthers the point of diversity among animals. Turns out, not all penguins are promiscuous. Here in New Zealand (on the Otago Peninsula) there is a sub species of penguins called Yellow Eyed Penguins that don't live elsewhere. You can visit their colony and view them from a distance. They are observed, only, not interacted with or influenced by the ornithologists who document them. These penguins are strictly monogamous. In fact, there is a male homosexual pair there who have been together for years. Several years into their relationship, they adopted a chick whose mother had died and have successfully raised this baby to adulthood together. Life is fascinating!

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T20:02:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69794>

> **KiwiLauren said
>
>
> > Most whales are downright promiscuous like penguins.
>
> **This does nothing to address Caroline's original question, but furthers the point of diversity among animals. Turns out, not all penguins are promiscuous. Here in New Zealand (on the Otago Peninsula) there is a sub species of penguins called Yellow Eyed Penguins that don't live elsewhere. You can visit their colony and view them from a distance. They are observed, only, not interacted with or influenced by the ornithologists who document them. These penguins are strictly monogamous. In fact, there is a male homosexual pair there who have been together for years. Several years into their relationship, they adopted a chick whose mother had died and have successfully raised this baby to adulthood together. Life is fascinating!

Dolphins on the other hand go in for pack rape...
Google it if you dont believe me.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T02:52:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69821>

Agreed, even within one species there are vast differences, as not all species of geese are like the grey geese.
But I do think that it ties in very well with Carolines initial questions which was if it is possible to have more than one. Humans, like animals have different behaviour. So my point was, that like in the animal kingdom in the human kingdom this is also possible.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T16:11:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-love-of-your-life-part-deux.5337/post-69906>

Re: Charlotte's thoughtful reply ... ya'll need to read "Sex at Dawn" ... a real eye opener and definitely food for thought.

As to the OP ... yes I believe you can have more than one "love of your life." We're all different and we love different people in different ways.

The first man I truly fell in love with and whom I thought I would marry, broke my heart into so many pieces I never really recovered. He was the first "love of my life" and is still. My DH is also the love of my life but the love I feel for him is completely different and not just because we've been together for (holy cripes) 39 years.

Open your heart to not only life but to all possibilities and you will discover many profound truths.

# Thread: More on Factor X:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-factor-x.5338/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T11:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-factor-x.5338/post-69297>

Mammals make body heat.......Why did this help them after a bollide hit the Yucatan 65-67 million years ago?

The Evolution of Body Heat?

When oxidized, the great majority of our food and stored energy goes to the production of HEAT. What governs this? It is multifactorial but adrenaline, thyroid, cortisol and mitochondria quality are just a few \[2\]. Active tissues contain more mitochondria. Heat makes us mammals and birds. We have hot bodies, precisely 37C for the great majority.

In the 'Hot Brain: Survival, Temperature, and the Human Body' the authors theorized that temperature gave us advantages over eukaryotic infections (yeast, fungal origins -- we are eukaryotic) which plagued bird/reptile species which were not armed with high 37C temperatures or fever-inducing capabilities \[3\]. It is a very interesting theory. Control of thermoregulation (heat loss v. heat gain) is believed to have evolved in the brain of therapsids. Our sinuses are larger. Mammalian brains have a Circle of Willis where 4 arteries (internal carotids and vertebral arteries) provide a complete internal brain circulation with collaterals, such that despite blockage of one or more of the 4 major arteries circulation in the brain and to the body remains intact. Unfortunately only 25-33% of us have a 'perfect' classic circle of Willis; others have degrees of narrowing or asymetry in certain areas or another. Photos courtesy: Hot Brain, pp. 44, 142.

Recently a microbiologist, Casadevall, from Albert Einstein had the same theory that the rise of mammals can be attributed to 'endothermy and homeothermy \[which\] are thought to contribute to mammalian resistance to mycosis by creating a thermal exclusionary zone that inhibits most fungal species. The remarkable resistance of mammals to mycotic diseases is probably a combination of a vertebrate immune system, with both innate and adaptive arms, and elevated body temperatures... The currently favored hypothesis for the demise of dinosaurs and end of the age of reptiles is a bolide impact approximately 65 million year ago with the possibility that other events, such as increased volcanism, contributed to disrupting the cretaceous ecosystem. That ecological calamity was accompanied by massive deforestation, an event followed by a fungal bloom, as the earth became a massive compost. Although one cannot know which spores were present at the time, the likelihood that pathogenic fungi existed at the K-T boundary is enhanced by the finding that the potential for pathogenicity probably arose independently several times in evolution...'\[3\]

'Although we do not know the timeline for the recovery of the planet climate, it is estimated that photosynthesis was shut down for 6 months and climate cooling persisted for at least 9 years, and the occurrence of a fungal bloom sufficient to have left fossil evidence implies that surviving animals were exposed to massive numbers of fungal spores. The darkened skies and cooler temperatures that accompanied the K-T cataclysm would have shielded the sun and reduced the ability of ectothermic creatures such as reptiles to induce fevers by insolation, a necessary activity for protection against fungal diseases. Hence, it is reasonable to posit that ectothermic creatures unable to induce behavioral fevers and in weakened states from environmental stress would have been at a severe disadvantage relative to small mammals with their innate thermal exclusionary zones for fungal growth. Further complicating the situation for reptiles is that eggs can be vulnerable to fungal attack, whereas mammalian progeny would be protected in placentae.'\[3\]- From Dr. B. G.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-04T15:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-factor-x.5338/post-69327>

pretty cool... so we're endothermic - due to the ability of our mitochondria's ability to produce heat from the uncoupling of bonds when ATP is transformed to ADP and energy is released. This energy management system allows eutherian mammals to fight off the fungal explosion in the environment through fevers, thus eliminating our susceptibility to those organisms. Three questions for you: so, how does the Cold Environment of the post KT event world leverage Heat Shock Proteins? is this part of the process or is it a side process as a result of the Cold Environment? How does the epigenetics and circadian mismatches that Factor X leverages make us more vulnerable to similar organisms - such as MRSA or candida overgrowth? It's not just the carbs that's contributing to this vulnerability - our environment is also at play with regard to our susceptibility, correct? Thus our mitochondrial in-efficiency, as a result of electron loss/leakiness through consumption of carbs is limiting our ability to fight infection through endothermic regulation.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T15:51:04+0200

all food puts at risk........cold is primordial. CT 2 all over again. 65 million years ago was a reversion back to primordial. Life has faced this before........the proof is found in our ability to adapt back to the mean........heat shock proteins came much later in evolutionary time to allow us to live away from the mean.......got it?

### PrimalPam — 2013-01-04T17:16:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69349>

Timely, because my niece & her mom just decided to go Paleo. From a lifetime of SAD, they are embracing it. I brought over all my Paleo books, plus a list of websites, Yes foods, No foods, and Limit Foods.

Under Dairy, of course, I put "Just say No". They said that in their reading, they read in some places certain dairy = yes, some place, no. I referred them to the Dr. Kruse forum and said (I promise I really said this!), whenever there are arguments on paleo forums about whether a food is good or bad, I use Dr. Kruse's advice because I feel he's studied it more extensively than all of them combined.

So they can do all the discussing back and forth; I have not had a glass of milk in a year.

### maggie — 2013-01-04T17:39:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69357>

Well for me Dr K nailed it with this: "If there is any evidence of mitochondrial dysfunction I think the best course of action based upon what we do know is to avoid the pitfalls and get the fat soluble vitamins from seafood skins, eyes, and other animal sources." and this: "When you see this many people clustered with neuro immune diseases and BCM 7 you must look why."

Really. If I'm choosing between the increased possibility of a brain tumor, MS, ALS, etc. and ditching dairy, I'll find something else to eat.

### freesia — 2013-01-04T19:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69371>

After being dairy free for a while I experimented with raw cheese early last year, but figured out I could smell something weird on my skin after eating it.

My n = 1: The only way I tolerate dairy is to (deep breath) obtain raw milk, separate the cream, ferment the cream 24 hours..chill...then make cultured butter, and make that cultured butter into cultured ghee.

I made the scientif....I mean outlier brain...observation this is some kind of a square peg for a round hole situation...and decided not to bother.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T20:01:12+0200

### hellojtm — 2013-01-04T21:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69383>

I think your point about context is a major one. Glad to see it articulated. I think you may have beat Mat and Robb out of that thread with pure verbiage though

I don't think you guys differ too much except for the context.

My opinion means nothing but I think there is a lot of importance to the idea that something in your blood is bad for you, but how it got there is even worse for you. Matt's example of low levels of the peptide that dissolves BCM7 in combo with a leaky gut would put you in a situation where BCM7 is in the blood stream. This is enough to imply nobody should really be ingesting it unless you are regularly testing all your peptide levels and gut permeability, etc... I can't see the low likelihood of one having that biological situation be a good argument that we should keep drinking it. I see all the grain proteins in the same light.... it seems like if your gut is tightly sealed and your general immune system is not taxed then you'll probably deal with it fine, but we know leaky gut exists with some unknown myriad of causes and we know that brings the grain proteins into contact with the blood stream. Not to mention the immune response that must be going on in the gut at all times with those "foreign bodies" (see V's enterolab, i.e.).

OK, this is getting long but the point I \_really\_ wanted to get at was that the context door swings both ways, too often we (and by we I mean me) turn into hypochondriacs when we find out new information. Have to look at our context, if it's summer and I'm 11% body fat and feeling great I'm eating fruit!!

take care Dr. K.

jesse

### kathylu — 2013-01-04T22:21:24+0200

I'm not. Since casomorphins are biologically active peptides with opioid effects, of course people are defending dairy strongly. They are addicted. You see it from that first pair bond formation with momma (maybe that's why mother's milk has morphine like effects?) to adults who can give up any food except dairy. I also think that dairy decreases selective gut permeability...useful in infants who must be able to absorb huge protein molecules in colostrum in order to receive functional immunity from their mother...but definitely detrimental in adults. Yup, no dairy after three years of age makes the most sense. Another example that our evolved brains get in our way...humans are the only animal that routinely consumes dairy past infancy.

I recently saw a story on how cheese was "invented". People realized they could drink cows' milk and therefore get substantial "nutritious" calories, but they had to separate it and use only the curd, as people started getting so sick. Lactose intolerance.

When people ask what's wrong with dairy, I always ask why humans are the only animal on the planet that drinks another animal's milk? I mean, doesn't that at least sound like a bad idea? You never see, in nature, one animal suckling off another. It's just not natural.

But we've been so ingrained with all the milk campaigns and how it "does a body good". If you consider mucous production, AI diseases, and gut permeability "doing a body good".

### Inger — 2013-01-05T15:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69459>

I am going to put my two cents in too...

THANK YOU... Thank you for pointing to this facebook thread/discussion. Art de Vany's answer made something say "kilck" in my brain. Gotta love the truth.

I have been indulging in raw, organic, grassfed butter from the mountains of Austria. It sounds good, or? I thought too.
I ate a lot, maybe 200 gram or more a day. In my coffee in my food.

Then a few days ago (after about 1-2 weeks of raw butter feast) I realized something is amiss. My heart feels not right. Believe me I have a weak heart and I feel it there at first mostly. It feels a tiny bit like inflamed. I thought it must be because of emotional stress I had last weeks but then.. thinking more about it I come to the conclusion it must be the butter.
I am going to put the rest into the freezer and save for special occasions. My mitochondria are not optimal yet.

And if someone as healthy as Art de Vany is abandoning it too.. it is crystal clear to me where I have to go. Zero dairy for me from now on. I have to get strict. The emotional issues are challenging enough and I need all the power I can get.

Wow. I can't believe how much it helped me to read this thread! Right now. With my recent issues.
I did not even thought I will read it at first as I thought it would be nothing for me. I have barely time to read all the new posts anyway. But I did. Serendipity?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-05T16:11:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69479>

My husband's motto is 'the truth is always friendly.' We all need to take responsibility for our health and choices. If we want to consume dairy, we should do so in face of the evidence about its significant risks, hearing all thinking on the topic. I think that's what Dr Kruse endeavours to do... pull no punches, tell the truth as he knows it at the time, and let us decide what choices to make.

Not only do people not want to be made uncomfortable, they also like to feel superior. So Paleo isn't just 'good enough' it's also 'better than most folks' and that allows people to coast on bad advice.

I live in a country where dairy is king. The politics of the dairy industry are not pretty. Money drives this the same way it drives doctors prescribing statins and mammograms. Milk powder is used throughout the food industry; the biggest consumers of milk powders are infants and the formula industry alone will keep the propaganda about cow's milk alive.

I really appreciate reading the discussion and hearing the insights. It may make life more complicated or difficult, but it also presents me with a greater freedom. Thanks to all of you for your comments.

### duceswild557@hotmail.com — 2013-01-05T16:56:59+0200

Well, I think that Mat Lalonde brought up some great points but I also think that you brought up some great points. I think that what you guys have in common is that you both want calcium and phosphorus but disagree on the source. Mattie ole' boy says why not milk? while you seem to be more in favor of sourcing it from possibly shellfish. At the end of the day I don't think anyone would argue with you that dairy is superior to fish/shellfish but I do think it is at least somewhat warranted to say that raw grass fed high fat dairy is likely okay for some people. Remember that the Sherpas eat straight butter while they climb Mt. Everest (from the podcast You did on fatburningman.com).

I also appreciate the difference in approach between you guys. You see more clinical cases where people with compromised health come to see you while Mat sees people probably in moderate+ health. For somebody with a totally compromised gut or AI milk is probably goign to be a super NONO, which shellfish is probably a super YES, and that makes perfect sense. For somebody with nice muscle mass, low fat, low stomach fat and very active though different rules are definitely going to apply.

So when someone says milk is good or milk is bad it is absolutely 100% necessary to finish that sentence and say milk is good for WHO, or milk is bad for WHO. See what I mean? Almost half of the US population is obese now and 2/3 are overweight or obese, many of these people are VERY unhealthy and have serious issues. Dairy is probably going to hinder them from recovery. But that doesn't mean that milk would compromise the health of an active, muscular low bf athlete. Context is key here.

### Linz — 2013-01-08T10:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-69848>

Lauren
Pigs have a digestive system similar to humans so they are very unlikely to be fed an ideal diet. Cattle and sheep are herbivores and it is not difficult to feed them a grass/forage based diet which they convert to good quality fat and protein but pig diets are probably no better than SAD. I think they are much healthier living with outside access for light/temp variation/exercise/stress reduction etc but they need more than grass. In the past in the UK they were actually fed human food as swill - left over food from hotels/hospitals etc which was all boiled up together - but it is no longer allowed due to disease risk. Without looking up any details I expect the feed used now is a mixture of cereal, soy and various proteins and oils to give consistent weight gains with little attention to 06/3 ratio or other long-term health aspects (they have short lives). If we can't even feed ourselves properly ...

I eat pork and bacon (preferably outdoor reared) but have reservations and don't know where an ideal source would come from other than wild boar in its natural habitat foraging for acorns, nuts, grubs, truffles! Your wild caught from the mountains sounds perfect.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-26T08:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-robb-wolf-or-the-kracken-are-correct.5342/post-72713>

From what I've read BCM7 isn't something that forms in all milk. There is BCM7 in human milk but it isn't at all the same. BCM7 doesn't even form in milk from cows containing a genetic variation referred to as A2 with Proline at position 67 of the amino acid chain. A1 or A1/A2 mix genetics produce milk with Histadine at 67 which is what forms BCM7.

Getting a good source of only A2 milk might not be the easiest though. I've heard certain types of cows have more of one than the other such as Holstein is all A1 while Guernsey/Jersey have more of the A2 only. Of course the only way to know is to have a cow tested.

I really want to try good raw Jersey milk because I've heard of how great the nutrition is and what benefits it has. I've heard quite a bit of benefit can come from good clean milk. I don't think my ancestry would be bad at processing milk so I'd love to try some of the good stuff.

So I think with the less estrogen maybe that's why I simply don't get any cycle at all. (But I don't know if my estrogen is getting all built up and E dominance comes with a revenge...).

So I would like to know if anyone would have any ideas/suggestions for me if helping to treat an IGF-1 deficiency with \[natural/bio-I\] progesterone would help it or if there is some way to directly target the IGF 1. \*I've used colostrum before but I can't always afford it...and I am not sure if it does anything for my progesterone levels.

I have a doctor's appointment on Tues, but with my luck over the last few months I don't put much faith.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-04T16:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-69340>

There are lots of ways to support IGF-1.

1) Eat Epi-Paleo - break out the MHS's that Ingrid is so fond of!
2) Follow the Leptin Reset (BAB, and focus on making sure you eat at least 3hrs prior to going to bed)
3) after dusk use red lights to see, and wear blue blocker glasses after the sun goes down
4) orgasm before going to sleep - it helps get the Leptin, and Meletonin to your brain faster, and helps you release prolactin to support autophage at night.
5) when you wake up look directly into the rising sun - then have another orgasm - this will help you re-set your circadian cycle and raise your am cortisol and dopamine levels.
6) CT as much as possible. every day if you can swing it - as it raises both Pg, Testosterone, and Thyorid hormones and IGF-1. do it right after you orgasm in the am, and as soon as the sun goes down... it help sets the table for success.

when you talk to your doctor tomorrow - let him/her know you're looking for a partner to help you get on the road to optimal health and that you need them to help you navigate the stuff you don't understand. do you're own research and be prepared to discuss...

note - some people on the forum are using cycloset to help reset their circadian cycles, their am cortisol and dopamine levels, a side effect is that cycloset also raises IGF-1. Depending on your doc - and your situation you may find this might help you. But note, this isn't for everyone. once you find a doctor who will work with you - check out the cycloset thread and read up on it. then talk to your doctor.

Jan. 5:
I decided to try to cut some supplements b/c I have no idea if any of them are bad for me or not...? I am on day 3 of not drinking it just to experiment/see how I feel and/or how my sleep is.

I am so sorry you are struggling Zoretica! Some suggestions I do have that I think could help!

I would never heat the raw honey, is your coffee or tea warm when you put it in? IMHO the raw honey changes in what it does my body if I heat it... and is it truly raw? Could be better to use honey comb.. Then.. maybe if you are still so messed up, you would do better to ditch the honey. And the coffee. That is what I would do. Ditch the fruits too. I do very bad on fruits, only wild berries work well, from those I can eat as much as I want and have zero issues! I tried our own good old apples from the garden twice this fall and both times.. bloating and fungus on my foot. I would ditch the eggs too. If they are not 100% pastured. But they are not because we have chickens and in winter they lay no eggs if you do not give them powerfoods like grains and stuff.. I do not eat their eggs now, because they get chicken feed and I feel not good from the eggs now. I tried. In summer they are free outside and get no grains and then I felt fine from the eggs. So I would say eggs in winter is not a good idea if you are broken.

What if you eat seaweeds for veges only, and then only seafoods and offal and grassfed meat and fat? Would that work? Because some people have reactions to different vegetables and if you eat only seaweeds as vegetable you would get all you need for K2 and minerals! Try to get raw dried or fresh and eat them soaked or raw.

I would suggest to eat as much raw as you can. If you are capable, do 100%. The most healing foods in my experience... I bet you will feel different in just a day or two!
Make high meat too, it is very easy! If you want it to get ready fast, you can ferment it in room temp too.

Zoretica, to me wild foods are the most healing. So if you want to hear my opinion on mega fast recovery... eat all wild and raw foods... try to get wild greens and herbs too if they grow now where you live. If all is full of snow and ice you will do perfectly well with just what grows. Like game and seafood and such. Soon spring is here and then you can eat from the small greens that starts growing. Very healing in my experience. You do not need much, just a little here and there.
If you can get Chaga mushroom you could drink that as tea too. AKMan knows its health benefits well.. Tastes a bit like coffee too!

And yes.. that with naked tanning and light management and CT you already know.

I am giving up coffee (for now) NOT a permanant thing...NO WAY IN HELL. But I will give my body a break from it for now. Not that I blame coffee, only that I think it did not allow for the correct improvement. After a few days with no coffee I hate to say it, but I do feel that it lessens my bad body odour. I still have it (sad) but I don't think it's as strong, so if that's an encouragement to take a break from it, I don't know what is. I am drinking green tea instead, and I am actually enjoying it too.

I'm making some bacon and pork and seaweed in coconut oil. YUM! It smells so good.

I must say that today I felt a lot better at least in being able to stay awake during the day. In the morning after breakfast I was not crashed (not to the point I needed to sleep) and again after lunch I did not crash so hard and I even went out skating.

I will be super strict for January (at least) and I will track my progress. I have no idea if the Vitex is starting to kick in and that explains a little bit better energy level or not...who knows. At night my sleep does still suck though, in that I am very flushed and hot (but not sweating). I might wake up a few times in the night but (at least in the last few nights) not have to pee. I would LOVE for my darkness under my eyes to finally go away...FINALLY! I thought with each passing month that for sure they would get better, but they only got worse in the last two months.

I do have confidence though that with the increasing light levels it will only get better from here on out. \*cross fingers\*

Next Fall I don't know if I can be an advocate for 'cold and dark'...and I will try using a light box in the AM. We'll see and we'll see based upon my health by then.

Thanks all for your help!

I will just keep writing about my experiences and treat this as my journal.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-08T09:36:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-69841>

Yesterday and today I had a bbt of 36.8! That makes me very optimistic. I really really hope it stays like that. By the way I've not had a bbt that high since I started recording in JULY! (Maybe I had it before in my life time, but not when I was recording).

I have a few questions. I find it hard to sleep in until almost sunrise ~7:45am. Today I got up at 6:45am and also there was no 'sun' as in it's overcast. I still did a face dunk in ice water. (Helps to clear the sinus---yesterday I got rid of THREE kleenix worth of 'crap'). I ate my breakfast before the face dunk though b/c I felt hungry.

When I go to sleep I 'massage' myself and I find this really helps. I also do the same when I wake up (and I feel the natural urge to do so anyway). So probably a good sign from my body. When I tried it a few nights ago my hands and feet went from cold to warm so I was happy for that.

The night before last my sleep sucked (falling asleep was fine w/ massage) but I woke up maybe once or twice during the night (but not to go pee or anything). I even had sharp pains in my feet (the soles) and my calves felt heavy. Last night it was a lot better, fell asleep just as good, felt like I woke up during the night but no bathroom visit and no sharp pains.

I know Jack does not like 'fish oil', but I caved yesterday and I tried some Carlesons cod liver oil that my friend had. At lunch I felt that distinct 'poisen' or heavy eyes feeling. So I tried the CLO and instantly I was brightened for the rest of the afternoon. I went skating before having the CLO but after the CLO I went to the lake for the sunset and I felt like I could bend my legs properly and had the urge to run, so I did. (Not long distance, just a sprint).

This morning since I don't want to waste my eggs, I had three and I did not enjoy them. I had to eat some salmon and seaweed to help make me full. So, I will not eat eggs again until at least Spring, where I will experiment again (and seeing as how they would be fresher /natural/ eating better grass etc), it is something for me to file away. I also took TWO teaspoons of CLO this morning b/c I just felt like I needed it.

Thanks again folks!!

O, also what was nice, last night while going out (wear blue blocker glasses) I went to choir, but noone was there---so instead I jammed on the piano instead. hehe. Good times! Then read before bed (I couldn't do that for a long while b/c my eyes felt too heavy 'for like the last 4 months'.

I hope I'm doing things better/right this time around. Because technically I started this all last year almost this time, but more like February with the CT protocols. I guess I didn't understand my own situation as well as I do now, and even now I still fly blind with crappy basic non-sensitive testing. I honestly can't afford fancy tests of ANY kind and I'm struggling with additional supplements (that I feel a benefit from). Clearly in the last few months it taught me that even super good epi-paleo was not enough for me (somehow).

Also I am familiar about the liver, T3, vit A and conversion to LDL cholestorol which should be converted to pregnenalone then to progesterone. So I am also wanting to target my liver health to help support this. This has direct impact on thyroid. I want my liver, ovaries and thyroid to be in tip top shape!!

\*\* p.s. I have a question: Does anyone know what Jack thinks for women once their ovaries are finished producing the hormones and the rest coming from adrenals? Does he advocate BHRT, or does he believe it can be healthily maintained with same focus on liver function with NAC, glutothione and/or silymarin etc.? I can theorize that if one does not have an estrogen dominance problem to begin with (or they got rid of it 'years ago', maybe it can be done this way? I always think about menopause even though I'm only 27 just because of my troubles with PMS and/or lack of menses... So I don't know if I have a genetic component that makes my ovaries a little less 'vigorous'...?

Thanks.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-08T23:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-70007>

Ok, it appeared I FINALLY got my period TODAY!

I ask if it's worth it though? Here's why:

\-I don't want my own kids (I want to adopt instead. I was adopted so I would like to give another child the fortunate experience I was able to have by being adopted by loving parents).
\-If being 'regular' just means super PMS EVEN when eating healthy...is that even normal? In September and even October I felt much better and more agile and WAYYYYYYYYYY better mood/attitude and I was not menstruating. I felt a little concerned only because females are 'supposed' to have 28 day cycle. Ok, well I'd rather be without one and have better health than with one and feel like slug, slow, weight gain SHITTY mood, can' concentrate, etc. etc. etc.
\-Can I just force myself into menapause? Seriously...but I mean without the E dominance of course (because that would just be asking for cancer).

So, I don't know if I should be happy or not about this.

p.s. In July on my last cycle it was very terrible for the few weeks leading up to it, BUT on the day it arrived it was SUPER sweet. Today's is not and I felt very sick.

p.p.s. I will look into ox bile (again).

I had beef marrow soup with seaweed-yum! For dinner I just ate the same thing and added sardines (not cooked sardines). I took extra zinc+b6+potassium today because I felt water retentioned.

The ONLY good thing I like about this so far compared to the last few months is that I at least got warm to HOT hands and feet. A nice change from cold hands and feet. \*I am pretty sure that December my body was trying hard to have a period too. I had all the PMS of this month but NO freaking 'detox'. So annoying. So at least this month I will have the detox part.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-11T15:50:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-70324>

It's day 4 of my flow and woke up with bbt of 36.4. I don't know if it's going to get super low again---I really hope with my Vitex this area can be evened out etc.

I started taking some ox bile yesterday. (500 mg/capsule) We'll see how that goes. I don't know if I notice a difference in bloating yet...but I did see something from MamaG where she posted after a week that bloating was not gone yet but that her stools were sinking. Now after a few months I see that she says her bloating is completely gone now. My stools are sinking so that's good.

Today though I honestly feel constipated again. When I woke up my tongue was even more swollen (than it normally is). In the afternoon I felt really bad cramps where it was very lower abdomen and into the anus. Very uncomfortable and painful. It seems to have subsided a bit in the cramping but I feel that my BM is not easy. I feel like stuff is stuck (but only in the lower colon part). Also with the pain it makes my hands, but more so my feet (or toes) go like ice block cold or a bit of a numb sensation.

I still have puffiness under my eyes. I wish this would go away. I'm also not really seeing any improvement in the darkness under my eyes. I felt like I slept more solidly the past 2 nights at least not where I am super awake...it's hard to tell. I was very hot and flushed over the past few nights probably because of my cycle. I do tend to wake up ~6 but I don't get up at this time anymore, since if I do it makes me have less energy the rest of the day so I stay and (go back to sleep) until I wake again ~7:30. I then go outside and also do my face dunks (in ice water). Then eat breakfast. My tongue is always swollen to some degree. It might still have a bit of a coating on it, just not as bad as the Fall. (So I guess that's good).

I am not eating the offending foods anymore. I am eating either seafood or offal/bone cuts of meat. I use coconut oil, pork belly and/or olive oil etc. I eat seaweed with every meal now. Just to let you know I was eating all these foods before, just that I added eggs, made the mistake of the dates and yoghurt/kefir in December.

Yesterday I did feel like my stomach felt a little less bloated, maybe just a tad.

I hope I am just having some detox or something and that I get better faster/sooner. I don't like how I am having the problems of Fall but with additional new problems. I would honestly rather go back to September and October. Sure I was concerned about some kind of infection, but I otherwise looked and felt pretty good. Now I don't feel very well, look bad and clothes don't fit, plus I don't know how the status of my possible infection is...sigh.

On the one hand my head does feel clear and I might be a little less tired AT LEAST COMPARED to December. I think December was very bad because I was fuzzy in the head PLUS very fatigued and the PMS (which didn't lead to a cycle in that month, only instead this month). So it was like a month long PMS.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-11T16:13:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-70327>

I think it might be rough just for this little while until things get more sorted after some time taking Vitex and ox bile in particular. I think taking the vit D has helped because I think my teeth are less stained. I really noticed in November they started to get yellow discolouration. I can't blame the coffee, because I was drinking coffee for months on end before with no changes in teeth colour.

I probably just need to be patient. I am just so cautious this time because last year I thought I was making all of this progress and it seemed like suddenly it all unravelled. So I don't want to be naive in thinking I 'fixed' everything, not just yet.

I do also have the complication of not being able to get a decent doctor and not affording proper tests. I am lucky enough that I am having some help from ppl on the forum, so that is a god-send! As if angels DO exist.

I know for sure my potential problems are:

\-infection of some sort: is it viral (and at that upper respiratory), fungal, parasitic etc? Exactly what?
\-sex hormone related issues such as tendency to either be estrogen 'collector' (probably related to the liver, because I managed to 'get rid' of a lot of the estrogen before, but if it comes back that means more of a 'collector'. Also I know that my P is low (if estrogen has such bad effects). So with Vitex I hope to help boost my P as naturally as possible without needing a Rx
\-Thyroid: I have a lot of hypo symptoms
\-Liver: I hope that if I address liver with ox bile and in general (I would like to see how NAC would help: I tried this a little bit in June....but didn't stick with it b/c of cost etc.) I was thinking of trying Silymarin but I don't think I will any longer since I have read it has 'estrogen mimicking' effects. Maybe not the right thing for me/my age.
\-Adrenals: If adrenals are related it's only b/c of the above things which have caused strain on them is all. I know I am prone to experiencing and/or relying too much on adrenaline rather than something more stable and calming. \*I will admit that I am not feeling overly elated but instead when a good mood strikes me it does feel much calmer. I feel like I can trust that feeling much more than when it's feeling super excited (but a bit on the unfounded side) ie. adrenaline.

It would be sweet if I can help the hormones and the thyroid simply by hard core addressing the liver. How sweet would that be? I am surely a fan of NDT for thyroid, but it's a. hard to get from a dr. and b. It's expensive and/or hard to get online (Paypal always has a problem and they don't seem to accept pre-paid credit cards either). ( I don't own a credit card...lol let's not even go there hehe).

As stated above I am eating epi-paleo. I sometimes forget to mention that I use the recommended spices as well as I am eating onions raw and cooked like crazy now. I eat either saurkraut or bubbies pickles (with the live cultures of course). I like green tea and I like chamomille sometimes too. I don't feel to drink loads and loads of green tea, just sometimes. I eat avacadoes too.

I would like to know if there are other veggies I should be adding or anything?

I think I will try a sleep mask just in case if the lights from outside are considered still too bright. I am always never sure though since in nature you'll still get the light from the stars and the moon. \*At my parent's place it is more like that, so I would probably not bother with a sleep mask there. But here I think maybe the street lights would still be considered too much (even though there are very few). I will try it.

I think I will also incorporate some squats. I think this might help over all as described in the blog post from JK. I do feel good if I do them. In karate we used to do horse stance (squat basically). Your legs get tired but it really does feel good---I miss karate so much.

Not being able to afford it for one but afterwards learning that it's probably not a good idea if one is having some adrenal issues makes it OK to not afford it AND the dojo I was at in Brantford, I only like that place. I don't know if I'd like it as much any place else b/c the sensai, and all the people-the community is what I really enjoyed. The first day I stepped into that dojo I was at home and I was welcomed. I still have friends on FB from karate. Such wonderful people!

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-13T14:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-70527>

This is what I wrote on September 18 regarding a thread on progesterone:

I wonder how Vitex and betaine helps with progesterone production. I know that Chocolate has discussed using this as a method to increase her pg. levels. Hmmmâ€¦.sounds very interesting.
See, in beginning of July I was coming out of a prolonged constipation and absence of period (and probably ovulation) of 3 months. I felt 'mad'. Anyways out of such a desperation I went to a naturalpath and got prescribed a tincture of 3 ingredients which included Vitex. I took it for about 3 weeks. (This is still in the presence of eating pretty well and having done CT since March and following this blog etc.) Then on July 21 I finally menstrated. I also started the seafood-rich diet in mid-July. So I don't know what was giving me the 'boost'. I felt immediately better after that date. At the end of July I felt so positive that I ditched ALL supplements (b/c I felt like I was being consumed negatively by them etc.) So for August I did not menstrate, but did get spotting the week before I 'should' have menstrated. I did not feel 'worried' though. I still felt pretty damn good. I'm pretty sure I ovulated at the end of August, but did not menstrate this month. Hmmmâ€¦ The thing is, I DO feel good though so that is interesting. Now with epi-paleo and super good circadian cycles, my mind is much clearer, and I have acquired and assimilated even more information in the last two months in this better mind state. So I think it would be a good idea to re-visit betaine and Vitex. I have been taking Maca for the last three weeks. I decided I should try that and be more disciplined with itâ€¦since in the past I never took it for long enough. lol I feel much more familiar with my body chemistry and what my unique needs are. This is helpful as I can have a better base from which to address things. Especially if in the presence of epi-paleo and circadian cycles (and CT in the form of natureâ€¦hee hee). So I know that despite super healthy, that mechanisms from past epi-genetics is still needing to be addressed. Overall though, I feel blessed and privileged to have even come across this website at all. Honestly I have to say life-changing and even 'saving'. I had the mentality that "I will NOT let myself get worseâ€¦I HAVE THE POWER TO HELP MYSELFâ€¦I'LL STOP AT NOTHING TO GET THERE!" While this can be viewed as extreme and it takes you on some dark paths occasionally, when you come out the other end, being stronger and triumphantâ€¦then you cannot argue against the necessity of having such a strong opinion.

Wow, it is remarkable to see the difference in what I write since September. I felt really great as you can see...but obviously I was headed in a bad direction without even knowing it. I was also stupid to not take my own freaking advice regarding the betaine and Vitex. If I had tried it in October instead of waiting until now. So I am taking Vitex since last days of December. I had my period and on day 6 (more like spotting than anything). I will probably still have a bit of release tomorrow too...

As mentioned before the PMS was super bad and even during the TOTM it's not really that great. I think my bbt's are at least a LITTLE higher even after the shedding eg. 36.48 around there instead of dropping to 36.3 or even worse like a month agao 35.9!

I am taking ox bile and I feel like it helps in that my stools are nice dark brown and sinking, but it's not helping with 'borderline' constipation (and that they are kind of small etc). I also still have massive bloat. It's kind of similar to that Sean guy where even after water, I bloat.

I will have to wait for February before spending any more money on supplement etc, but I will try to re-add the betaine hcl as well as ox bile and Vitex. I will keep using D3 b/c I feel like that helps although there's not quantifiable method to prove this on...I just feel like it helps as opposed to when I tried a lot of vit C in December, I did not really feel a benefit (instead I felt kind of bad).

I must have a thyroid problem of some sort, but I am really wanting to be able to see if fixing my Pg (and liver) can then help the thyroid down the stream.

I registered with Dzugan online so I will see if through that I can somehow find a doc in my area to help me out etc. Also if I can find a way to pay or what would be covered etc. Cross fingers that this can help.

I also know that Shijin /Gretchen stated on that thread that Vitex had helped her in the past (or that she had had success with it).

I am drinking a beet carrot and seaweed tea that I made myself. I am just trying anything and everything to help me as much as possible.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-28T22:56:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igf-1-and-progesterone.5343/post-73047>

Today is January 28 so I will write in this journal. I don't think anyone reads it anymore so maybe I can move it to the journal section....makes more sense to do that. We'll see if I do. hehe

I got magnesium oil shortly after that last post. So almost 2 weeks (that's it) lol. Already I think it DOES give a benefit for sure. I know with magnesium it can take a few months to notice a true benefit and/or be truly restored at cellular level. Also I continue with the Vitex and I also consume massive amounts of green tea. Here are some benefits so far I gain:

\-I don't think I am constipated
\-I 'think' darkness under eye is actually decreasing/going away
\-I honestly think my skin tone has 'darkened'. Does that even make sense? I'm less pale and I feel like I look like I have a bit of a 'tan'. Hmmm, cool and very welcome. I was lamenting starting end of December that I 'lost' all my colour for winter. Well now I got it back. yay!

I am in the period leading up to my cycle (will it come normally in February, we shall see). I still have to fight water retention a little bit but not so much. On the one hand I feel like my stomach has some how gotten a little flatter but of course not ass and thighs. (sigh). I don't even care about 'belly' I only care about bad composition. Not cool. Meas my IGF 1 still sucks. \*I don't know what happened to the package from Charlotte either....FEDEX is /was giving a problem...

If I compare this date in December to this month at least I feel MUCH better. I don't feel like on a decline.

I've also been doing more grounding techniques at least to my best ability. Every morning now I will go out in bare feet and place my hands on a tree. I also try to climb a tree if I can. I just 'go outside' a bit more (which I thought I did well enough before).

So cross fingers that the continued use of magnesium oil and Vitex (in particular) help to restore quicker/sooner etc.

I am trying to find nagiri flakes which apparently is magnesium chloride to make my own mag oil which is obviously cheaper so that instead I can afford the ox bile still. I like the ox bile and I think it is helpful even if I am not regular in using it.

ok... I've finally had the time to dig into this thread...

so, I have a question. what does it mean when your IL-6 is low, but your TNF is high, yet you're converting T4 --> T3, but your T4 is low and your TSH is high? obviously continued inflammation - but the question is where? is it at the brain, liver - or is it specifically at the mitochondrial level as the result of leakiness at level 1 b/c you're not yet a fat burner? I'm still showing the "Dawn Phenomena" when I wake before the sun on work days. but when I sleep in or CT its in the low 80s, which I see as yet another indicator of inefficient mitochondria....

next set of labs should be interesting...

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=69432)
>
> fructose has zero role .............but the rest of it is not bad at all........if leptin Resistance Part deux is perfect.......(yes another blog I wrote) that means gluconeogenesis is perfect and you make your own 6 carbon sugars to make enough D ribose from the Hexose Monophosphate shunt to replenish ATP.
>
> Fructose ingestion actually depletes ATP faster than any sugar.
>
> Andrew and Ray Peat dont get that part.....they believe it increases it. RQ in patients who eat it clinically say otherwise
>
> We are all about ATP production.
>
> Without it, they call us cadavers.

[http://www.andrewkimblog.com/2013/02/response-to-dr-paul-jaminets-rebuttal.html](http://www.andrewkimblog.com/2013/02/response-to-dr-paul-jaminets-rebuttal.html)

Kim seems to acknowledge this point in point 5:

"Fructose does, in fact, deplete ATP more than glucose does simply because of the nature of its metabolism, but the effect is subtle and not necessarily undesirable. By rapidly depleting the high-energy phosphate bonds of ATP, fructose (1) forces glucose to be used at a higher rate and (2) depletes the reducing cofactor called NADH, which is a good thing (and a topic for a whole other post).
So, through these dynamic processes the excessive accumulation of ADP, AMP, and adenosine (which irreversibly diffuses out of the cell and is ultimately degraded to uric acid) is effectively kept in check, and ATP levels inside cells don’t decrease too much."

i am not sold yet.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-30T14:44:18+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-30T14:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diabetes-and-fuel-switching-low-carb-flu-poor-mitochondrial-ineffieciency.5346/post-197583>

Low quantum yield exacerbates the Dawning phenomena. Why? You need AM sunlight to yoke the melatonin, dopamine and cortisol cycles. So the 4 AM spike of cortisol ends the melatonin surge from midnight. This raises AM blood glucose because fat burning is not optimized when AM sunlight is out. During darkness it is optimized. So when the sun hits the retina you initially need that small burst of glucose to help jump start AM metabolism with a photopic eye. An environment with low quantum yield exacerbates the Dawning phenomena and the spike grows larger than one would expect. This is why most diabetics show a large respond because their AM light environment sucks.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-02-10T20:29:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diabetes-and-fuel-switching-low-carb-flu-poor-mitochondrial-ineffieciency.5346/post-309890>

The same key can open the doors to heaven or hell. The environment it occurs in or the redox time stamp of the gut when it occurs decides which outcome you experience is the take home. Stop blaming food for what you environment is causing. Lesson is here. [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-04408-7](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-04408-7)

Here you can see protein fuels can do two things based upon redox power from the environment.

In my thesis – an argument for high cholesterol [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-5#post-310676](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-5#post-310676)
I investigate the cholesterol molecule. It turns out it has hypoxic superpowers _\-> hypoglycemia._

Most researchers don't even try to formulate the mechanism of action. At best author(s) will append "more research is needed."

The question is always - _What is the therapeutic dose?_
In this "inquiry", how much hypoxia with how much cholesterol?

We know breath work comes in many flavors. The question maybe which provides the most sustained levels of lowered oxygen blood levels?

high fructose (mostly gotten from corn)

He gets up at 5:45 am Monday through Friday and is at work 7-3. He has a job in maintenance so some days he is inside all day and some days outside for hours.

Food is mostly carnivore in that it's eggs,bacon, sausage,beef 70/30 (his bloodsugar temporarily seems to go down when he eats the beef), chicken, some cheese, peanuts (not carnivore and not great for him but it's his only snack and he still likes a snack at night), salami,venison...it's a very limited list because he tends to be a picky eater.

Meals are generally all the same during the workweek with the first meal at 9:30 and its not large generally a large helping of slices salami and chunks of Colby cheese. I have the details in his myfitness pal app that I don't have access to right now...I tracked his food for 2 weeks to make sure his carb count was good. Lunch is a large portion if whatever meat we had the night before and if it's lean I add some fat either as a dip or melted cheese (once again, picky eater). Dinner is generally the same, a meat with some fat. Tonight is chicken breast grilled and he will have some ranch to dip and shredded cheese melted on top.

We hunt pretty often this time of year so he gets the setting sun but not often the rising sun. I just ordered blue blocking lenses for the evening because we have LED lights that I need to get rid of in our house. He is an electrician so most of what I have communicated to him from what I've learned here makes sense to him it's just putting it in practice.

With his glimpride and metformin he takes B1, berberine, and biotin. I guess I should get some liver support.

His blood sugar has been running 150-170 consistently unless he eats a bolus of protein or carbs in any amount at which point it might touch 200. Then it will plummet down lower then where it started...be there for a few hours in what I would call a good range of 100-130 then slowly climb back up over the course of a few hours. He does have dawn syndrome...like a clock it spikes at his waking time no matter what time he wakes. Generally it goes to 170, sometimes it stops 150ish but I can't find a reason why it differs.

### Sue-UK — 2022-11-14T17:06:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diabetes-and-fuel-switching-low-carb-flu-poor-mitochondrial-ineffieciency.5346/post-317580>

> [Christina marks said:](/goto/post?id=317566)
>
> Sorry I should have added he is on metformin and glimpride. He does eat within 4 hours of waking but probably not the recommended 50 grams of protein...he wears a continuous glucose monitor and we have seen that if he has a large bolus of protein without significant fat his blood sugar spikes. I maybe have to get his first meal higher in the protein range. He really has been mostly carnivore for the last month but was primarily eating keto for the last 2-3 years.
>
> Thanks for replying to me. He's the love of my life so I'm doing my best to gather information.

Hi Christina

If he is wearing a continuous glucose monitor it may be worth testing a few things and seeing what works ..... One is the idea of eating a source of fibre first, so it could be a seasonable salad/veg starter followed by the protein, and then if any carbs are eaten, do that last with extra fat. (Check out Jessie Inchauspe on youtube)

You could adapt the epi paleo using seasonal foods, but she tested using a continuous glucose monitor and the results are interesting.

In her book she gives lots of tips but one of them that I've used is vinegar. A tablespoon of ACV in a large glass of water before meals reduces blood sugar spikes in the following meals. How it works is explained in another book but put simply, its an uncoupler ... it signals the mitochondria and repair and make more of themselves, i.e. "uncouple" and "waste" fuel as heat, which positively affects blood sugar control.

You don't necessarily need to cut out fructose from seasonal fruit, the effects on blood sugar are different if the fruit (say seasonal local berries) are eaten at the end of the meal, particularly if they are eaten with a source of fat such as cream. Its the juicing, smoothies and eating it between meals or first that is the biggest problem. Seasonal berries for example also contain polyphenols, which apart from its nutritional compounds also trigger uncoupling, and the slow absorption through the liver does not have the same signalling effects as a hit of a juice or smoothie. Something else (from a different book!) is that dehydration can promote fructose production endogenously so not enough water and/or high salt can lead to fat accumulation, which can affect organs. (Its apparently how camels and desert animals store fat in their hump/tails etc, it provides water when used as fuel).

Another thing worth considering is googling spirulina and diabetes. (and chlorella). Just one quick link is
[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/B9780128157763000334](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/B9780128157763000334)
