> He gets up at 5:45 am Monday through Friday and is at work 7-3. He has a job in maintenance so some days he is inside all day and some days outside for hours.
>
> Food is mostly carnivore in that it's eggs,bacon, sausage,beef 70/30 (his bloodsugar temporarily seems to go down when he eats the beef), chicken, some cheese, peanuts (not carnivore and not great for him but it's his only snack and he still likes a snack at night), salami,venison...it's a very limited list because he tends to be a picky eater.
>
> Meals are generally all the same during the workweek with the first meal at 9:30 and its not large generally a large helping of slices salami and chunks of Colby cheese. I have the details in his myfitness pal app that I don't have access to right now...I tracked his food for 2 weeks to make sure his carb count was good. Lunch is a large portion if whatever meat we had the night before and if it's lean I add some fat either as a dip or melted cheese (once again, picky eater). Dinner is generally the same, a meat with some fat. Tonight is chicken breast grilled and he will have some ranch to dip and shredded cheese melted on top.
>
> We hunt pretty often this time of year so he gets the setting sun but not often the rising sun. I just ordered blue blocking lenses for the evening because we have LED lights that I need to get rid of in our house. He is an electrician so most of what I have communicated to him from what I've learned here makes sense to him it's just putting it in practice.
>
> With his glimpride and metformin he takes B1, berberine, and biotin. I guess I should get some liver support.

Getting the blue blocking lenses is a good start, but its also what his sleep is like and whether he is willing to change his eating pattern. The diet seems restricted and not seasonal, and eating in the evening will affect sleep and blood sugar control and the circadian rhythm of cellular repair at night. If the peanuts are salted he could be flipping the switch to store fat, which may accumulate in the liver, and getting some liver support isn't likely to overcome that. As he is already in a diabetic hole, working towards not eating for 4 hours before bedtime will give him a better chance of being able to enter the right stages of sleep at the right time, and give the liver time for housecleaning in line with the liver's circadian rhythm. So if he currently has a snack say 2 hours before bedtime, perhaps stretch it to 2 and a half hours and then 3 etc, aiming for no snacks after the last meal eaten 4 or more hours before bedtime. There's benefits from time restricted eating too, but its working towards that, rather than going all in and giving up because energy crashes etc.

Eating some fibre (like seasonal plants and fruits, or microalgae like spirulina and chlorella) will also feed the microbiome, which in turn will provide nutrients, some free fatty acids, and signalling molecules, which in turn may reduce appetite for large amounts of protein. There is also a link of high red meat intake to uric acid - not necessarily to gout producing levels, but this paper refers to it being a risk factor to diabetes. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3131180/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3131180/).

I've also read of a possible link between sleep apnoea and the low blood oxygen levels causing increased uric acid levels, so even if its not full blown sleep apnoea, that's another possible link. (My husband stopping snacks and eating earlier led to a dramatic improvement in his snoring!) Peanuts are higher in purines than other nuts so more uric acid ... Seafood contains purines too, but comes with anti inflammatory compounds such as DHA, and is a major component of the epi paleo diet. As a legume, peanuts are not on the plan and eating them at night is deffo not ideal.

[https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/13/2/563/htm](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/13/2/563/htm) is about microalgae such as spirulina and chlorella and metabolic syndrome and related conditions. I've also read papers about it being protective against diabetic complications such as eye problems, so it could be worth thinking about while he is working on the light water magnetism angles. They are grown as food crops, which as well as a powder can be bought as tiny tablets which can be swallowed whole with water, which might be helpful for a picky eater.

With the ACV question, I just put it in a large glass of water before a meal and just chug it down, but you can use it in a dressing with olive oil. Other vinegars are possible to use. Balsamic or other fruit vinegars will contain sugars, (although also polyphenols) which for diabetes might not be a first choice to start with in a drink on an empty stomach, but Balsamic mixed with olive oil and drizzled over a salad could be one of the benefits of the Mediterranean diet, (other cultures using different condiments and side dishes that provide vinegar, all sources of acetic acid).
,

# Thread: Did Dr. Kruse just trump Weston Price?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-dr-kruse-just-trump-weston-price.5347/>

### duceswild557@hotmail.com — 2013-01-04T22:13:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-dr-kruse-just-trump-weston-price.5347/post-69385>

Well a lot of people I've come into contact with have turned to the price foundation or paleo based on the work of Weston A. Price. A dentist who travelled the world examining primitives and writing a big ole book on what he found. He said that the mouth was a window to overall health. He found that when people had perfect teeth that they also had good bone structure low rates of tuberculosis, cancer and CHD among other things.

In Brain Gut 6 and other places Jack Kruse states that cold therapy increases alpha-msh massively in humans. I've found at least one study in rats that supports his claim.

You guys might not be familiar with a study from 2010 in the Journal ACS nano but they basically found that a gel with the active ingredient being alpha msh actually regrew damaged teeth with a resoundingly high success rate. Something like all the cavities regrew after one month. http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/37978810/ns/health-oral\_health/#.UOeIvWt5mSM .

Weston price based his work on the diet that promotes best dental health, and I still agree that vitamins d and k2 work to build stronger bones. But what if price overlooked lifestyle? Is it possible that how Dr. Kruse uses CT to help spinal surgery patients recover could also be incorporated for superior dental health?

Whatever you think about â€“ and thank about â€“ you bring about. I think about a lot of stuff and how it all fits. Alpha MSH is the one NT in the brain where the confluence of health occurs. If you go back and read the Ancient Pathway post of CT -6........It is called the leptin-melancortin pathway.

Yep, it is that important for life.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-04T23:12:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-foundation-for-factor-x.5348/post-69390>

Mammals are very different: In a series of human experimentsperformed by Wengerand colleagues (1976 ), the sweating threshold during physical exercise was lower at night than during the day, suggesting that humans thermo regulate during the night at a different level than during the day. That the circadian rhythm for body temperature is deeply rooted in the brain and independent of the sleep-wake , drinking , feeding , and motor activity cycles was shown by Satinoff (Satinoff and Prosser 1988 ). She lesioned the suprachiasmatic nucleus and found that the rhythms for drinking and motor activity were abolished , but the rhythm for body temperature remained .

Now ask yourself why?

What animals did we come from?

What was the environment like back then?

Why does she avoid it?

Estrogen excites warm sensitive neurons and inhibits cold sensitive neurons (Boulant and Silva 1989 ) Low progesterone levels makes it even worse.......
This may help to explain why postmenopausal women on estrogen therapy have high sweat rates by report and improved thermal tolerance in the heat.

# Thread: Are supplemental fish oils always bad?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-05T08:40:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69403>

One of my goals on this journey is to learn to like eating fish, but at this point, I hate it. I keep trying to eat it every couple of days, and have been supplementing wild krill oil, and more recently wild salmon oil (a cost decision, will go back to krill when this lot is finished) to get my o3/o6 ratio in line. I have been reading a lot of hate for supplemental fish oils lately, but I can't get the reasons straight in my head <-- eat MOAR fish, right?

Can someone please tell me if I am better off getting some fish oil in supplement form than none at all?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-05T12:11:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69434>

depends upon your current context........I personally do not like Fish oils much.....some is OK but I want you to eat BG 6 style.

Read this: http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/

### ashryn — 2013-01-05T23:49:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69546>

Ta Dr K,
I have read it a couple of times, but taken with other things I've read on the site, it just got me more confused.. Will read it again now and see it it makes more sense today { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Ok, here's what I get from the blog post..maybe writing it down will make it crystalise.
1\. We need more o6 than o3
2\. O6 travel an inflammatory pathway to produce arachidonic acid, which leads to prostaglandin production
3\. just because AA is needed for prostaglandin production doesn't mean it always occurs when there is o6. If the body has no need of them, it doesn't produce them from o6
4\. EFAs are produced when we need them, and we do need them sometimes so preventing the production of AA is pretty short sighted.
5\. AA is needed to create a platelet adhesion inhibitor that stops blood clotting when it is required, and it creates another substance that oxidises to seal wounds and creates the inflammatory pain response that assists healing by increasing the blood flow to the area and making it hurt so we are careful with the wound.
6\. O3 and o6 work together in wound healing, but o6 is needed for the fast response to an injury site
7\. A good ratio TO EAT is 6-1 but closer to 2:1 is better. <--so blood level ratios are likely to be different depending on how you are using them up
8\. The brain has a o6-3 ratio of 100:1, so it clearly wants it for something (some evidence of what it is important for, but not relevant to current question)

Conclusion: make sure the diet has a o6-3 ratio of 6:1 aiming for 2:1 as the best possible ratio. Eating more o3 than o6 might cause changes in VAP and HS CRP <--bad, but what specifically?
PS supplemental fish oil is a PUFA and subject to peroxidation and ALE formation <--free radicals? "When taken improperly" <--what is the proper way to take them?

Answer to my question: no fish oil sups aren't always bad so long as they are good quality and you are taking them to ensure your dietary intake of o63 is aiming at being 2:1

Thanks for the workout Dr K (I much prefer a brain workout to a cardio one ) Do I pass or fail?
I need to go read up on VAP and hs CRP again to work out why that's bad, and PUFAs and ALEs and work out why we have to avoid this too..

### Linz — 2013-01-07T05:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69718>

Ashryn,
I think that krill oil is useful for pain relief once O6/3 ratio is good and is probably less harmful than the drugs which modify AA pathway to reduce pain.
I have gut problems and can't use many common painkillers so I use krill oil ( and cold!).

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T06:35:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69720>

Oh! That's good to know.. My brother is allergic to painkillers, so i shall have to pass that on. Thanks!

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T20:19:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69799>

Dr K,
I have been tracking my o3-o6 intake, and eating keto paleo style limiting nuts and chicken, i am struggling to get enough o6 compared to o3... Even if i dont supplement. Where does your o6 come from in your diet?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T22:27:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-supplemental-fish-oils-always-bad.5349/post-69809>

oils......EVOO, Bacon, Meat.......

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T22:37:15+0200

# Thread: Factor X squared:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factor-x-squared.5350/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-05T11:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factor-x-squared.5350/post-69427>

my top 3 labs based upon what I know right now for a quick hack: HDL, DHEA, Vit D level Why? HDL tells me three main things.....overall inflammation in the body and at the mitochondrial level, Zeta potential (how viscus blood is die to inflammation, EMF, artificial light exposure and how badly their body is lacking in electrons to make ATP at their mitochondria) , HDL levels how permeable the intestinal border is now. DHEA is the middle of the hormone chain so it gives me a decent idea of how metabolically efficient one is.......it tells me about the proximal chain (free T3 and pregnenolone) an it tells me what I should expect from the distal sex steroid hormones if this one is low........the most important thing DHEA tells me is sleep efficiency because of its 98% correlation to IL 6 production in the brain.......from its level and the persons age I can extrapolate what their IGF1 and salivary melatonin are doing to circadian cycles. The vitamin D levels tell me two main things.......how bad immune system is off, and is there a potential slow methylation problem if both the HDL and Vit D level is low.........Then I can test the methylation pathways if I think the clinical story matches the history. Low Vitamin D levels also tell me about mitochondrial efficiency and how tight new synapses are or will be now based upon age and level of the lab.......if the age is above 40 and the level below 20 I know that this patient likely has a severe low brain dopamine level and low sex steroid hormones too. Those are my top three and why. Testing allows us to challenge our beliefs. Understanding the hormone panels and the labs is the Rosetta stone for understanding mitochondrial efficiency. We only are as good as our mitochondrial function. They are the batteries of our cells and much like a new Xmas toy that has bad batteries placed in it it wont work optimally. The same analogy is true for human mitochondria. We need to assess the exhaust from the mitochondria......CO2, H2O, and Tissue O2 levels. This tells us all about how good or bad our Ferrari engines really are running. We should define bioenergetics as the studying the flow of chemical bond energy within organisms. In a living cell, the principal reactions of fuel metabolism take place in the mitochondria, where food energy is released,oxygen is consumed, and water and carbon dioxide are produced. All life on earth utilizes the energy formed from water formation to power pathways and metabolism. Remember the Calvin cycle/photosynthesis, where carbs (glucose) are formed from air (CO2), and energy of the sun? In the mitochondria, the opposite reaction occurs. Energy from the exothermic reaction of water forming from air (O2) and the enzymatic burning of fuel (oxidizable food, glucogen, glucose, fat) result in HEAT and ATP. When one mole of H2O is created from one H2 (hydrogen) and half O2 (oxygen), 286 kJ of power are released (in other words, 68 kcal, which is about one small potato).... FROM FORMATION OF ONLY ONE MOLE OF WATER. So clearly, a high BUN/creatinine ratio on a chem 7 also tells us we are energy starved. This is why dehydration is a major clinical sign of mitochondrial damage and or stress. This is another reason why I ascribe to the mitochondrial theory of aging. We have no direct ability as humans to repair mDNA. It is our weakest link and as I have said in the Brain gut 5, we are only as good as our weakest link. Well, this is it for all mammals.

CO2 1/2 O2 = one mole H2O (~18 grams water = 3.5 teaspoons) = 286 kJ

With cellular respiration, instead of an enormous, exothermic explosion (like a hydrogen bomb), the electrons and protons are added step-wise on a gradient known as the electron transport chain (ECT) in plants and animals. A biological mitochondrial 'battery' is created with the 'anode' on the inner mitochondrial membrane side and the 'cathode' on the other. Heat is energy released when oxygen is the final proton acceptor and coupled to the enzyme (F1F0-ATPase) that forms ATP, the universal currency of cellular energy in the body. When the protons fall across the proton channel, ATP is formed. We use ATP as fuel every minute every day for all cellular work, then recycled back to ADP. In one day, it is estimated that our mitochondria may produce our own weight in ATP (1). Efficiency of the theoretical transfer of energy from oxidizable fuel to ATP and heat is shockingly good: 39% ATP and 61% heat \[1\]. Mitochondria are energy giants. To give you an idea of how good they are read this: This is is from my blog on June 14, 2011 called What powers life and death? "Bacteria generate energy across their outer membrane. This limits their energy production due to geometry. Their energy production falls off due to a falling surface area to volume area ratio. Animal cells, by absorbing the power plant, now figured out how to internalize energy production and expand the surface area of the inner membranes with massive folding like we see in the surface of the brain. These evolutionary changes are believed to have occurred only once in the history of our planet. Endosymbiosis is rare in bacteria. Peter Mitchell was initially ridiculed for his theories on bio-energentics of mitochondria. He showed how effective this evolutionary maneuver was. He showed that this change caused a pH gradient as well as an electrical charge of about 150 millivolts across the inner membrane. This may sound like a small amount of charge, but consider this fact. The inner membrane is only 4-5 nanometers thick, so the voltage across this membrane is about 30 million volts per meter! For comparison, that is equivalent to the energy in a bolt of lightening. That one bolt has the power generate energy of 1500 three thousand square foot homes in one mitochondria. Each cell has hundreds to thousands of those power houses in them. That is the power surge that fueled evolution and complex life forms."

Obviously many biological factors determine true efficiency: iron status (cytochromes are composed of heme), ubiquinol (not ubiquinone) (huge and another test I often perform) , oxidative and inflammatory state (mentioned above in the three tests), thyroid, HPA axis function (DHEA as a proxy for other hormones) , hormones, etc.

Plants (chloroplasts) get 3-6% efficiency from transfer of solar energy to the energy bonds of plant starches and fatty acids. Particular C4 plants can get 7-8% (sugarcane) and one super cyanobacteria strain Chlorobaculum tepidum achieves 10%. Various modern fuel efficiencies are approximately -- for coal (~20-30s%) and solar (20%). This puts into perspective how important mitochondrial efficiency really is to optimal health.......with out the ability to make ATP what do we call a person? They are called a cadaver. Illnesses are the stops on the train line between optimal and being a cadaver.......if your batteries are off it is a measure of your real wellness or illness in my view.

CITES:

1\. http://highered.mcgraw-hill.com/sites/dl/free/0073525502/930160/mad25502\_ch08.pdf

Stay strong and on point........and when you feel weak.......re listen to the December 2012 Webinar.

dehydration measures the mitochondrial exhaust.......if you have no exhaust if tells you the engine is not efficient. The reason for that have to be worked up. Best way to reverse it in my view is steeped green tea.......1-2 L per day.

Low dopamine can result from low dopamine, fast clearance, poor receptor function, or imbalance with serotonin in the brain due to excessive carbohydrates or exercise routines. Mr. Blum has stated that he believes that glucose cravings and obesity are due to a lack of dopamine receptors in the brain......Dr. Kruse has stated that any low Dopamine state for any reason leads too poor choices in the Dopamine Rx.

Low Vitamin D levels and a high HCY should point us to a problem of dopamine clearance in the methylation path.

Exercise in any excess causes increase hunger, increase methylation demands, and increase usage of ATP. It causes cells to swell and loss of bio energenic substrates. Longer term it will destroy your Vitamin D, DHEA, and cortisol cycles.

Those who say exercise makes them feel good with bad labs need a reality check..........your mitochondria determine your mood and affect. They are what power the production of dopamine and serotonin.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T18:10:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factor-x-squared.5350/post-69500>

Everytime you talk about exercise I have to wonder just how much damage I did to myself 20 years ago. I started doing step aerobics in my living room and worked up to an hour 5 times a week. Then I found The Firm workouts which combined low impact aerobics with free weights. When I finally quit doing those workouts a couple of years later I was doing them 6 days a week, an hour at a time.

Want to guess how much weight I lost? If you said zero you'd be dead on target. Everyone kept saying "well you've probably put on muscle. Are you clothes fitting any differently?"

Well not just no but hell no. I weighed about 10 pounds less then than I do now. I will say the heavy duty workouts sure helped me deal with the stress at work but I can only shudder at what they did to my biochemistry. Lesson learned.

Re: Mag oil, Dali Dula posted a 'recipe' to make your own for pennies a while back. I have since done it and it's easy. Epsom salts are another super cheap way to get Mg.

Another thing are those mRNA fragments, jumping genes, ... (you name it) coming from the ocean and enter human cells and even alter our genetic code. The door was opened by our leaky guts and primarily zonulin. So far so good. But what the hell is the purpose of those new genetic materials? It could be helpfulâ€¦ or just destroy us. And where did those genetic programs came from? I think there must have been an initial reason.

Its a weird thingâ€¦

These fragments are then used as the spare parts to expand our genome as we assimilate them over time. # Thread: How would you CT in the snow?

### jib — 2013-01-05T18:34:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-would-you-ct-in-the-snow.5353/post-69505>

It's winter here in the Midwest and I woke up sometime in the darkness and had an idea about CTing out in the snow. I could not go back to sleep for thinking about this and I think it's because a day or two ago someone mentioned making like a "bathtub" out in the snow, and then Shijin was talking about being out in her swimsuit in the snow, so I think the idea must have come from these.
How would you CT in the snow?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-05T20:08:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-would-you-ct-in-the-snow.5353/post-69521>

> **jib said**
>
> It's winter here in the Midwest and I woke up sometime in the darkness and had an idea about CTing out in the snow. I could not go back to sleep for thinking about this and I think it's because a day or two ago someone mentioned making like a "bathtub" out in the snow, and then Shijin was talking about being out in her swimsuit in the snow, so I think the idea must have come from these.
> How would you CT in the snow?

naked or close to it........rolling in it until your skin is slightly wet. Make sure your feet have leather soles.......once a bit wet run sprints or walk around a bit. The best place to CT is where water hits any part of the earth.

### Dali Dula — 2013-01-05T22:15:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-would-you-ct-in-the-snow.5353/post-69531>

I lay in the snow, preferably naked, in the sun. I do about ten minute rotations- face up then face down. Repeat as long as you like.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-06T13:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-would-you-ct-in-the-snow.5353/post-69601>

when I was at my parents DH and I would lay in the snow at the end of each day of snowboarding... I'd cover my legs. didn't last long in ambient temps btwn 15-20 degs though... I'd cover my legs until they were numb and I'd start to shiver uncontrollably. Eventually I'd just "belly flop" into the snow....then roll over... each day it got longer. DH always complained b/c I'd pink up much faster than he would - and I'd stay that way for longer - even after I'd warm up... we did this in bathing suits & wool socks... that's it! couldn't go naked b/c neighbors had direct view of our snow bank!

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-06T15:31:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-would-you-ct-in-the-snow.5353/post-69615>

> **Dali Dula said
>
>
> > **KiwiLauren said**
> >
> > > Dali Dula said
>
> **
>
> > > I lay in the snow, preferably naked, in the sun. I do about ten minute rotations- face up then face down. Repeat as long as you like.
> >
> > Remember AKMan talking about this last year? A vulture was circling overhead and he had to shout out it, I'm not dead!
>
> That was me, Dali Dula, being perused by buzzards last year. I'm still not dead.

Thank goodness!

# Thread: Greetings from a newbie Cold Therapy Lady
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-newbie-cold-therapy-lady.5358/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JenniferSprung — 2013-01-06T10:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-newbie-cold-therapy-lady.5358/post-69576>

hello...
finally got on the site! hooray! i have been sitting in cold tubs (55-60 degrees) periodically for five months. submersing body up to sternum as there was just not enough room in the tub to go any deeper. sometimes once per day, sometimes twice. usually for 20 minutes, give or take.
this experience was fantastic! The abdominal muscles were visible. Any sick or achy feelings were completely eliminated. Sore or overworked muscles-gone...
sometimes i would take a warm/hot bath after (not often) to warm up as my extremities would not warm up through the night.
right now i am experimenting with submersing my entire body, head and all in a cold swimming pool. not ideal (chlorine) but
challenging...68 degrees is not very cold for piece work but when you put the whole body in, it does take awhile to catch your breath.
what i love so much is the feeling of body temp rising in response to the therapy. this reminds me so much of when i was young, swimming in a cold pool and how my body would warm up. so much so that it was better to stay in the pool than outside...for awhile anyway.
i look forward to sharing more of my experience and pictures too.
Sweet Potatoes

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T11:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-newbie-cold-therapy-lady.5358/post-69585>

Welcome and glad to have you!

### caroline — 2013-01-06T15:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-newbie-cold-therapy-lady.5358/post-69617>

Welcome from down under. This is an amazing journey.

### diane — 2013-01-06T17:05:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-newbie-cold-therapy-lady.5358/post-69633>

Welcome Sweet Potatoes! Good for you for doing the CT!

### Lava — 2013-01-06T15:14:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hawaii.5363/post-69613>

Hi all ~ Just to introduce myself: I am 54, usually do ok with my weight but after being put on t3/t4 thyroid meds I jumped up 20 pounds...omg!!! Off all meds now but still struggle with chronic active Epstein Barr Virus and unexplained bedridden fatigue (they tell me it's not the EBV)....I am radical about changing whatever it is I need to change....I have been off of grains and sugar for many months but I do have the occasional "cheat" episodes...I am so excited to be here and to meet all of you. Mostly, I thank Jack for his passion in doing this and making it available for us. The information here blows my mind as well as my doctor husbands mind. Yes, he is one, but tries so hard to expand beyond his training. I am telling you, I was at my wits end until I found this information....HOPE and ZEN!!!!

Much Aloha and Mahalo,

Lava.....

### freesia — 2013-01-06T17:15:47+0200

Hi Lava,
Welcome! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Chronic EBV sounds familiar. I don't know if any of this fits your picture but I had a slightly swollen spleen, chronically swollen glands, and frequent sinus headaches/infections, that I believed to be symptoms of ongoing viral reactivation and immune problems. These symptoms appeared after I got Mono in 1998 (I'd already had ME/CFS 14 years at that point). Symptoms were always much worse in winter.
I responded really well to cold thermogenesis last winter and (via a general strengthening of the immune system) these symptoms have improved 80%. Other ME related symptoms have improved a lot too.
^ Sorry that's All about me!! But I wanted to you to know what you read about here can be very effective if applied consistently.
Anne.

# Thread: astaxanthin....is it good to supplement?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astaxanthin-is-it-good-to-supplement.5366/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2013-01-06T18:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astaxanthin-is-it-good-to-supplement.5366/post-69655>

It seems like a great way to get antioxidants, etc. but I've read that it could deplete calcium a little..?...it's from....shellfish? so why would it do that? Is it a good thing to take? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Thor — 2013-01-06T20:52:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astaxanthin-is-it-good-to-supplement.5366/post-69667>

.
Why bother taking mother nature in a pill ? Eat mother nature for lunch - have some salmon and shrimp

An 8oz serving of Sockeye Salmon has 8 grams - that's double the amount used in clinical research studies. If you research it you'll find thousands of papers showing the benefits of Astaxanthin. Time to get my geek on lol.....

Astaxanthin classed as Carotenoid. Carotenoids are a family of natural fat-soluble nutrients important for antioxidant defense While over 600 carotenoids have been identified in nature, fewer than 50 are part of the human diet. Humans cannot synthesize carotenoids and must ingest them via algae, plants, fungi and seafood. There are two types of carotenoids (based on chemical structure): carotenes and xanthopylls. Lycopene and beta-carotene are examples of carotenes, while lutein, zeaxanthin, and astaxanthin are xanthopyll carotenoids.

In a 2011 study (Effects of astaxanthin on human blood rheology. J Clin Biochem Nutr 2008;43:69-74.), overweight individuals were randomized to receive astaxanthin and were compared to a control group with normal body weight (BMI <25.0 kg/m2) who received no intervention. The researchers measured markers of oxidative stress in the blood. At baseline, the obese individuals had significantly higher levels of two oxidative biomarkers: malondialdehyde (MDA) and isoprostanes (ISP). At the same time, plasma levels of two antioxidant measures â€“ superoxide dismutase (SOD) and total antioxidant capacity (TAC) â€“ were significantly lower in obese participants. In other words, the antioxidant defenses were down in the obese patients. After three weeks, the astaxanthin group showed significant lowering of oxidative markers MDA and ISP (both p<0.01), and they also had significant increases in SOD and TAC (p<0.001). Marked improvements on all four measures caused the overweight and obese subjects to become statistically indistinguishable from the control group, suggesting that astaxanthin supplementation lowered oxidative stress and improved aspects of the antioxidant defense system.

That same study researchers looked at whether Astaxanthin could improve blood flow. Better blood flow means better overall nutrition being delivered. Think of it this way - do you want a delivery truck with flat tires that takes 2 hours to ship a package or do you want a delivery truck that ships super fast? Same applies to your body and it's "delivery" truck - in this case it's your blood flow. In this study, Venous blood was forced using mild pressure through tiny â€œmicrochannels,â€ each just seven millionths of a meter wide, approximating the diameter of an red blood cell and the width of a capillary. The time required to traverse these capillary-type tubes under a set pressure was termed the transit time. Twenty men were randomly allocated to receive either astaxanthin or a placebo for 10 days. They tested their transit time both before and after the ten day period. Upon retest, the astaxanthin group had significantly faster transit time (p<0.05) compared to placebo. This study suggests astaxanthin could potentially improve microcirculation.

Here's another great research team: Astaxanthin might improve cognitive function. In a small, open-label trial, 10 healthy men ages 50-69, who had been complaining of forgetfulness, received astaxanthin (12 mg/day) for 12 weeks. (Preliminary clinical evaluation of toxicity and efficacy of a new astaxanthin-rich Haematococcus pluvialis extract. J Clin Biochem Nutr 2009;44:280-284.) On a computerized test designed to accurately detect early cognitive deterioration (â€œCogHealthâ€), they showed improvement in reaction time, attention, and working memory.

And, the best for last.... Astaxanthin helps boost our "powerhouses" into overdrive - Mitochondria improvement ! I'm surprised Astaxanthin wasn't mentioned in the December webinar as a booster to mitochondria output.

In a series of experiments with various cultured cell lines (Astaxanthin protects mitochondrial redox state and functional integrity against oxidative stress. J Nutr Biochem 2010;21:381-389.) , astaxanthin improved cell survival under oxidative stress. Astaxanthin reduced the mitochondriaâ€™s endogenous production of oxygen radicals and protected the mitochondria against a decline in membrane function that typically occurs over time in these cultures. But astaxanthinâ€™s benefits went even further: it increased mitochondrial activity by increasing oxygen consumption without increasing generation of ROS. It actually made the mitochondria more effective. The researchers then inserted into the mitochondria another molecular probe that measured their ability to conserve glutathione and re-reduce oxidized biomolecules.25 The mitochondria were then challenged with hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), a reactive oxygen species (ROS) that should normally oxidize and reverse this redox state. Astaxanthin was found to protect against the H2O2 oxidant effect. Its capacities both to protect mitochondria and to boost their energy efficiency makes this Carotenoid one mighty warrior fighting the war of Anti-aging.

I've started to notice my wrinkles have almost disappear - most guys may not notice wrinkles but I do. Having seafood that's loaded with astaxanthin helps get rid of wrinkles - it's mother natures way providing plastic surgery for free! Wooohooo...

One of the leaders in Skin Care is Dr. Nicholas Perricone MD, who highly recommends astaxanthin for reducing wrinkles and age spots, improving moisture levels, elasticity and smoothness, and for giving your skin a beautiful, healthy glow. In human trials, astaxanthin has been shown to reduce visible signs of UV-aging through dietary supplementation within 4 to 6 weeks of use. This data is supported by a number of in-vitro studies. Research suggests potential skin benefits from the use of astaxanthin to maintain a youthful appearance, reverse premature signs of aging - Oâ€™Connor & Oâ€™Brien (1998), et. all.

A double-blind placebo controlled study (Yamashita 2002), showed that astaxanthin in combination with tocotrienol, (a superior form of vitamin E), improved several aspects of overall skin condition. Eight female subjects with dry skin conditions (mean age 40 yrs) received daily doses containing 2 mg astaxanthin and 40 mg natural tocotrienols. Several types of data were collected at 2 and 4 weeks and compared to the initial baseline readings. Measurable differences were observed starting just 2 weeks after starting use of Astaxanthin. By the 4th week, the treated subjects with dry skin characteristics exhibited the following: increased moisture levels, consistent natural oils; reduction of fine wrinkles and a reduction in pimples.

Here is a list of the other ways astaxanthin supports the body:

â– Functions as a powerful antioxidant, decreasing free radical damage to tissues, cells and even DNA â€“ itâ€™s 6,000 times more effective at protecting DNA than vitamin C
â– Lowers elevated blood pressure
â– Supports healthy digestion by preventing gastric ulcers
â– Prevents eye fatigue and Age-Related Macular Degeneration
â– It protects against at least seven types of cancer, including liver, colon & breast cancers
â– Protects against skin damage from the sun
â– Enhances the immune system
â– Acts as an anti-inflammatory, reducing symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis - it also lowers the systemic inflammatory marker, C-Reactive Protein (CRP)
â– Inhibits mercury damage to the kidneys

Human trials suggest that it is among the most beneficial of the antioxidants, and almost certainly the most beneficial carotenoid.

Hope that helps

Clearly this Epi-Paleo diet has kick started my brain into working rather well lol

### Martha Ray — 2021-05-13T18:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astaxanthin-is-it-good-to-supplement.5366/post-298503>

**\[QUOTE="janagram, post: 69655, member: 1400"\]It seems like a great way to get antioxidants, etc. but I've read that it could deplete calcium a little..?...it's from....shellfish? so why would it do that? Is it a good thing to take?

\[/QUOTE\]**

I was a big fan of Astaxanthin 16+ years ago 2005 when it was newer to the market. I first began taking it for joint pain and to prevent sunburn took [https://www.nutrex-hawaii.com/](https://www.nutrex-hawaii.com/) BioAstin Hawaiian Astaxanthin. It worked miraculously for protection of sunburn when I was working out in an outdoor pool and moderately for joint pain (but I was 100 lbs overweight and very inactive back then). I took it for about 3 years was off of it but when I tried again in 2011 it did not agree with me. Wish I had noted why cause I can't remember but thought at the time I was more sensitive and perhaps quality had been lowered due to it's popularity.
**Happy to be getting from Seafood now.
I used to eat crispy fried shrimp tails but not the body shell... but not frying anything anymore.
Remember I did have an preventive medicine doctor back in the 70's who suggested we eat a small particle of egg shell for calcium! May go back to that and the shrimp tails or shell as well.
Some Nose to Tail thinking.
I am not a fan of synthetic supplements.**

It is good to supplement - even JK says it's good to supplement it... one of like 2 things he would supplement...

### Martha Ray — 2021-05-14T11:07:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astaxanthin-is-it-good-to-supplement.5366/post-298530>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=298510)
>
> I was a big fan of Astaxanthin 16+ years ago 2005 when it was newer to the market. I first began taking it for joint pain and to prevent sunburn took [https://www.nutrex-hawaii.com/](https://www.nutrex-hawaii.com/) BioAstin Hawaiian Astaxanthin. It worked miraculously for protection of sunburn when I was working out in an outdoor pool and moderately for joint pain (but I was 100 lbs overweight and very inactive back then). I took it for about 3 years was off of it but when I tried again in 2011 it did not agree with me. Wish I had noted why cause I can't remember but thought at the time I was more sensitive and perhaps quality had been lowered due to it's popularity.
> **Happy to be getting from Seafood now.**
> **I used to eat crispy fried shrimp tails but not the body shell... but not frying anything anymore.**
> **Remember I did have an preventive medicine doctor back in the 70's who suggested we eat a small particle of egg shell for calcium! May go back to that and the shrimp tails or shell as well.**
> **Some Nose to Tail thinking.**
> **I am not a fan of synthetic supplements.**

It is good to supplement - even JK says it's good to supplement it... one of like 2 things he would supplement... \[/QUOTE\]
Wonder if any one likes another brand...sometimes I think when something get hyped too much it gets adulterated like EVOO did. Or worse it quality gets cut for profit...I'm mistrustful at times...

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-06T20:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69660>

On reading the blog and various responses Jack mentions that those with autoimmune disease need to avoid eggs, milk, potatoes and other solanums. Is there a post that goes into detail with a full Rx for autoimmune disease?

Or is it just the Leptin Rx plus CT Rx plus epipaleo avoiding the above foods?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-06T21:37:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69676>

I think you can follow the autoimmune Rx of the paleo world (eg Robb Wolf)... basically it is further food elimination... so no nightshades, no eggs, no nuts, no dairy. Alongside epi-paleo leptin Rx with CT and circadian adherence would be the JK version as I know it.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-06T21:46:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69680>

I would also eliminate corn, soy and rice. Good luck to you!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T22:05:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69687>

There will be one soon..........

### MartiD — 2013-01-07T21:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69805>

Soy is important to eliminate if you have Hashis.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-08T19:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-someone-direct-me-to-autoimmune-rx.5367/post-69952>

Thanks.
I'm epi paleo so no grains, legumes, soy, no dairy, no eggs, no nightshades. Hadn't picked up on the no nuts though so will add that, though I wasn't eating many.

Also doing CT, waking at dawn and rolling out of bed shortly after, go to sleep an hour after dark - its summer here. Cravings are cut by 80%. Adrenal surges are starting to reduce. AI symptoms a bit slower to go but my flare is no longer active. Still there, but not making me feel like crap.

# Thread: Products/Brands in Australia

### lauren_r — 2013-01-07T03:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/products-brands-in-australia.5374/post-69715>

Just wondering what products you have found that you use in your epi-paleo journey to optimal?
Anyone found a "live" sauerkraut, or pickles?
Any other awesome finds? Or Aus websites?

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T06:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/products-brands-in-australia.5374/post-69719>

I use a coconut oil by nuigini organics that travels the least number of food miles... Comed from organic plantations in New Guinea

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-07T17:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/products-brands-in-australia.5374/post-69776>

Jimmy Moore, Paul Chek and Nora Gedgaudes have all been in Australia in the last 6 months. My guess is that if you find the groups who brought them, you will likely be connected with people who can tell you about specific Australian products. I've never found any non-pasteurised ferments here in New Zealand (except at one farmer's market where a German woman makes sauerkraut) so I make them myself. With coconut oil, there are many online suppliers who source the oil from the islands. I buy from Zenian Organics... don't know if they ship to Oz.

### Kiera2 — 2013-01-07T17:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/products-brands-in-australia.5374/post-69779>

You can get raw kraut products from Kitsa at http://www.kitsaskitchen.com.au/.
But it's a VERY expensive way to go, considering the price of cabbage. A sauerkraut crock is certainly worth the investment.

# Thread: Success you've seen in your journey - what have you noticed!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 63
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-01-07T11:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69734>

so MamaG - mentioned in another post... that she'd like to see peoples success all listed in one place.... Here's an opportunity for you to post the success you've seen since starting your journey.

here are some of the things I've seen in my journey....

better sleep, waking fully rested (Even on the nights when Chaos or Havoc is up sick!)
constant energy - I no longer have that after lunch or 3-4pm midday slump!
great skin, hair and nails; bad hair days are things of the past!
I recently went in and had my semi-annual bra fitting - and in 6mths I've lost a cup size! Whoot I'm finally conquering my E-dominance!
better sex drive
clarity of thoughts
more insight-fullness regarding work, my relationships.
my intuition is finally in tune with my surroundings!
faster recovery from injuries and illness with the help of CT.

These are just some of the things I've noticed...what are some of the things you've noticed?

### diane — 2013-01-07T13:15:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69750>

Awesome idea Shijin!

The biggest thing that I've noticed is that I don't feel like crashing and crawling under my desk at 2pm for a nap. That's huge! I don't feel so tired in meetings that I can't keep my eyes open. And it has been a very long time since I can't get out of bed and have to spend the day on the couch due to lack of energy. That is so amazing in itself, and changed primarily due to getting better with circadian rhythms.

My cravings are getting less and less. Actually, they are almost gone. Another amazing thing!

My finger nails are growing like crazy - and for some reason I can't stop staring at them. They're very strong as well. My skin feels amazing, and it hasn't been dry and cracking in the winter like it has in the past. Actually, dry spots on my face from my runny eyes is what started me on this journey! My eyes don't run anymore either, which means my vision is much better too.

I don't get cold like I used to - I haven't even worn a winter coat yet this year. My energy usage in my house is almost 50% vs. same time last year. Isn't that crazy? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

One interesting thing I just noticed this week. I hit my leg pretty hard on the edge of the bed, and usually I would have a really large bruise and swelling in the middle. The spot is still a bit sore, but I have minimal bruising, in fact I could barely see it. That is really new!

My attitude about a lot of things has changed. I'm not so hard on myself, nor am I trying to find perfection like I used to. I'm just accepting things and then working on how to make them better without beating myself up along the way. That's another huge change.

Overall I love how I feel, and just want to keep feeling better.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-07T14:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69756>

isn't it awesome when you realize you're small wins suddenly have compounded into BIG WINS!

### freesia — 2013-01-07T18:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69781>

The doctor said something a bit like...."Take one Winter and update me in the Springtime."

...Only he said it in 271,935 words.

I took care with circadian rhythms/signalling (including the big player - leptin), and I've CT'd solid since the beginning of April last year.

So, the IMPROVEMENTS:
Disappearance of the 3pm crash was the first big change I noticed, and that translated to a couple of extra hours when I could be active in the PM. Sleep improved too. I understand why now....it's in the Leptin blogs.

I read the CT stuff and it hit me on an intuitive level. I didn't understand much on first reading. I found it easier to understand as I went on and experienced it. I had to manage cold adaptation carefuly because I was pretty unwell. 28 years unwell.
I started CT 2 weeks into Leptin reset.
I felt like energy connections were being made. I understand now what's enabled that to happen.
....Cell swelling \*down\* enables respiratory pathway and ATP efficiency to go \*up\*. I'm functioning more like a well person....less like a cadaver.

This translated to....better stamina, and fitness out of nowhere. The fitness part actually stunned me, and my husband. Also I noticed very quickly - much improved autonomic function, and no more post exertional malaise.

And, immune function improved! I really want to understand MORE about that. Webinar request?

I still have a way to go. Putting even more miles between me and Cadaverville is this year's exciting mission!

Immensely grateful.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T18:28:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69784>

CT 6 has the immunity angle in it.........if you want to have your head hurt.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-07T18:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-69787>

Awesome Anne. Big wins for you!

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-09T13:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70055>

sometimes you've got to step outside both your comfort zone, and that of your docs doing an n=1 to see if you might be different from the "normal" rules of treatment. one size does not fit allâ€¦ each of us are different and we respond differently to things based upon our epigeneticsâ€¦ pay attention, your body knows what it needs.

All I can say is those 2 500mg metformin pills started "Calling" to me yesterday morningâ€¦ so I decided yesterday evening after dinner â€“ why not? it will make a difference or it won't. intuitively I knew the 1000mg would make a differenceâ€¦ but my PCP was hesitant b/c literature said at my FBG levels 500mg should have worked.

I. AM. NOT. LITERATURE. I. AM. ME. MY. N=1. DOES. NOT. FOLLOW. WHAT. LITERATURE. AND. RANDOMIZED. TESTING. SAYS. IT. SHOULD.

LESSON: I am my N=1. it is the only thing that drives my health.

### caroline — 2013-01-12T21:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70458>

I had to add something here today ..... I have talked lots about how great the raw meat and seafood have been for me - better digestion etc.
my body comp changed when I wasn't looking .... by 2 sizes - down! \[literally when I wasn't looking - my clothes were hanging off me and I didn't know why\]
but today something really weird happened ..... my waist came back!!! it has been missing for a very long time! how is it possible to just show up one day???
also - I have noticed lately that a lot of the spider veins in my legs are mostly gone - I don't know when that happened! I spent many. many years working 12 hour days on my feet
and my arms - don't look too bad at all........I actually went out and bought a tank top yesterday - for the first time ever!
and now I love going to the beach - kinda old to be a beach bunny - but there you have it! My grandsons love it that I play in the ocean with them - that alone makes it all worthwhile

as Gretchen said - small wins compounding into big wins ......go us

### diane — 2013-01-12T22:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70459>

Amazing Caroline! You've definitely been an inspiration for me to try new and different things! (Like the steak tartare I had for dinner lol).

### Kiera2 — 2013-01-12T22:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70460>

Ok. you've drawn me out of the woodwork Caroline. I can't believe I did this, but, drumroll please.....

I bought a pair of shorts! I haven't worn shorts for 35 years. And they're size 12! OMG that's a US size 8! And I wore them in public. I even wore them to the supermarket. And it felt goooood.

And all the clothes I bought last summer because I had nothing that fit? Well, they don't fit anymore. But I'm not investing in a new wardrobe yet, because I KNOW I'll be dropping another size soon. 15Kg down, another maybe 7 to go. Wow!

### diane — 2013-01-12T22:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70461>

Whoot whoot Coriander! Love the attitude that goes along with your shorts!

### caroline — 2013-01-12T23:14:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70462>

Corriander - I just did the same about a month ago - I bought shorts too - I haven't worn shorts in probably 20 years! and I feel okay going out in them too! I used to always say that I am too old for shorts. These are really superficial things I guess - but - it does feel good doesn't it? I think I made a mistake in buying a lot of new stuff and I expect my body will change once again - now that I sort of have a waist.

### Inger — 2013-01-13T03:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70482>

Wow Caroline!!! I can see you playing wth your grandkids at the beach all day long wearing only a bikini on your hot body..huh! Your vit D levels will be high soon enough! Did you knew that your back is the most effective to make vit D from he sun? I read that in a German book I have about the sunlight and its heath benefits. To let the sun shine on our women parts are the most effective for healing our hormones. So cool!

I never need to pee at night anymore! I often needed before, even if I ate very healthy ketogenic diet and tried to not drink before going to sleep! I guess that is something good! I can drink a glass of water right before going to bed without any issues too.

My waist stay thin but when I add weight it goes to my butt (and my upper arms too). I have this winter gained some because of stress and cheating with HWC (at work with coffee). You inspire me to drop the HWC 100% to have a hot butt when the summer arrives! Then I can wak around in shorts all the time because I love to have little clothes on and feel the wind and the sun on my skin.. it feels so sensual!

### caroline — 2013-01-13T04:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70483>

Oh my Dear Inger ........I haven't laughed that hard in a long time......a bikini??? A hot bod? Definitely not! What a visual that is! I have never had anything remotely close to an hour glass figure. I have always had narrow hips and a thick waist.....that is why I am so happy to see a little bit of a waist to show up.
I wonder why you don't pee as much at nite? I don't either ..... But I have a problem when I drink green tea. You don't have dairy at all now? What about butter?
With your hot bod ....there is going to be an amazing guy show up and want to make babies with you.........

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2013-01-13T06:04:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70488>

The biggest thing for me is waking up at 6am feeling like I have had a great sleep and not wanting to roll over and go back to sleep for 2 hours. My husband can testify to this - we have been together for 20 years and he has NEVER seen me get up before him! My nickname used to be Slumber because of my sleeping in in the mornings (I'm ashamed to say). I now get up often before he does and I actually want to go to bed earlier and go to sleep (around 9ish). I was never asleep before midnight before. So learning about circadian rythyms and following it has been amazing, I also no longer get up in the middle of the night to pee - I thought it was because I have had 4 children and I'm not so strong down there anymore, but now I sleep right through.
\- my skin feels like a babies bottom
\- my mood is so much more even and according to my kids I'm much more fun and adventurous - we have just been away camping for the weekend at a gorgeous river which has deep swimming holes to wallow in. There was a very long rope swing tied very high up a tree which swung across the river quite a long way, and I didn't see many adults having a go at it, in fact only a couple of Dad's but no Mum's, I got on it and it was such an adrenaline rush, but I was so proud that I did it! (It also probably had something to do with the 15kg I have lost so far)
\- I have lost 15 kg (33 pounds)
\- I feel so much more healthy especially with the all over tan that I have now. Interesting Inger that you mentioned your back is the best place for collecting vit D - I love to lie on my stomach.
\- I suffered from aches and pains especially in my knees, but in my back and hips on a regular basis which does not happen anymore.
\- sweet cravings gone. A while ago chocolate was being handed around which I said no thank you to. My husband looked at me as if I had two heads - he has never seen me refuse chocolate before, and it was amazing to me that I just DID NOT WANT IT!!!

I could probably think of more, but some of the changes are subtle. I have a long way to go but so far these things are life changing for me and I'm enjoying the journey!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-13T06:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70489>

So nice to read, Amber, these are really significant changes. Especially I like the sleeping part and not having to get up in the middle of the night or even several times. And, that your kids notice the changes is really big too. Having a fun Mom is the best for them.
My youngest son actually complained the other day when he saw me on Skype, because my fat cheeks are gone (he used to love pinching me)

### caroline — 2013-01-13T17:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70558>

Amber - That is the one thing I really notice at the ocean .... it is almost always the Dad's playing in the waves with their kids ... the Mom's are usually almost fully clothed and standing watching ...very sad... When you have more confidence about your body - the sky is the limit really - that swing sounds amazing!
I now go to bed around 9pm too - and wake up at 5:30 raring to go. The light cycles have been huge for me..

### vkiernan — 2013-01-13T17:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70575>

You guys really give us all inspiration. Please keep posting all the wonderful changes you are experience even the little ones! Sometimes, we have little wins and really don't notice them until someone else points theirs out.

### caroline — 2013-01-13T18:16:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70579>

### Linz — 2013-01-14T12:06:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70666>

Caroline, for the last few years I have often woken up with my wrists turned in and numb/weak fingers although I didn't have much pain and the strength returned with movement. I thought I was heading for carpal tunnel problems but over the last few months I am aware that I have been waking up with strong wrists.
I started out trying to solve health problems with the scd diet about 8 years ago and did quite well until Reynauds symptoms appeared about two years in and lasted for a whole winter.. At that time I used a lot of good fats but also vegetable oils. Just months after cutting out the oils the numb white/red/blue fingers were gone.

### caroline — 2013-01-14T16:02:38+0200

### Huck — 2013-01-14T16:24:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-70717>

Some successes and some work in progress.
On the success side:

Lost 75 lbs.
Glucose serum below 100 for 1st time in many years (95 at last lab)
CMP-14 all in normal range
triglycerides (2010) 194 (<150) now 70 (39) now 70 (>39)
T Chol/HDL Ratio (2010) 5.6 now 4.0 (which is from avg risk to low risk)
Vitamin D was 110 at the end of summer and now is 83.
Uric Acid is in range and lowest I have ever seen it.

Problems still to be fixed are LDL, prostate, steroid hormones, RT3 (have prescription for cytomel), and Estradiol (have prescription for Arimidex and non-prescription progesterone). I think RT3 and estradiol are the 2 biggest problems right now. I'm hoping to get them under control by end of Spring so that I can really use the sun to fix the other hormones.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-29T21:41:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-73204>

I had some really big great news last week.
I've been seeing a TMJ specialist for two years. I had gotten to the point where I couldn't really open my mouth to eat normally in the Fall of 2010. I found out that I had fused vertebrae in my neck that I had no idea about (apart from knowing I had a painful neck and scoliosis). I was given a splint and told to address generalized arthritis in my joints, take lots of calcium (HA!) and come back for more X-rays in a year.

The splint helped a lot with the pain, but a year later I was still losing bone and my doctor said I couldn't consider the orthodontics or surgery to fully correct my bite unless I had stablized the bone degeneration. That was last January...a little while later I found this forum and climbed aboard.

SO...after eating epi-paleo, starting CT, taking BHRT, thyroid support, working on adrenals, minding the light, embracing change, and all the other wonderful ideas, insights and laughs I get from this forum MY BONES HAVE STOPPED GOING SOUTH! In fact I'm sure they are going to become stronger and more flexible the longer I live like this.

Because I have these very clear images of my jaw and neck I can see the changes over the past year. I know I was feeling different, but to see change in a picture is exciting. Now I have to go make up for all the nasty EMF that come along with the imaging....and carry on to repair the rest of me.!!

### Inger — 2013-01-31T07:07:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-73431>

I do not think Jack meant diet does not matter.. you need it ALL. When he says EMF is worse that a bad diet.. it does not mean people get better when they eat a bad diet and ground a lot. They could, but as we see many suffer still. People are so broken, they often need to change in every aspect of their life today to get well.
When he says EMF is more important then diet I think he means you can eat a perfect diet and get EMF pollution and remain sick. That is what I think.

Some people, the less broken me thinks.. get well when changing only one thing. My mom does just a short dip in the river year round and she is very healthy. She eats a quite ok diet but also bread and stuff. But she is coming from a different generation and has collected so much energy in her youth living in North Norway and growing up without cellphones and electronics. They do not even had a landline, and in winter they used "shoes" from wool and lethersoles they made themself! They was freezing all the time too..lol I love to listen to my mom's stories about how life was when she grew up. Hubby too. He grew up in Estonia when it still belonged to Russia. It was so poor. They had no telephone either, no water inside, they had to go and get the water from outside and it was always cold. They lived off the land. I bet that is why he is so strong and I am not. He can do lots of things I can not. Sometimes I so wish I could turn the clock back.. and jump into these peaceful times without all these electronic equipments we have today. Man.. it is a totally different feeling. Everything goes slower.. wow.

My mom fails to understand how food impact her kids, because she does not get impacted that way. It is sad to see. I always try to explain, mom, this is a different time, a new generation that is destroyed because of...(I explain why)! But she has so hard times to see it. And some of her kids suffers from mental illness and stuff. In today's world.. we need to do all we can to remind healthy.

### MamaGrok — 2013-01-31T15:31:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-73451>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=73400)

> Mamagrok - personally - I think the high grains would be a disaster for her. For years I have had multiple problems with arthritis that goes up my arm into my shoulder and across my back and up into my skull also a rotator cuff problem etc - all documented on xrays etc. I am absolutely and completely pain free now - I do a lot of ocean time....

Exactly. I think EMF matters, a lot. But when food doesn't change, the ocean alone doesn't have much of a chance.

Inger - is your mother familiar with Weston Price or Dr. Pottenger? The research of both shows that degradation is worse for every successive generation. The first generation is susceptible to cavities & contagious disease more than their parents, but the next generation is subject to much worse problems, because they were nutritionally (and in other ways) deprived since their conception. The next generation is even worse, b/c they were deprived since the egg that became them was conceived. It piles on and on till we reach the point that people can no longer conceive and there are no more generations.

That can happen in just one or two generations if the difference between what you need & what you get is big enough.

### diane — 2013-03-08T04:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-79596>

I've noticed especially over the last week how clear my thinking is and how I'm really getting back to my game at work. Just as background - I've always been the go-getter at work, on the rise, a sharp thinker who contributes and is great at coming up with solutions. So the brain fog that I've been experiencing the last few years has been so infuriating. I have a hard time thinking, understanding, paying attention and sometimes even caring. When things are discussed in meetings, I often have drawn a blank. I would say I even had low level headaches most days. It was a feeling in my head - that's the best way to describe it. More than foggy, less than a full blown headache. But I know the feeling when it comes. And actually d-rhibose has seemed to help this (at least I think that's what's helped).

It's been getting progressively better, but I have noticed over the last week that I've been quicker on my feet. I volunteered to add more responsibility.

I can see that I still have improvements to make. But weight aside, losing my brain power has been one of the hardest things for me to see happen. Energy was the other. And I've seen huge changes in energy levels since getting my circadian rhythms more in line.

That is so great Diane - that is a really powerful thing to have your brain come back on line.

I feel like I could have written your exact post! This is so me. One of the most frustrating things for me the last few years has been my lack of focus at work. I had always been so driven and motivated and when I finally got my last promotion, which really put me at the top of my game, my attention/drive/focus/ability all just seemed to vanish. I have had the exact same experience in meetings where, not only can I not pay attention to what is being discussed, but I have absolutely zero input to offer.

I've had a few days here and there where I've felt a major improvement, but I'm waiting for the days when it's the norm. I cannot wait to feel productive and like I'm actually contributing to the work again. Right now I feel as if I'm just kind of doing the bare minimum I can to get by.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-08T15:27:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-79653>

Have you found what you're good at? Do you stick with it until the job is done? If not why have you lost your focus? It is what will bring you success. Whatever is at the center of your attention is, is your current focus. When you develop laser-sharp precision in decision making it removes the peripheral to make ephemeral decisions and to give us pinpoint solutions to life's problems. Our eyes are designed as life is, to take light at many angles and perspectives and focus the rays through a "filtered lens" and onto our retina. On the retina the light creates electrical info that are developed into vision by our brain. You create the "lens" for your life with beliefs and perspectives. Focus is important, it takes the diffuse in life, and aligns it into a coherency of clarity. Wherever you are, be 100% all in.

> Now I need to ask the women in my family, and all my sisters, to check their too.... cant wait to tell them this!

I just read that last night in Wiley's book. Apparently all women used to menstruate together back when we were in touch with nature. She is, in not so many words, predicting distinction event because of fertility rates and the impact on women's bodies. Her perspective is that we are in real trouble 200 years from the time electricity came into play given that women are reproducing at the lowest rates ever.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T20:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-80217>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=80195)
>
> Fitness, is there a particular spot or just in general all around your body. I have been getting that buzzing feeling too around my groin area. I wa thinking maybe the estrogen I started was doing something but I only buzzed on the grounding days.

Hey, no fair! I want!

Heh, I usually get the buzzing in my feet/ankles, after they are in contact with the grounding pad for around an hour. I also get some slight tingles in my back while laying down. None of those Good Tingles, though, darnnit!

The first night I was grounded to the earth it was crazy, though. I was twitching and it felt like little sparks in various places over my body, but just every 10 to 15 minutes or so. I was a bit concerned, but I knew I was only connected to the earth, so I just went back to sleep. Haven't had a night like that since. I was a bit disappointed the second night, actually. I poured some water on the grounding rod, but everything has been fairly sedate, since that one night.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-12T22:03:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-80233>

In my feet and lower legs. I take off my shoes and socks at lunch at plant my feet solidly on the ground. Another thing of note is that I go back to work in a very good mood. Sunshine + Grounding + Fresh Air = Positive Mood

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-13T00:25:29+0200

I love the positive mood, rather an exalted feeling when I go back to work after my grounding lunch. I don't eat, just drink 1/2 of my bulletproof coffee from the morn and take a little d-ribose, walk on the beach in the water and I feel like I could do anything!

### caroline — 2013-03-13T01:02:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-80275>

### harmony — 2016-07-12T02:40:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-198237>

I know this is an old thread but I'm new to all this ... is this part of what grounding is? When I was walking the beach day before yesterday, I kept getting small prickles or tingles like static electricity in various different areas of my body - I thought maybe they were sandflies! But couldn't see any signs of any biting flies and had no sign of bites. Still so much to read and learn about...

### nonchalant — 2016-07-12T02:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-198238>

But couldn't see any signs of any biting flies and had no sign of bites. Still so much to read and learn about...

Yes, that is grounding. Lots of us felt that at first. I think it is energy starting to move in your body. Were you off by yourself? Away from buildings and such?

### harmony — 2016-07-12T08:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-198243>

Yes, the beach I go to is backed by national park there and practically deserted, closer to the boat ramp there are a few houses, and more up higher, but those are some distance from where I was walking, and I had just had a swim so I was still wet. Fascinating - I would never have thought of it again probably, if I hadn't read this thread...cool! Thanks for confirming it for me...

### nonchalant — 2016-07-13T01:35:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-youve-seen-in-your-journey-what-have-you-noticed.5375/post-198277>

> [harmony said:](/goto/post?id=198243)
>
> Yes, the beach I go to is backed by national park there and practically deserted, closer to the boat ramp there are a few houses, and more up higher, but those are some distance from where I was walking, and I had just had a swim so I was still wet. Fascinating - I would never have thought of it again probably, if I hadn't read this thread...cool! Thanks for confirming it for me...

I had a nice experience at the beach in cool weather. The sun was setting, the wind was blowing, and we were in chairs around a campfire. I started to get a little chilled. Instead of getting up for a jacket, I dug in the sand an inch or two with my feet, and put the soles of my feet in the damper sand, I suddenly was able to tolerate the temp. I sat that way for quite a while, then got chilly again. So I just dug a little deeper, and got relief. Grounding rules! I think the connection with the earth gave me more energy to control my own body temp.

In winter, I noticed my first symptom of decreasing energy was feeling chilled, no matter the ambient temperature.

I took advantage of the $99 price last month and got my results back today. Now I'm wondering how best to interpret them.

I know about geneticgenie.org and the Yasko spreadsheet and have started to dig into http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm. This is all complex enough, though, that I am interested in finding someone to help me interpret. I suspect that one misinterpretation on my part could have a major impact on the conclusions.

So, for those of you who have gone down this path: did you do a consult with Dr. K to better understand your results? Somebody else (Tim Jackson)? Or just a lot of research on your own???
